PRESIDENZA: ANTONELLA SBERNA

2. Preparation for the 2025 EU芒聙聯China Summit - Tackling China's critical raw materials export restrictions
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 L'ordine del giorno reca, in discussione congiunta,

- le dichiarazioni del Consiglio e della Commissione sulla preparazione del vertice UE-Cina del 2025 (2025/2757(RSP)), e.

- la dichiarazione della Commissione "Far fronte alle restrizioni imposte dalla Cina sulle esportazioni di materie prime critiche" (2025/2800(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, it's a big honour standing here for the first time representing the Danish Presidency of the Council of the EU. We are taking over the Presidency at a crucial time for Europe, with the geopolitical landscape changing dramatically. I look very much forward to working together with all of you in the next six months for a common resolve for Europe.

Now, turning to the subject at hand, this year we marked the 50th anniversary of diplomatic relations between our Union and the People's Republic of China. The world has changed greatly since 6脗聽May 1975, when Premier Zhou Enlai and Commissioner Sir Christopher Soames agreed to establish bilateral diplomatic relations.

Our policy approach to China, defined by the European Council in June 2023, still stands. China is a partner, a competitor and a systemic rival. As partners, we continue to cooperate with China where we can and where our interests align. We will continue engaging on tackling global challenges. We encourage China to take more ambitious action on matters such as climate change and biodiversity.

The next stop on that road is the upcoming EU-China High-Level Environment and Climate Dialogue in Beijing. This summit could be an opportunity to strengthen our cooperation with China on global public goods, and to deliver a clear message on a joint commitment on climate change ahead of COP 30.

The European Union and China continue to be important trade and economic partners. Our trade in goods and services is worth more than EUR脗聽840脗聽billion. The respective stock of investment is similar, with EUR脗聽185脗聽billion of Chinese investments in the EU, and EUR脗聽184脗聽billion of European investment in China.

The summit will be an important occasion to also discuss a number of global and regional issues with China.

Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine is a direct threat to our own security, and a fundamental challenge for the EU. We remain very concerned about China's support to Russia's war effort. As a permanent member of the UN Security Council, China has a special responsibility in upholding the rule-based international order, the UN Charter and international law. We will continue calling on China to press Russia to stop its war of aggression. China's diplomatic and material support to Russia impacts EU-China relations negatively.

The stability and reduction of tensions in the South China Sea and the Taiwan Strait continue to be important to us. We oppose any unilateral attempts to change the status quo by force or coercion, and remain fully committed to our own One China policy.

We also remain firmly committed to the promotion of respect for human rights and fundamental freedoms, with well-known concerns regarding forced labour, the treatment of human-rights defenders and persons belonging to minorities, the situation in Tibet and Xinjiang, as well as the honouring of China's previous commitments related to Hong Kong.

The 40th Human Rights Dialogue with China took place in Brussels on 13脗聽June. The EU stated again its serious concern, and raised a number of clear individual cases, calling for the immediate release of those detained for the peaceful exercise of their human rights. The EU side made specific requests to improve the treatment and conditions of several individuals, including contacts with families.

Concerns have also been expressed about malicious cyber activities originating from China's territory. This is unacceptable.

To summarise: to advance in our relations, we need to speak together and to pursue concrete solutions to our problems where possible, while remaining firm on our own interests and principles.

Despite our different political and economic systems, the European Union and China have a shared interest in pursuing constructive and stable relations, based on respect for the rule-based international order, balanced engagement, and reciprocity.

In that respect, the upcoming EU-China summit presents an opportunity not only to reflect on the results of the past 50 years, but also to look forward and discuss how the European Union and the People's Republic of China can work together into the future.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ursula von der Leyen, President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Madam Minister, honourable Members, indeed, we discuss today EU芒聙聭China relations ahead of the EU芒聙聭China Summit, where I will be with the Council President, Ant脙鲁nio Costa. Therefore, let me complement what you said, so that we have the whole view of the European Union's position.

Indeed, this year marks half a century of diplomatic relations between our Union and China. And this has coincided with possibly the most remarkable chapter in China's long history. In just 50 years, its GDP has grown by more than ten times. Villages were replaced by megacities. More than 800脗聽million people lifted themselves out of poverty. Once a society of agriculture, China has become an industrial giant and a clean tech leader. Once an underdeveloped country, it has turned into the largest lender to developing nations. China has always been one of the great global civilisations, but in the last 50 years, it has also become one of the great global powers.

Honourable Members, this makes our relationship with China one of the most defining and consequential for the rest of this century. But our relations with China must be rooted in a clear芒聙聭eyed assessment of this new reality. I have always said it: Europe is fully committed to a result芒聙聭oriented engagement with China. And this Commission is leading the way when it comes to de芒聙聭risking, not decoupling.

We, the EU and China, are two of the three largest economies and trade powers in the world, but we only trade some EUR脗聽2脗聽billion worth every day. This is only twice the volume we are trading with Switzerland because while the Chinese market is enormous, our access to the market remains limited.

The point here is that while we are leading voices on global challenges, there are very real risks at play for Europe. These risks are both strategic and systemic in nature. They affect our security and our competitiveness. These risks emerge from the fact that China has an entirely different system and it has unique instruments at its disposal to play outside the rules. This, for instance, allows China to flood global markets with subsidised overcapacity 芒聙聯 not just to boost its own industries, but to choke international competition.

China has also become a formidable actor in the global information and cyberspace. We very much welcome the voices of all Chinese on global issues of shared concern, but we will be very vigilant against any form of influence operations and cyberattacks in Europe.脗聽This is plain and simply unacceptable.

Honourable Members, these realities do present a real challenge for Europe to confront. And we have started to address these challenges, whether it is de芒聙聭risking our economy and industry, using our new toolbox for trade defence measures or diversifying our supply chains and sectors where China holds dependencies, if not outright monopolies.

At the same time, I believe there is also an opportunity here to build a more meaningful partnership with China. But to move our relationship forward, we need to make real progress and find fair solutions on the issues where we have been deadlocked for far too long. Predictability and reliability 芒聙聯 this is how we can work in our mutual interest and this is the message I will bring to the EU芒聙聭China Summit later this month.

There are three priorities which we need to focus on. First, rebalancing our economic relationship with China. Second, de芒聙聭risking. And third, advancing diplomacy on global issues, including climate.

On my first point, China is running the largest trade surplus in the history of mankind. Its trade surplus with our Union has surpassed EUR脗聽300脗聽billion last year and this is while it is getting harder and harder for European companies to do business in China. Our products are systematically discriminated in public procurement because of Beijing's 'Buy China' policy. Goods and services that are made in China get an automatic 20脗聽% price advantage in public bids. This is simply not fair. The system is explicitly rigged.

So, we have taken action to rebalance the public procurement market for medical devices, for example, because it is a matter of basic reciprocity. Europe remains fundamentally open, but most of our companies are rapidly losing market share in China. We want to see tangible progress on our long芒聙聭standing requests for market access.

The same is true on state芒聙聭subsidised overcapacity. China cannot rely on exports to solve its domestic economic challenges. Overcapacity must be addressed at its source. It cannot simply be offloaded onto global markets. That is the clear message behind our investigation into electric vehicles.

Interestingly, we see that a serious debate within China on excessive production, disorderly price undercutting and distorted markets has started. They understand that the domestic challenge cannot be solved at the expense of others. And let me be clear: if our partnership is to move forward, we need a genuine rebalancing, fewer market distortions, less overcapacity exported from China and fair reciprocal access for European business in China.

Honourable Members, the second priority is to speed up with de芒聙聭risking. China invested early in many of the technologies of the future, but then it started flooding global markets with cheap subsidised goods to wipe out competitors. Entire Western industries closed, from solar panels to mineral processing, leaving China to dominate.脗聽I have always spoken about China's domination of the rare earth permanent magnets market and how that can be used for economic leverage. This is why we are developing alternative supply sources in close cooperation with our partners.

Of course, dialogue with China is also essential. We are engaging with Beijing so that it loosens its export restrictions because, as I said at the outset, we do not believe in strategic decoupling and this is not in our interest. I believe that for Europe it would be inefficient and ineffective. But we will continue to de芒聙聭risk because we have learnt the lesson about the extent to which dependencies are vulnerabilities and how tech, trade and security are inherently linked. De芒聙聭risking is simply a matter of European independence.

Honourable Members, my next point is on geopolitics. Security is more interlinked between the Euro芒聙聭Atlantic and the Indo芒聙聭Pacific than it has been in several generations, and it is evolving rapidly. So, it is in our interest to work together, but we also know that China's unyielding support for Russia is creating heightened instability and insecurity for Europe. We can say that China is de facto enabling Russia's war economy and we cannot accept this.

I have always said: how China continues to interact with Putin's war will be a determining factor for EU芒聙聭China relations going forward. If China claims to defend the international rules芒聙聭based order, then it should unequivocally condemn Russia's gross violation of Ukraine's sovereignty, territorial integrity and internationally recognised borders, and act accordingly. And if China claims to defend multilateralism, then it must respect the rules and principles of international trade anchored in the WTO.

Honourable Members, my final point is about our cooperation to address climate change. While脗聽China is the largest emitter in the world with 30脗聽% of global emissions, clean energy also accounted for over one quarter of China's GDP growth. China invested over USD脗聽900脗聽billion in clean technologies alone, more than the US, Europe, Japan and India combined. Beijing is at once a staunch competitor in the clean tech race and, on the other hand, a vital partner for global decarbonisation.

This is the complexity we must deal with. We must switch gear in our competition, but also explore every avenue for cooperation. We are both convinced that the triple planetary crisis of climate, biodiversity loss and pollution requires a strong multilateral lateral framework. We want COP30 in Belem to deliver ambitious results. We both see net芒聙聭zero targets and policies as a growth strategy and a real driver for industrial modernisation. We both see the opportunities to work closer together in sectors such as emissions trading, carbon capture and storage, and the circular economy.

Honourable Members, the point is that there is a lot we can do together if China is ready to work together in a spirit of predictability and reliability. Just like Europe, China is a continent芒聙聭sized power. Just like our Union, it is a complex and fascinating giant. Our relationship must reflect this complexity. We will always defend our interests. We will de芒聙聭risk our economies, but we do not want to decouple. As we enter our second half芒聙聭century of cooperation, we know that China is changing. But this is also a new era for Europe 芒聙聯 an independent Europe. So, we are ready to build a more balanced and a more stable relationship, and to write a new chapter in this defining relationship. Long live Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Pascual de la Parte, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, thank you very much President of the Commission, reciprocity, transparency and respect for a level playing ground. These are truly the three principles upon which our relationship with China has to build up.

Firstly, on the security front, we cannot accept that China continues supporting and assisting Putin's Russian war of aggression against Ukraine. If China wants to be recognised as a big power, it has to assume also big responsibilities. It is a member of the Security Council, that means that it's supposed to be a guarantor of peace and stability in the world, and you cannot guarantee peace and stability in the world if you assist Putin's war of aggression. Our relationship with China cannot be normalised as long as it continues supporting Putin.

Secondly: human rights. We cannot ignore that China massively and grossly disregards the human rights of racial minorities and religious minorities. We have to remember that.

Thirdly: trade and services. China has to abide by all the dispositions of the World Trade Organisation and not select those it likes and disregard those it doesn't like. We know the pattern of behaviour of China; they subsidise the industries in order to eliminate the competition, they get the monopoly and they impose the conditions afterwards.

Fourthly: rare earths and raw materials, critical raw materials. We cannot accept the threat and the menace of control of exports of raw materials. It goes against the rules of the World Trade Organisation. By the way, this particular point is not the responsibility of the Chinese only; we have renounced exploiting our own raw materials and rare earths because of the dispositions of our green agenda. This has to be reconsidered if we want to not depend entirely on China. So: reality, reciprocity, transparency and a level playing ground.

脗聽脗聽Hermann Tertsch, en nombre del Grupo PfE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidente, de lo muy poco bueno que hizo la Uni脙鲁n Europea en la legislatura pasada fue advertir del grave peligro que entra脙卤a la dependencia y debilidad estrat脙漏gica que se hab脙颅a creado Europa respecto a China en los pasados lustros: el informe sobre China fue el marco para esa propuesta de giro radical para paliar dependencias y riesgos. En esta legislatura, la Comisi脙鲁n Europea y sus principales socios quieren, al parecer, volver un poco a la senda del error. Si yo creyera en algo todav脙颅a a la presidenta de la Comisi脙鲁n, estar脙颅a aqu脙颅 contento, porque le veo tomar una serie de distancias, pero no es el caso, desde luego, que yo me vaya a fiar a estas alturas.

La animadversi脙鲁n, el odio hacia el presidente Trump, que ciega aqu脙颅 a tant脙颅simos en la delirante idea de dar la espalda a los Estados Unidos para sustituirlo por China 芒聙聰que es propia de dictaduras comunistas o de tiran脙颅as como Brasil o Sud脙隆frica芒聙聰, se est脙隆 viendo y se palpa tambi脙漏n en estos ambientes. Y, como conocemos la capacidad corruptora del r脙漏gimen chino, hay que tener especial cuidado con la compra de voluntades. China es siempre una amenaza: en la esencia totalitaria de su r脙漏gimen est脙隆 la voluntad de imponerse y fagocitarnos como hace con pa脙颅ses en otros continentes.

Incidentes recientes nos han alarmado una vez m脙隆s: diplom脙隆ticos chinos se atreven a amenazar y amedrentar a diputados al Parlamento Europeo en esta casa, porque no les gusta que se defiendan los derechos humanos y la libertad en China, en Taiw脙隆n o en cualquier otro rinc脙鲁n del mundo. Debe saber China que queremos relaciones sobrias y correctas en reciprocidad y que se va a acabar que ellos hagan aqu脙颅 lo que quieren y nosotros no podamos hacer all脙颅 nada.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniele Polato, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, che la Cina ricorra a pratiche commerciali sleali non 脙篓 una novit脙聽. Che queste restrizioni arrivino in un momento estremamente delicato per la nostra economia, nemmeno.

Dobbiamo reagire in modo coeso, difendendo un mercato davvero libero, ma chiediamoci: come siamo arrivati fin qui?

Inseguendo visioni ideologiche, abbiamo smantellato interi comparti produttivi come l'automotive. In nome di promesse futuristiche, che oggi si stanno rivelando realt脙聽 scomode, abbiamo permesso che intere filiere strategiche diventassero ostaggio della dipendenza da paesi terzi per le materie prime critiche.

Puntare il dito contro la Cina non basta: dobbiamo cogliere l'occasione del cinquantenario delle relazioni bilaterali per riflettere su quali errori abbiamo commesso noi, in Europa, in questi anni.

脙聢 tempo di cambiare rotta. La nostra reazione deve ripartire da ci脙虏 che ha reso grande la nostra economia: il know-how, l'impresa e la produzione.

Serve un nuovo slancio industriale. Serve una politica mineraria europea che riduca la nostra vulnerabilit脙聽. Serve riportare al centro l'economia reale. Ecco le risposte che i cittadini si aspettano da quest'Aula.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Engin Eroglu, im Namen der Renew-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Sehr geehrte Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrter Herr Kommissar, sehr geehrte Frau Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin von der Leyen 芒聙聯 willkommen in unserem Haus! Wir diskutieren heute脗聽芒聙聯 sehr wichtig 芒聙聯 脙录ber den Summit, den wir am Ende dieses Monats haben. Frau von der Leyen, ich m脙露chte Ihnen gerne drei Punkte mitgeben, die Sie aus Sicht des Europ脙陇ischen Parlaments vielleicht einbringen k脙露nnen.

Wirtschaft. In der Wirtschaft haben wir im letzten Mandat, Frau von der Leyen, leidenschaftlich gek脙陇mpft daf脙录r, dass wir tats脙陇chlich Seltene Erden und Rohstoffe in der Europ脙陇ischen Union abbauen k脙露nnen. Wenn Sie mich fragen, hat die Kommission nach dem Beschluss, den wir hier im Europ脙陇ischen Parlament gemacht haben, tats脙陇chlich in der Umsetzung vielleicht ein bisschen geschludert. Deswegen der klare Auftrag: Bitte, wir brauchen mehr Abbau der Seltenen Erden, aber auch aller anderen Rohstoffe in der Europ脙陇ischen Union. Warum? Tats脙陇chlich ist es so, dass 98脗聽% der Seltenen Erden, die wir in der Europ脙陇ischen Union brauchen,脗聽aus China kommen脗聽芒聙聯 98脗聽%! Wir sind quasi vollkommen abh脙陇ngig von China. Die einzige M脙露glichkeit, uns in eine bessere Verhandlungsposition zu bekommen, ist der eigene Abbau und nicht der Aufbau von Abh脙陇ngigkeiten zu anderen Drittstaaten. Deswegen der Appell: Wir brauchen den Abbau. Wir brauchen ein europ脙陇isches Minenprogramm, eine Task Force Mine.

Das andere ist Sicherheitspolitik. Ich war erschrocken 脙录ber das, was Au脙聼enminister Yi gesagt hat, als er vergangene Woche Frau Kallas besucht hat, in Bezug auf China. Ich glaube, das m脙录ssen Sie adressieren, das m脙录ssen Sie ansprechen. Das ist ein zentrales Thema f脙录r uns hier im Europ脙陇ischen Parlament 芒聙聯 die Sicherheit.

Und ein dritter Punkt, das sind die Menschenrechte, Frau von der Leyen. Wenn Sie jetzt nach China reisen, die Chinesen sind in einer besseren Verhandlungsposition, weil wir in der Europ脙陇ischen Union Fehler gemacht haben. Bitte, wenn Sie was hergeben, keinen Millimeter bei den Menschenrechten! Das sind die Lehren aus dem Zweiten Weltkrieg. Da stehen wir als Europ脙陇isches Parlament ganz klar, und es wird kein Abweichen von uns hier geben. Frau von der Leyen, ich w脙录nsche Ihnen viel Erfolg da dr脙录ben.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ville Niinist脙露, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, let's not sugar-coat it. China isn't just restricting exports. It is testing Europe's weakness. Seven rare earth elements and one loud message: 'we control your clean tech'.

This is not about trade. This is geopolitical blackmail with magnets. The EU imports 98脗聽% of its rare-earth magnets from China. That's not a supply chain; that's a stranglehold. We cannot walk into the EU芒聙聯China Summit begging for exemptions. That's not strength; that's surrender.

We cannot be quiet about China's support to the Russian economy, and we cannot be quiet about the deterioration of human rights in China. Therefore, our response to blackmail must be bold: resilience, secularity, autonomy. We need to stop chasing virgin materials and start valuing what we already have. Recycle more, waste less, design better. Permanent magnets must be built to last and to be reused.

Let's stop being naive. China has done this before, weaponising rare earths in 2010. Now they're doing it again. So that means that we in Europe must de-risk seriously. Secure strategic stocks and force risk assessments. Plan like it's already a crisis because it already is.

We have the tools and we have the laws. Now we need the political courage. When we source materials from the Global South, we must build on true partnerships. No extraction without consent. No transition built on exploitation.

Let's not trade our climate future for cheap magnets. Let's build a circular, just and sovereign Europe.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Martin Schirdewan, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Ich begr脙录脙聼e sehr, dass es im Vorfeld des Gipfels anl脙陇sslich eines halben Jahrhunderts europ脙陇isch-chinesischer Beziehungen zu einer Entspannung im gegenseitigen Verh脙陇ltnis gekommen ist. Denn darin besteht eine gro脙聼e Chance, dass wir die notwendige Zusammenarbeit auf ein gemeinsames Fundament stellen k脙露nnen.

Die Welt im Wandel stellt China und die Europ脙陇ische Union vor Herausforderungen, und wer die gro脙聼en Menschheitsfragen angehen will脗聽芒聙聯 den Kampf gegen Armut, Hunger und Ungleichheit, gegen den Klimawandel, Krieg oder Frieden脗聽芒聙聯, der muss sich f脙录r eine multilaterale, regelbasierte Weltordnung einsetzen. Daf脙录r muss auch die Europ脙陇ische Union ihre Hausaufgaben erledigen auf dem Weg zu strategischer Unabh脙陇ngigkeit: Massive Investitionen in die Zukunft, in unsere Industrie, digitale Infrastruktur, in eine unabh脙陇ngige und nachhaltige Energieversorgung und in die Daseinsvorsorge sind notwendig, um mit China und den USA auf Augenh脙露he agieren zu k脙露nnen.

China ist nach den USA der zweitgr脙露脙聼te Handelspartner der EU. Ein weiterer Handelskrieg wie mit den USA wird nur sch脙陇dlich sein und Millionen Arbeitspl脙陇tze bedrohen. Wir brauchen fairen Handel im Interesse der Menschen und nicht entfesselten Freihandel im Interesse der Konzerne. Aber es muss auch klar sein, dass wir uns in keine einseitige Abh脙陇ngigkeit von China bei Zukunftstechnologien, bei kritischen Rohstoffen oder bei strategischen Sektoren begeben k脙露nnen. Deshalb, Frau von der Leyen, sollten Sie nicht nur mit einer Idee f脙录r wirtschaftliche Kooperation nach Beijing reisen, sondern auch mit einer konkreten diplomatischen Initiative: Seit fast dreieinhalb Jahren tobt der Krieg Russlands in der Ukraine. Donald Trump scheint trotz seiner Telefonate keinen Einfluss auf Putin zu haben, Xi Jinping wahrscheinlich schon.

Wenn Sie Geschichte schreiben wollen, dann fahren Sie mit einem konkreten Vorschlag f脙录r eine gemeinsame chinesisch-europ脙陇ische Friedensinitiative f脙录r die Ukraine nach China. China ist die einzige internationale Macht, die Putin zur Vernunft bewegen kann, und die EU muss endlich eine Schl脙录sselrolle f脙录r Frieden in Europa an- und einnehmen. Jetzt ist die Zeit f脙录r Diplomatie!

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ren脙漏 Aust, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! China ist ein Land mit 1,4脗聽Milliarden Menschen und einer staatlichen Ordnung, die sich aus 脙录ber 3脗聽000 Jahren Kulturgeschichte herleitet. Wer glaubt, man k脙露nne ein solches Land mit Belehrungen und Ermahnungen ver脙陇ndern, der irrt nicht nur, er gef脙陇hrdet die Stabilit脙陇t globaler Beziehungen. China hat bei allen Auseinandersetzungen immer wieder bewiesen, dass es bereit ist zu Zur脙录ckhaltung. Ich erinnere daran, dass im Jahr 1999 w脙陇hrend des Kosovokrieges eine chinesische Botschaft in Belgrad von NATO-Bomben getroffen wurde. Die Reaktion der chinesischen F脙录hrung darauf war keine Eskalation, sondern Nachsicht.

Europa darf sich nicht in ein feindliches Denken treiben lassen, weder von au脙聼enpolitischen Rivalit脙陇ten noch von innenpolitischer Symbolpolitik. Wir brauchen eine Au脙聼enwirtschaftspolitik, die sich an unseren Interessen orientiert, nicht an Wunschbildern. Europa muss gegen脙录ber gro脙聼en Bev脙露lkerungs芒聙聭 und Wirtschaftsr脙陇umen geeint auftreten. Allein w脙陇ren selbst Deutschland, Frankreich, Italien und Polen in der heutigen Welt zu klein. Nat脙录rlich m脙录ssen wir unsere Interessen formulieren und sie auch vertreten. Aber dies muss ohne moralische 脙聹berh脙露hung geschehen. Partnerschaft wo m脙露glich, Standfestigkeit wo n脙露tig und immer Respekt gegen脙录ber einem kulturell ebenso alten wie selbstbewussten Gegen脙录ber. Wir m脙录ssen als Europa die Verhandlungsmacht st脙陇rken, indem wir Abh脙陇ngigkeit verringern und doch auch immer wieder die Hand reichen zur Zusammenarbeit.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hildegard Bentele (PPE). 芒聙聯 Sehr geehrte Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Wenn China es will, stehen in Europa die B脙陇nder still. So klar muss man die Realit脙陇t benennen. China nutzt sein Monopol bei Seltenen Erden und unsere Abh脙陇ngigkeit gezielt als strategische Waffe脗聽芒聙聯 durch Exportbeschr脙陇nkungen, durch langwierige Lizenzverfahren und neuerdings durch die Forderung, sensible Unternehmensdaten offenzulegen. Das verz脙露gert Lieferungen, verteuert die Produktion und gef脙陇hrdet unsere technologische Souver脙陇nit脙陇t und unsere Sicherheit. Seltene Erden sind unersetzlich f脙录r unsere Industrie, von Smartphones 脙录ber Solaranlagen bis hin zur Verteidigungstechnologie. Europa verf脙录gt bislang kaum 脙录ber eigene Minen oder Verarbeitungsst脙陇tten. Das 脙陇ndern wir nun mit der Umsetzung des EU芒聙聭Rohstoffgesetzes. Doch die aktuelle Lage zeigt: Wir m脙录ssen schneller und entschlossener handeln, auch mit kurzfristig wirksamen Ma脙聼nahmen wie besserer Sammlung, gezieltem Recycling und gegebenenfalls eigenen Exportstopps.

Wirtschaft, Politik und Finanzwelt脗聽芒聙聯 alle m脙录ssen jetzt an einem Strang ziehen. In der Rohstoffdiplomatie m脙录ssen wir einen Gang h脙露her schalten. Ich bitte Sie, Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, die unlauteren Praktiken beim EU芒聙聭China芒聙聭Gipfel klar zu benennen und sich mit Nachdruck daf脙录r einzusetzen, dass sie beendet werden. Gleichzeitig m脙录ssen wir in Europa auf allen Ebenen schneller vorankommen, mit eigenen Projekten, neuen strategischen Partnerschaften, gezielter Bevorratung und mehr Innovation. China handelt hoch strategisch. Wir m脙录ssen das auch tun. Es ist h脙露chste Zeit, dass die EU Verantwortung 脙录bernimmt und eine verl脙陇ssliche Rohstoffversorgung f脙录r unsere industrielle Basis sicherstellt, damit Europas Wohlstand auch morgen noch Bestand hat.

脗聽脗聽Ren脙漏 Repasi (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Kommissionspr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! 300脗聽Milliarden Euro lautet das Handelsdefizit zwischen der EU und China. Sind chinesische Waren unter fairen Wettbewerbsbedingungen tats脙陇chlich so viel besser als europ脙陇ische? Wohl kaum. Denn unsere Unternehmen sto脙聼en in China auf Marktzugangsh脙录rden, auf unfaire Subventionen, die fairen Wettbewerb unm脙露glich machen, und auf eine politische F脙录hrung, die wirtschaftliche Macht als Druckmittel einsetzt. China fordert Respekt脗聽芒聙聯 zu Recht脗聽芒聙聯, aber verweigert Gegenseitigkeit脗聽芒聙聯 zu Unrecht. Das ist dann keine Partnerschaft. Es ist ein Ungleichgewicht.

Partnerschaft bedeutet Gegenseitigkeit, Transparenz und Respekt. Respekt vor unserer politischen Kultur bedeutet auch, dass gew脙陇hlte Vertreter f脙录r ihre freie Meinungs脙陇u脙聼erung nicht mit Sanktionen belegt werden d脙录rfen. Ich fordere daher die Aufhebung der chinesischen Sanktionen sowohl gegen den ehemaligen Europaabgeordneten Reinhard B脙录tikofer als auch gegen unsere Kollegen in den nationalen Parlamenten. Das w脙陇re ein klares Signal und ein wichtiger Schritt hin zu echter Partnerschaft.

脗聽脗聽Anna Bry脜聜ka (PfE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! W tej debacie powinno wybrzmie脛聡 to, dlaczego Unia Europejska nie jest dzi脜聸 r脙鲁wnorz脛聶dnym partnerem dla Stan脙鲁w Zjednoczonych i dla Chin. Pot脛聶脜录ny deficyt handlowy, zale脜录no脜聸脛聡 technologiczna, a wreszcie produkcja przemys脜聜owa zbli脜录aj脛聟ca si脛聶 do upadku 芒聙聯 to wszystko jest efektem dzia脜聜alno脜聸ci Komisji Europejskiej, kt脙鲁ra niszczy europejsk脛聟 gospodark脛聶.

Wymuszacie na pa脜聞stwach cz脜聜onkowskich inwestycje w OZE czy samochody elektryczne, czym doprowadzili脜聸cie do uzale脜录nienia si脛聶 w zakresie metali ziem rzadkich od Chin. I dzisiaj Chiny wykorzystuj脛聟 swoj脛聟 dominacj脛聶, aby ograniczy脛聡 suwerenno脜聸脛聡 Europy. Zielona ideologia, upadek europejskiego przemys脜聜u, chory plan w postaci redukcji emisji gaz脙鲁w cieplarnianych o 90% do 2040 r., walka z paliwami kopalnymi. To wszystko jest przeciwie脜聞stwem polityki, kt脙鲁r脛聟 prowadz脛聟 Chiny i Stany Zjednoczone. Dzi脜聸 nie ma twardej i stanowczej polityki handlowej opartej o interesy pa脜聞stw cz脜聜onkowskich. Najwspanialszy kontynent pod waszymi rz脛聟dami stanie si脛聶 za chwil脛聶 symbolem upadku, biedy i braku bezpiecze脜聞stwa.

脗聽脗聽Marion Mar脙漏chal (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, dans les ann脙漏es脗聽1990, les pays europ脙漏ens ont abandonn脙漏 l芒聙聶extraction et le raffinage des m脙漏taux critiques. Trente脗聽ans plus tard, faute de vision strat脙漏gique, les Europ脙漏ens se trouvent dans une d脙漏licate situation de d脙漏pendance 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏gard de la Chine, qui compte pour 60脗聽% de l芒聙聶extraction mini脙篓re mondiale des terres rares et pour 92脗聽% de leur production raffin脙漏e. P脙漏kin couvre 脙聽 ce titre plus de 90脗聽% de nos besoins. Toute restriction d芒聙聶exportation dans ce domaine fait peser de lourds risques sur nos industries et notre d脙漏fense.

Alors, face aux d脙漏fis que nous pose la Chine, cinq axes prioritaires doivent guider notre politique: 1)脗聽r脙漏duction de l芒聙聶utilisation des terres rares si des solutions de substitution fiables existent; 2)脗聽diversification des partenariats d脙篓s lors que l芒聙聶agriculture europ脙漏enne n芒聙聶est pas une monnaie d芒聙聶脙漏change 芒聙聯脗聽ce qui serait totalement inacceptable; 3)脗聽recherche et exploitation de nouveaux gisements europ脙漏ens; 4)脗聽d脙漏veloppement de fili脙篓res de recyclage; 5)脗聽relocalisation des capacit脙漏s de raffinage.

Bruxelles semble enfin s芒聙聶脙陋tre d脙漏cid脙漏e 脙聽 suivre cette voie. C芒聙聶est un motif de satisfaction, qui doit contribuer 脙聽 rompre avec les carcans id脙漏ologiques que les 脙漏cologistes d脙漏croissants nous ont impos脙漏s jusqu芒聙聶ici.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bart Groothuis (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, Europe faces dual coercion: from US tariffs, yes, but far more dangerously from China's strategic chokehold on rare earth exports. Gallium, germanium 芒聙聯 critical elements 芒聙聯 are already no longer reaching our industries.

Colleagues, remember that no guided missile can be produced without gallium. It's important because the Chinese Foreign Minister told High Representative Kallas that Russia may not lose the war, but also because our economic and our industrial survival is at stake.

And as President von der Leyen rightly said, this is about dominance, about building dependency and about state-organised blackmail. Some here still claim this is just collateral damage in the US-China rivalry. It's not. It's about showing where the power is.

Some here suggest we strike a deal: drop tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles in exchange for the withdrawal of Beijing's retaliatory measures. That's appeasement 芒聙聯 it won't work. Feeding the beast won't pacify it.

Some here argue that European Member States should go for themselves and defend their own industries without the backing of the EU. Don't! It will only invite more coercion in the years to come.

I argue differently: economic leverage is there in Europe, at least theoretically, if we use it. So, use every tool in the EU economic toolbox: close markets if necessary, impose export controls, sanction Chinese banks fuelling Moscow's war of aggression. Leverage what we have, or we don't have leverage at all.

Commissioner, act with strength for European sovereignty and industrial survival, and above all, don't encourage more coercion.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mark脙漏ta Gregorov脙隆 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, Council, Commission, the upcoming EU芒聙聯China Summit is more than a diplomatic event. It is a test of Union's credibility. We often hear 芒聙聯 and we have heard it today from the Commission 芒聙聯 that the EU should engage with China as an equal partner, but it's time we actually behave like one. We allow our markets to be flooded with heavily subsidised Chinese goods, and also allow blocking our companies entering Chinese market, undermining them.

Most gravely, we ignore China's support for Russia's war of aggression in Ukraine. Yes, ignore. I have heard you saying: 'We are very concerned. We will continue criticising.' When have you ever done that? That has never been done. What's the success of the Commission? Has any heavy machinery not been sent from China to Russia thanks to your doing? I've never seen that.

China runs massive trade surpluses, and after a trade war with the United States, it really needs the European market. And do you know what that is called? Leverage. So I thus urge President von der Leyen, 芒聙聯 or please, Mr脗聽脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, if you could convey that message, because she left 芒聙聯 to stand firm in Beijing and defend our strategic interests. And our strategic interests are Ukraine, not German cars.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Danilo Della Valle (The Left). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, ieri la Presidente von der Leyen, in quest'Aula, ha dichiarato che nelle relazioni con la Cina si intendono tutelare gli interessi dei paesi dell'Unione europea.

Sono sei anni che i cittadini europei attendono che si inizi davvero a difendere i loro interessi, invece di pensare a quelli degli Stati Uniti o delle multinazionali come Pfizer e Rheinmetall.

Gli scambi commerciali tra Unione europea e Cina valgono circa 720脗聽miliardi di euro l'anno. Le esportazioni verso la Repubblica Popolare sono cresciute del 50脗聽% quest'anno e il potenziale di crescita 脙篓 enorme.

Tuttavia, quando si parla della Cina o di altri paesi non allineati agli interessi statunitensi, si utilizza sempre un linguaggio conflittuale, semplificando la realt脙聽 in termini di amici e nemici.

Perch脙漏 sostenere la guerra commerciale avviata da Trump dovrebbe significare difendere gli interessi dei cittadini europei? Come si pu脙虏 ignorare l'importanza dei mercati dei paesi BRICS, ad esempio, per le imprese europee? O, soprattutto, il ruolo fondamentale che la Cina pu脙虏 svolgere come attore di mediazione nei conflitti in cui ci avete trascinato, come in Ucraina, ma anche in Medio Oriente?

Allora, si abbia per una volta un sussulto di dignit脙聽, si riconosca che il mondo sta cambiando e diventando sempre pi脙鹿 multipolare, si tutelino davvero gli interessi dell'Unione europea e si lavori per la cooperazione e per la diplomazia, non per gli interessi di un mondo destinato a scomparire insieme a chi lo sta rappresentando.

脗聽脗聽Diana Iovanovici 脜聻o脜聼oac脛聝 (NI). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, chiar m脛聝 脙庐ntrebam dac脛聝 doamna Ursula von der Leyen o s脛聝 aib脛聝 脠聶i bunul-sim脠聸, 脠聶i respectul fa脠聸脛聝 de to脠聸i europarlamentarii care vor lua cuv脙垄ntul, dar 脠聶i curajul de a m脛聝 privi 脙庐n ochi dup脛聝 interven脠聸iile sale directe 脙庐n alegerile preziden脠聸iale din Rom脙垄nia, unde eu am fost interzis脛聝 la solicitarea d脙垄nsei.

Vorbi脠聸i despre China. Sunt avocat 脠聶i lucrez de 30 de ani cu chinezii. V脛聝 este fric脛聝 de ei pentru c脛聝 nu-i cunoa脠聶te脠聸i. Vre脠聸i s脛聝 v脛聝 duce脠聸i la summit 脠聶i s脛聝 v脛聝 lua脠聸i de China, c脛聝 e prieten脛聝 cu Rusia? E prieten脛聝 cu Rusia dintotdeauna. Face parte din BRICS. Asta nu 脙庐n脠聸elege脠聸i. Noi facem parte din NATO. Eu a脠聶 prefera s脛聝 facem parte din BRICS, pentru c脛聝 este mult mai interesant BRICS 脠聶i mult mai respectuos.

Vre脠聸i s脛聝 vorbi脠聸i despre Taiwan? Mare gre脠聶eal脛聝! V脛聝 duce脠聸i s脛聝 discuta脠聸i 脠聶i v脛聝 spun eu ce s-a discutat la misiunea diplomatic脛聝 a Chinei c脙垄nd i s-a repro脠聶at despre Taiwan. A spus c脛聝 a脠聶a cum Germania s-a unit, a脠聶a trebuie s脛聝 se uneasc脛聝 脠聶i ei.

Vre脠聸i s脛聝 vorbi脠聸i despre drepturile omului? 脙聨n Europa avem cea mai mare tiranie, dictatur脛聝 脠聶i interdic脠聸ia drepturilor omului 脠聶i 脙庐nc脛聝lcarea drepturilor fundamentale ale omului. A脠聶a c脛聝 nu ave脠聸i nicio 脠聶ans脛聝, pornind de la premizele pe care le-a脠聸i discutat aici. Mai 脙庐nv脛聝脠聸a脠聸i un pic de diploma脠聸ie 脠聶i de politic脛聝 interna脠聸ional脛聝.

脗聽脗聽Juan Ignacio Zoido 脙聛lvarez (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, dentro de dos semanas celebraremos una Cumbre hist脙鲁rica: el 50.脗潞脗聽aniversario del establecimiento de las relaciones diplom脙隆ticas entre la Uni脙鲁n Europea y China, unas relaciones que han tra脙颅do beneficios en el aspecto comercial, al promover inversiones y crear empleos. Pero en los 脙潞ltimos a脙卤os, la relaci脙鲁n ha acusado un concepto geopol脙颅tico turbulento, as脙颅 como el surgimiento de crecientes asimetr脙颅as. Por eso, esta Cumbre 芒聙聰m脙隆s all脙隆 de la efem脙漏ride芒聙聰 debe servir para reequilibrar esta relaci脙鲁n con reciprocidad como base.

En primer lugar, debemos trabajar en el mantenimiento del orden internacional multilateral y de instituciones como la OMC. En segundo lugar, debemos poner coto al impacto negativo que genera la sobrecapacidad industrial. Tenemos que acabar con los precios artificialmente bajos que impiden que nuestras empresas compitan en igualdad de condiciones. En tercer lugar, tenemos que dar carpetazo a las investigaciones sin fundamento contra productos europeos tan sensibles como los productos de origen porcino, el brandy o los l脙隆cteos. En cuarto lugar, tenemos que lograr el fin de las restricciones selectivas a la exportaci脙鲁n de materias primas fundamentales que ponen en jaque nuestra propia seguridad econ脙鲁mica. Y, por 脙潞ltimo, debemos avanzar hacia una posici脙鲁n m脙隆s responsable por parte de China en el marco de la guerra de Ucrania.

A partir de estas premisas, China y Europa podremos tomar un impulso renovado y garantizar que la relaci脙鲁n contin脙潞e siendo mutuamente beneficiosa. No podemos ni debemos desaprovechar esta oportunidad.

脗聽脗聽Lucia Annunziata (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, subito dopo il cessate il fuoco con l'Iran, la Cina ha consegnato allo stesso Iran una nuova fornitura di missili terra芒聙聭aria, i famosi SAM, secondo il Middle East Eye, notizia non smentita.脗聽Del resto, il 90脗聽% del petrolio iraniano 脙篓 destinato a Pechino ed 脙篓 importantissimo per i prezzi generali del mondo.

Tutto questo per dire che la Cina non 脙篓 la nazione benigna interessata solo al commercio.脗聽Non possiamo illuderci: la Cina 脙篓 interessata, come tutte le grandi potenze, anche alla guerra. Del resto, non c'脙篓 miglior protezionismo economico che quello di avere un forte apparato militare.

Dunque, questa 脙篓 la ragione vera per riaprire il dialogo con la Cina. Dobbiamo capire che cosa sta succedendo in quel paese. Non trascureremo i diritti umani, che resteranno il nostro principale parametro, ma dobbiamo aprire un dialogo con questa nazione, la Cina, che sia indipendente anche dalle informazioni delle altre nazioni e soprattutto dalle informazioni sulla Cina che ci propongono gli Stati Uniti.

Il dialogo in qualche modo 脙篓 parte della nostra indipendenza strategica.

脗聽脗聽Dominik Tarczy脜聞ski (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear leftists, China is laughing. China is laughing at you. Russia is laughing. India is laughing. You know why? Because of your eco-madness, because of your eco-God.

We are responsible for 7脗聽% of pollution of the globe. When China is responsible for 30脗聽%, and Madam von der Leyen wants to save the planet with our money! This is madness.

This madness will be stopped by people of common sense. We Republicans 芒聙聯 we Republicans 芒聙聯 we people of common sense, will restore Europe, rebuild Europe.

It's your fault, communists. It's your fault. Illegal migrants 芒聙聯 it's your fault, communists. Eco-God 芒聙聯 it's your fault, communists. So do not be like communists, be like Poland. We're going to give you another King Sobieski very soon. We're going to restore Europe. We're going to rebuild it. So do not be like communists, be like Poland.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Dost脙隆l (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Dear colleague, I would like to ask for a clarification. You speak against communists, but I'm not aware of communists liquidating the steel industry, liquidating the car industry, liquidating the pharmaceutical industry.

From 1949, we in Czech Republic were able to manufacture our own antibiotics, penicillin. Now we are unable. So why are you speaking against communists when the problem is somewhere else?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dominik Tarczy脜聞ski (ECR), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Because Stalin was the biggest one to kill people of common sense, of values. There is no bigger threat to the world than the communism. And your agenda, your sick agenda 芒聙聯 new God, illegal migration 芒聙聯 is part of the communist way of thinking. They even call themselves communists. It's like someone would call themselves a fascist or a Nazi. How can you call yourself a communist?

This is a shame. I'm going to fight the communists till the end of my life, so help me God.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, collega芒聙聶s, we hebben het hier vandaag al meermaals gehoord: de Chinese exportbeperkingen op onze grondstoffen zijn een van de grootste bedreigingen voor onze Europese industrie. Daarom moeten we ook nu een grens durven stellen aan China. China zal dat niet leuk vinden en zal terugslaan. Het zou echter veel erger zijn om nu te blijven doorgaan alsof er niets aan de hand is, want dat zou ons op termijn nog veel meer kosten. Dan dreigt echt wel het scenario van het Russisch gas.

Deze resolutie, met dank aan het harde werk van collega Bart Groothuis, is dan ook een heel duidelijk signaal aan jullie van de Europese Commissie. Ga voor strategische autonomie, cre脙芦er nieuwe toevoerlijnen met onze bondgenoten, binnen en buiten Europa en wees creatief. Bekijk wat mogelijk is inzake delving op zee en in de ruimte. Collega芒聙聶s, de wereld is groter dan China en de VS. Er zijn hele continenten die op Europa zitten te wachten. Niet het minst Afrika, dat erg naar Europa kijkt, maar door ons stiefmoederlijk behandeld wordt.

Dus, beste Commissie, ik hoop van harte dat jullie een sterke top gaan hebben. Stroop je mouwen op en ga de wereld in.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Majdouline Sbai (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, le 4脗聽avril脗聽2025, la Chine a adopt脙漏 une loi qui interdit l芒聙聶exportation des terres rares. L芒聙聶aveuglement de nos institutions a permis 脙聽 la Chine, depuis des d脙漏cennies, d芒聙聶脙陋tre en situation de quasi-monopole sur les terres rares, avec 61脗聽% de la production et, accrochez-vous bien, 92脗聽% de la capacit脙漏 de traitement.

Vous nous placez donc devant un choix corn脙漏lien: d芒聙聶un c脙麓t脙漏, faire des accords opaques avec un dealer que nous avons nourri depuis des d脙漏cennies, ignorant ainsi le cri des travailleurs de l芒聙聶industrie sacrifi脙漏e; de l芒聙聶autre, 脗芦ne rien c脙漏der 脙聽 la domination, 脙聽 la d脙漏pendance et au chantage脗禄 芒聙聯脗聽je cite ici Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen lors du脗聽G7. C芒聙聶est une 脙漏vidence: il n芒聙聶y a rien 脙聽 脗芦dealer脗禄 avec ceux qui ont r脙漏duit en esclavage le peuple ou脙炉ghour, ceux qui nient l芒聙聶autod脙漏termination des peuples. Rien 脙聽 n脙漏gocier face 脙聽 des pratiques commerciales douteuses.

Une autre voie est possible, celle de l芒聙聶autonomie strat脙漏gique. Pas celle de la course 脙聽 l芒聙聶extractivisme n脙漏ocolonial, non, celle de la planification 脙漏conomique et 脙漏cologique, de la sobri脙漏t脙漏 des usages, de l芒聙聶脙漏conomie circulaire, de la protection des travailleurs et des consommateurs. Aucun secteur, aucun pays ne doit 脙陋tre sacrifi脙漏. L芒聙聶Europe est forte lorsqu芒聙聶elle est unie et d脙漏mocratique, ind脙漏pendante des strat脙漏gies d芒聙聶influence de la Chine.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jussi Saramo (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madam President,脗聽China has shown with raw materials that we are dangerously dependent on it.脗聽But China is also dependent on us. When Trump's United States openly attacks both the EU and China, it would be tempting to leave the United States to fight with itself.

However, China shows every day that it cannot be trusted, by supporting Putin's Russia to kill the people of its neighbouring countries.脗聽Nor must we turn a blind eye to China's shocking human rights violations, for example in Xinjiang.

Since our destinies are intertwined in many ways, China cannot and should not be isolated. However, all strategic dependencies must be broken and the support for killing must end.

The EU must also look in the mirror. To be credible in the world stage, Europe itself must also stop supporting the genocide in Palestine. Now we are considered hypocrites, and for a good reason.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Miriam Lexmann (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, current export controls on several minerals is yet another example of the toolbox China uses for its political purposes. Misusing its dominance over rare earths and deliberately causing disruptions in the supply chains is part of China's trade strategy, which allows it to maintain global dependencies. This puts in danger not only our car producers, but also healthcare, defence and our energy sector.

I expect the Commission to formally raise the issue of unjustified export controls with the WTO, and also to use the anti-corruption mechanism the EU has at its disposal.脗聽It is also high time to accelerate the implementation of the Critical Raw Materials Act we agreed upon about two years ago.

Finally, the upcoming EU-China summit is an opportunity for the EU to raise the dependencies and firmly defend the interests of our industry, our citizens, and also defence of human rights worldwide, but also particularly in China.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bernd Lange (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Ministerin, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! J脙录ngst hatte ich eine Diskussion mit einem Vorsitzenden eines gro脙聼en Unternehmens, der mir sagte: Gegen脙录ber China waren wir etwas naiv. Da habe ich ihm gesagt, wir waren nicht naiv, das war wirtschaftspolitische Strategie. Die Lohnkosten waren gering und vor allen Dingen die Umweltauflagen waren gering. Deswegen sind die Verarbeiter von Rohstoffen zurzeit alle in China. Lithium 芒聙聯 70脗聽% wird in China verarbeitet, obwohl es da nicht gewonnen wird. Also, ich glaube, diese Frage ist eine Frage, die an uns gerichtet ist, wie wir st脙陇rker souver脙陇n werden k脙露nnen, wie wir st脙陇rker andere Zulieferer bekommen aus anderen L脙陇ndern und diese Abh脙陇ngigkeit, die auch von uns verschuldet worden ist, gegen脙录ber China abbauen. Nat脙录rlich werden wir gegen脙录ber China einfordern, dass es fair den Regeln der WTO entsprechend sich verh脙陇lt, und insofern muss die Lizenzgeb脙录hr und das Verfahren gegen脙录ber kritischen Rohstoffen abgebaut werden. Das muss Ziel des Gipfels im Juli sein.

(Der Redner ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Madam President, in accordance with Rule 178 of the Rules of Procedure of the European Parliament, I would also like to ask you for the possibility to react to the reply that Mr脗聽Lange gives to me right now.

Vielen Dank, Herr Kollege, dass Sie die blaue Karte akzeptiert haben. Meine Frage w脙陇re, was man Ihrer Ansicht nach denn tun kann, wenn China sich nicht darauf einl脙陇sst, diese Praktiken, die wir hier mit gro脙聼er Mehrheit kritisieren, tats脙陇chlich dann wieder sein zu lassen? 脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bernd Lange (S&D), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Gl脙录cklicherweise, lieber Kollege, haben wir in den letzten Jahren den Werkzeugkasten deutlich vergr脙露脙聼ert und verbessert: Wir haben das internationale Procurement芒聙聭Instrument geschaffen, was wir jetzt anwenden; wir haben das Foreign芒聙聭Subsidies芒聙聭Instrument angepasst, das wir auch anwenden und das dazu gef脙录hrt hat, dass die Wettbewerbsbedingungen deutlich verbessert worden sind; und wir haben das Anti芒聙聭Coercion芒聙聭Instrument auch noch in der Tasche, sodass, wenn wir wirklich politischen Druck erleben, wir auch das als Gegenma脙聼nahme machen k脙露nnen. Und nat脙录rlich Klagen bei der WTO 芒聙聯 werden wir auch machen. Auch mit einem Partner kann man in aller Deutlichkeit, wenn der Partner unfair spielt, die Ma脙聼nahmen, die man hat, nutzen.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), Reaktion nach dem Verfahren der "blauen Karte". 芒聙聯 Vielen Dank, Herr Kollege, f脙录r diese Ausf脙录hrungen. Ich kann Ihnen da nur zustimmen. Ich glaube, das Entscheidende an dieser Sache ist: Von den ganzen Sachen, die Sie gerade genannt haben脗聽芒聙聯 ich pers脙露nlich kannte davon ungef脙陇hr die H脙陇lfte, ich denke, der durchschnittliche B脙录rger, die durchschnittliche B脙录rgerin der Europ脙陇ischen Union kennt so gut wie gar keins davon. Ich denke, umso wichtiger ist es hier, dass wir diese Instrumente, dass wir die M脙露glichkeiten, die wir haben, in diesem Wettbewerb, in dieser Rivalit脙陇t, der Bev脙露lkerung auch ausreichend erkl脙陇ren, damit die nicht das Gef脙录hl haben, dass die Chinesen uns den ganzen Tag nur auf der Nase herumtanzen.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Isabella Tovaglieri (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, la Commissione europea 脙篓 riuscita a distruggere l'economia del nostro paese, ma di certo ha reso ricca l'economia della Cina.

Infatti, a causa dell'elettrificazione e della transizione ecologica forzata, siamo ormai del tutto dipendenti da un paese comunista e non certamente democratico.

E mentre noi ci impantaniamo nel Green Deal, tra vincoli, divieti e burocrazia verde, la Cina ci ha invaso di merci prodotte senza rispettare alcuna regola, e ci ha rubato la propriet脙聽 intellettuale e i nuovi brevetti tecnologici.

E nel mentre scava, raffina e vende al prezzo che vuole le materie prime, quel prezzo che oggi, guarda caso, sta alzando. Indovinate chi pagher脙聽 il conto definitivo? Noi.

Ecco che il paradosso quindi 脙篓 servito: vogliamo le auto elettriche, le pale eoliche e le batterie, ma ci rifiutiamo di cercare o di produrre le materie necessarie per costruirle in Europa.

Quindi, dobbiamo capire che 脙篓 gi脙聽 troppo tardi e si deve invertire la rotta, altrimenti dalla transizione verde in realt脙聽 rimarremo soltanto al verde.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rihards Kols (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, in two weeks the EU lands in Beijing to mark 50 years of relations with China.脗聽It looks like a celebration; in truth it is a stress test.脗聽Trade still tops EUR脗聽739 billion, yet our deficit is EUR脗聽292 billion, and exports are falling.

State-subsidised overcapacity floods our market while China芒聙聶s stays selectively closed.脗聽As Commissioner 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧 fights in Washington to shield European jobs from blanket US tariffs, Beijing slaps duties on medical devices and cognac, rations rare-earth licences, and declares it 'cannot accept' a Russian defeat in Ukraine.

Treat the summit as business-as-usual and we tell Washington and Moscow that Europe swaps security for cheap batteries.脗聽After five decades China is still no responsible partner.脗聽We need thorough de-risking: tighten investment screening, rebuild critical supply chains with trusted allies, and enforce WTO rules wherever Beijing undercuts fair competition for European workers, business and innovation.

Speak with one transatlantic, not transactional voice. Autonomy built on dependency is not autonomy at all.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Svenja Hahn (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! China flutet unseren Binnenmarkt mit subventionierten 脙聹berkapazit脙陇ten 芒聙聯 von Spielzeug bis Stahl. Ich erwarte von Ursula von der Leyen, dass sie beim EU芒聙聭China芒聙聭Gipfel pragmatische L脙露sungen f脙录r faireren Wettbewerb f脙录r unsere Industrie findet. Aber vor allem darf Europa nicht wieder so naiv gegen脙录ber China werden. Denn seit Jahren k脙陇mpfen wir m脙录hsam darum, uns von diesen asymmetrischen Abh脙陇ngigkeiten zu befreien. Wir haben uns als Deutschland, als Europa von China einlullen lassen. Gerade zeigt China doch mit den Restriktionen im Export f脙录r schwere Seltene Erden, dass sie bereit sind, unsere Abh脙陇ngigkeiten als Waffe zu nutzen. Denn im Konflikt zwischen Autokratie und Demokratie d脙录rfen wir nicht vergessen, dass China ein autokratisches Regime ist, das Demokratie und Menschenrechte nur st脙露ren. Denken wir an die Unterdr脙录ckung der Uiguren, an Hongkong, an die Drohungen gegen Taiwan.

Jetzt ist nicht die Zeit f脙录r Appeasement, weder gegen脙录ber Trump/USA noch gegen脙录ber China. Jetzt ist Zeit f脙录r klare Kante und geschlossene St脙陇rke. Wir brauchen mehr Handel mit mehr Partnern. Ich baue auf die d脙陇nische Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft, Ministerin Bjerre, sammeln Sie die Mitgliedsl脙陇nder f脙录r eine Offensive f脙录r mehr Freihandel. 脙聳ffnen wir neue M脙陇rkte und Quellen f脙录r Rohstoffe.

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Szczerba (PPE). 芒聙聯 Wysoka Izbo, 脜录yjemy w czasach, kiedy ca脜聜y czas wyzwaniem pozostaje pe脜聜noskalowa wojna na Ukrainie. R脙鲁wnie脜录 wyzwaniem pozostaj脛聟 relacje transatlantyckie i w tej sytuacji Komisja i uczestnicy szczytu b脛聶d脛聟 rozmawia脛聡 z Chinami. Po pierwsze wsparcie dla wojny na Ukrainie. I o tym trzeba m脙鲁wi脛聡 wprost. Ta wojna nie mog脜聜aby trwa脛聡, gdyby nie wsparcie kraj脙鲁w, kt脙鲁re ka脜录dego dnia dostarczaj脛聟 albo ira脜聞skie drony, albo komponenty, dzi脛聶ki kt脙鲁rym ta wojna jest prowadzona i r脙鲁wnie脜录 wspierana jest gospodarka wojenna Federacji Rosyjskiej.

I bardzo prosimy o to, 脜录eby to podej脜聸cie by脜聜o uwzgl脛聶dnione. Nie ma business as usual wtedy, kiedy od trzech lat wspiera si脛聶 wojn脛聶, kt脙鲁ra jest r脙鲁wnie脜录 ludob脙鲁jstwem.

脗聽脗聽Javi L脙鲁pez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, China representa hoy una sexta脗聽parte脗聽de la humanidad, ocupa una silla en el Consejo de Seguridad de las Naciones Unidas, tiene el 30脗聽% de la producci脙鲁n industrial del planeta y ha pasado de ser el principal socio comercial de diez脗聽pa脙颅ses en el mundo en el a脙卤o 2000 a serlo hoy de 120. Es una realidad innegable, gigantesca, compleja y, sin duda, aprender a lidiar con ella es uno de los mayores retos geopol脙颅ticos y econ脙鲁micos que tiene Europa, porque est脙隆n nuestros intereses en juego.

脗驴Qu脙漏 es lo que espero que hagamos en la Cumbre? 脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pierre-Romain Thionnet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, 脗芦cacher ses talents pour mieux attendre son heure脗禄. C芒聙聶脙漏tait le conseil donn脙漏 par l芒聙聶ancien num脙漏ro un de la R脙漏publique populaire de Chine Deng脗聽Xiaoping. P脙漏kin a d脙漏ploy脙漏 une grande strat脙漏gie visant 脙聽 combler ses d脙漏pendances critiques 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏gard du reste du monde. La Chine a constitu脙漏 des stocks strat脙漏giques et a organis脙漏 la transhumance vers son territoire des industries de l芒聙聶Occident pour mieux l芒聙聶inonder en retour de ses surproductions.

Les 脗芦Trente insouciantes脗禄 volent en 脙漏clats, m脙陋me si certains voudraient s芒聙聶accrocher 脙聽 cette parenth脙篓se hallucin脙漏e. 脗聽脗聽Tobiasz Boche脜聞ski (ECR). 芒聙聯 Szanowna Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Ta debata pokazuje, 脜录e szanowna biurokracja i szanowna wi脛聶kszo脜聸脛聡 w tej Izbie odlecia脜聜a w kosmos bez pomocy rakiety Elona Muska. O czym my dyskutujemy? Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo, w roku 2005 nominalne PKB Chin wynosi脜聜o 2,3 biliona dolar脙鲁w, za to kraj脙鲁w Unii Europejskiej 13 bilion脙鲁w. Byli脜聸my 5 razy bogatsi od Chin. A w tym roku? Zawdzi脛聶czamy to waszej absurdalnej, nonsensownej polityce gospodarczej, kt脙鲁r脛聟 uprawiacie od lat. Zawdzi脛聶czamy to Ursuli von der Leyen. Zawdzi脛聶czamy to Zielonemu 脜聛adowi. 脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wouter Beke (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, leden van de Commissie, collega芒聙聶s, wij spreken hier in dit halfrond vaak over strategische autonomie als het gaat over onze defensie, als het gaat over onze voeding, als het gaat over onze medicijnen. Telkens is de boodschap: we moeten de touwtjes meer in eigen handen kunnen nemen om onze burgers in de toekomst te kunnen beschermen. Dat geldt ook voor kritische grondstoffen. Zonder soms goed en wel te beseffen, zijn zij de onzichtbare motor geworden van onze samenleving. Want zonder kritische grondstoffen, geen smartphones, geen elektrische wagens, geen windmolens.

Net voor die kritische grondstoffen zijn we ongelooflijk afhankelijk van China: 90脗聽%. We spreken dus eigenlijk niet over een louter handelsonevenwicht. Nee, het gaat over een strategische zwakte, die China gebruikt. Want China bepaalt of en op welke manier kritische grondstoffen gebruikt kunnen worden en mogen worden in Europa. Wel, dit is onaanvaardbaar.

Tijdens de aankomende EU-China-top kan het geen business as usual zijn. Europa mag niet de speelbal worden van Beijing en moet daarom, wat de kritische materialen betreft, op tafel kloppen, zoals u net gedaan heeft, mevrouw de Voorzitter.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Niels Fuglsang (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Fru kommiss脙娄r! Fru minister! K脙娄re kollegaer! Kina er en vigtig samarbejdspartner for Europa, en vigtig handelspartner og ogs脙楼 en samarbejdspartner i forhold til at l脙赂se nogle af de store udfordringer som f.eks. klimakrisen. Men vi m脙楼 samtidig indse, at vi har f脙赂rt en naiv Kinapolitik, hvor vi har gjort os afh脙娄ngige af det store rige i midten. Vi har solgt kritisk infrastruktur, og Gr脙娄kenland har solgt sin st脙赂rste havn til Kina, Athens havn, Pir脙娄us. Vi har gjort os afh脙娄ngige af kritiske r脙楼stoffer, sj脙娄ldne jordarter fra Kina. Vi har gjort os afh脙娄ngige af deres teknologi, n脙楼r det g脙娄lder den gr脙赂nne omstilling. 95% af de solceller, der s脙娄ttes op i Europa i dag, kommer fra Kina. Vi bliver n脙赂dt til at g脙赂re os uafh脙娄ngige. Det m脙楼 v脙娄re det, vores Kinapolitik best脙楼r i. Skal vi ikke give hinanden h脙楼ndslag p脙楼, at vi fremover ikke s脙娄lger kritisk infrastruktur til Kina, at vi beholder den i Europa, og at vi g脙赂r, hvad vi kan for at g脙赂re os uafh脙娄ngige, n脙楼r det kommer til den kritiske teknologi. Det ville v脙娄re en god start.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paulo Cunha (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, a Uni脙拢o Europeia tem de ser mais firme e agir rapidamente perante a China.

Setores vitais, como o t脙陋xtil, a metalurgia, os pl脙隆sticos, o autom脙鲁vel, citando apenas alguns, vivem uma crise sem precedentes.

Enquanto as empresas europeias cumprem regras sociais, laborais e ambientais justas e exigentes, as empresas chinesas aproveitam-se das falhas das nossas leis ou ignoram-nas por completo. Com subs脙颅dios dados pelo Estado, abuso das exce脙搂脙碌es de minimis, falta de reciprocidade no acesso ao mercado chin脙陋s, entre outros, s脙拢o a concorr脙陋ncia desleal que fecha empresas, muitas delas pequenas ou familiares, que elimina empregos na Europa e que tira recursos e impostos essenciais para financiar o nosso modelo social.

Como pretendemos falar de competitividade, se permitimos que as nossas empresas sejam arrastadas por pr脙隆ticas injustas? Passou o tempo das palavras. Precisamos de a脙搂脙碌es firmes e concretas para defender quem gera emprego, paga impostos e sustenta o nosso modelo europeu que tanto valorizamos.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elio Di Rupo (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, la pr脙漏sidente von der Leyen l芒聙聶a rappel脙漏, on a de profondes divergences avec la Chine: libert脙漏s fondamentales, soutien 脙聽 la Russie, pratiques 脙漏conomiques d脙漏loyales, monopoles 芒聙聯脗聽notamment pour ce qui est des mati脙篓res premi脙篓res critiques脗聽芒聙聯, sanctions contre les d脙漏put脙漏s芒聙娄 Mais la Chine est un acteur mondial incontournable, et il serait irresponsable de freiner le dialogue avec P脙漏kin.

Bien entendu, l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne doit d脙漏fendre ses valeurs et affirmer ses int脙漏r脙陋ts tout en intensifiant un dialogue honn脙陋te, sans donner l芒聙聶impression de faire la le脙搂on, et en pleine compr脙漏hension mutuelle. Mais, Monsieur le Commissaire, je pense aussi qu芒聙聶il faut 脙陋tre ferme et montrer 脙聽 la Chine que l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne est un vaste march脙漏 et que, si nous nous bloquions, nous pourrions entrer dans un cercle qui serait un cercle v脙漏ritablement n脙漏gatif, bien s脙禄r pour nous, mais 脙漏galement pour la Chine.

Je vous invite vraiment 脙聽 r脙漏tablir un dialogue renouvel脙漏 avec les Chinois.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasa Juknevi脛聧ien脛聴 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, my first message: engagement policies and economic dependence on autocratic regimes are dangerous. This is exactly what happened to us with Putin's Russia. Let's not make the same mistakes.

Secondly, the EU-China Comprehensive Agreement on Investment cannot be ratified until China stops its economic coercion against EU Member States. China's economic coercive actions against Lithuania and the EU back in 2021 grossly violated the legal norms of the World Trade Organisation. These discriminatory trade restrictions, directed at an EU Member State, challenged the unity of the entire bloc. Failing to take appropriate action against China's economic coercion will cause long-term damage to the reputation of the European Union as a reliable geopolitical actor.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Procedura "catch-the-eye"

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francisco Jos脙漏 Mill脙隆n Mon (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, la pr脙鲁xima Cumbre con China es una buena ocasi脙鲁n para abordar al m脙隆s alto nivel asuntos bilaterales y globales.

La relaci脙鲁n bilateral no pasa por un buen momento: tenemos un enorme y creciente d脙漏ficit comercial, en buena parte debido a las dificultades de nuestras empresas para acceder al mercado de China,脗聽una China que, adem脙隆s, bloquea nuestro acceso a minerales cr脙颅ticos mientras sus empresas se benefician ampliamente de nuestro mercado.

En la Cumbre debemos exigir un campo de juego equilibrado y avanzar en la diversificaci脙鲁n hacia otros mercados mientras continuamos la reducci脙鲁n del riesgo. La Cumbre tambi脙漏n ser脙隆 una gran oportunidad para tratar retos globales: cambio clim脙隆tico, pandemias, crimen organizado y narcotr脙隆fico, pesca ilegal y, desde luego, la paz.

Es decepcionante la cooperaci脙鲁n sin l脙颅mites de China con Rusia. China debe contribuir activamente a la paz, no ayudar al agresor. Tambi脙漏n debemos vigilar que nuestras exportaciones a China de material de doble uso no acaben convertidas en armas rusas.

Para terminar, vivimos en un mundo muy complejo. China, como gran potencia, debe asumir sus responsabilidades globales. En la Cumbre debemos insistir en este punto y tambi脙漏n avanzar para corregir los desequilibrios.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, domnule comisar, stima脠聸i colegi, 50 de ani de rela脠聸ie cu China 脠聶i, dac脛聝 facem un bilan脠聸, constat脛聝m c脛聝 am pierdut 脙庐n ace脠聶ti 50 de ani mult din ceea ce puteam s脛聝 avem.

Dar eu am 脙庐nv脛聝脠聸at de la un investitor din 脠聸ara doamnei pre脠聶edint脛聝 Ursula von der Leyen c脛聝 nu po脠聸i s脛聝 omori concuren脠聸a, ci trebuie s脛聝 脙庐nve脠聸i inteligent cum s脛聝 o dep脛聝脠聶e脠聶ti. De aceea, eu cred c脛聝 la summit noi trebuie s脛聝 punem condi脠聸ii foarte clare. Noi trebuie s脛聝 avem posibilitatea s脛聝 ne ap脛聝r脛聝m.

Ieri am discutat despre pachetele, 91% venite din China, de p脙垄n脛聝 la 150 de euro, miliarde de pachete f脛聝r脛聝 siguran脠聸a produselor, o concuren脠聸脛聝 neloial脛聝 pentru IMM-uri. Iat脛聝 de ce, domnule comisar, eu cred foarte clar c脛聝 avem nevoie de rela脠聸ia cu China, dar trebuie s脛聝 o facem pe principii asem脛聝n脛聝toare. Dac脛聝 China d脛聝 20% la autohtone la licita脠聸ii publice, de ce nu punem 脠聶i noi aceste condi脠聸ii la licita脠聸iile publice cu China?

脠聵i da, infrastructura important脛聝 脠聶i de securitate nu trebuie v脙垄ndut脛聝 de niciunul dintre statele membre ale Uniunii Europene. Doar a脠聶a putem s脛聝 avem. Singuri nu putem fi pe lume. Cu Statele Unite, nu avem o rela脠聸ie bun脛聝, cu China, nu avem o rela脠聸ie bun脛聝, cu Rusia, 脠聶tim de ce nu mai e bun脛聝.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, it's hard to believe that some politicians call for reducing dependency on the United States 芒聙聯 our closest ally 芒聙聯 while simultaneously pushing for closer ties with China. A regime that systematically violates human rights, oppresses minorities, and steals European technology is a serious security threat.

The balance of trade between Europe and China is already sharply in China's favour 芒聙聯 in favour of a communist dictatorship that has long traditions of using economic relations as a tool for political pressure.

Europe should not build stronger trade relations with a country whose representative for European relations questions the independence of sovereign Eastern European states that seceded from the Soviet Union. This says enough. The growth of Chinese influence in Europe must be prevented.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dainius 脜陆alimas (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, as the President of the Commission emphasised yesterday, when we go to China to defend our interests, Europe must show strength. This strength only comes through our unity, and unity must be based on our common values. Therefore, we must have in mind several important aspects.

Firstly, China's position in Russia's war against Ukraine 芒聙聯 China's support to the aggressor cannot be tolerated. Secondly, inadmissibility of political, economic and diplomatic pressure on individual EU Member States, for example on Lithuania. Thirdly, inadmissibility of the use of, or threat of, force against the liberal democracy in Taiwan, which is freely established and developed on the same principles as ours. Fourthly, stopping the destruction of the unique identity of the Tibetan people, as well as interference into their religious freedom. Fifthly, ceasing the grave breaches of human rights against Uyghurs in Hong Kong and elsewhere in China.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Billy Kelleher (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I welcome the EU-China summit that's happening in the near future, because we do need a reset of relationships between the European Union and China. We need to have reciprocity. We need to have mutual respect and fairness. We need to have access to the Chinese market for European companies. We need to ensure that we wean our dependency off China in certain areas of raw materials and rare earths.

And we need to insist on a rules-based order, an international rules-based order, the World Trade Organisation, the United Nations and the others that keep this world revolving around fairness and respect for each other.

On the issue of Ukraine, we have to call it out straight: China is actively supporting Russia in the war in a European country. And that has to be said very loud and clearly. We can't have a situation where we dance around this particular fact. And until such time as China says clearly that they respect the international integrity of the Ukrainian borders, well, then we must always call them out.

But I wish the summit well, because we do need a reset and we need to rebalance it, ensuring that it is in Europe's favour as well.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). 芒聙聯 Gerbiama Pirmininke, nor脛聴脛聧iau pritarti Komisijos pateiktai pozicijai ir i脜隆ties paraginti, kad derybose, atnaujintose derybose, b脜芦t脜鲁 i脜隆keliami tie klausimai, kurie b脜芦t脜鲁 apibr脛聴脜戮ti kaip tarptautin脛聴s teis脛聴s prioriteto palaikymas visuose santykiuose. 脜聽iuo atveju ypatingai svarbu atkreipti d脛聴mes脛炉 Kinijos 脛炉 tai, kad karas, kur脛炉 Rusija t脛聶sia prie脜隆 Ukrain脛聟, turi b脜芦ti sustabdytas, nes tai kenkia pasaulinei tvarkai, daugia脜隆ali脜隆kumui ir tarptautin脛聴s teis脛聴s vir脜隆enyb脛聴s principui. Prie脜隆ingu atveju mes i脜隆 ties脜鲁 tur脛聴sime labai sud脛聴tingus santykius. A脜隆 skatinu b脜芦tinai pragmati脜隆kai, dalyki脜隆kai, bet apsibr脛聴脜戮iant tuos principus, kurie 脜隆iandien yra numeris vienas. 脜聽alies teritorinis integralumas, tarptautin脛聴 teis脛聴 yra tai, apie k脛聟 turi b脜芦ti pasakyta labai ai脜隆kiai, kad Kinijos parama Ukrainai b脜芦t脜鲁 sustabdyta.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, contra o perigo da guerra, construir solu脙搂脙碌es de paz. Contra a pol脙颅tica de confronta脙搂脙拢o, refor脙搂ar os la脙搂os de coopera脙搂脙拢o em benef脙颅cio m脙潞tuo dos povos.脗聽Esta deve ser a orienta脙搂脙拢o para o desenvolvimento das rela脙搂脙碌es com a China e, naturalmente, para a cimeira que agora se realiza.

A Uni脙拢o Europeia n脙拢o deve ser uma caixa de resson脙垄ncia da pol脙颅tica de confronta脙搂脙拢o e de escalada de tens脙碌es dos Estados Unidos, que aponta a China como seu alvo preferencial. O caminho tem de ser outro: refor脙搂ar rela脙搂脙碌es de efetiva coopera脙搂脙拢o, no respeito pelo Direito Internacional, incluindo o princ脙颅pio da n脙拢o inger脙陋ncia e o respeito pelo princ脙颅pio de uma s脙鲁 China, sem hesita脙搂脙碌es ou distor脙搂脙碌es.脗聽Estreitar rela脙搂脙碌es pol脙颅ticas, diplom脙隆ticas, econ脙鲁micas e culturais em condi脙搂脙碌es mutuamente vantajosas para os povos e tendo em conta as suas necessidades.

Esse caminho deve ser percorrido, respeitando o espa脙搂o pr脙鲁prio dos Estados芒聙聭Membros para a sua pr脙鲁pria pol脙颅tica externa e rela脙搂脙碌es bilaterais, designadamente脗聽as rela脙搂脙碌es entre Portugal e a China, aprofundando rela脙搂脙碌es hist脙鲁ricas de amizade, paz e coopera脙搂脙拢o entre os povos portugu脙陋s e chin脙陋s.

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maro脜隆 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Ministers, honourable Members, I know that we are very much pressed by the time and we expect the arrival of the Prime Minister of Denmark, so I'll be very short.

First and foremost, I would like to thank all the honourable Members for their interventions, which for sure will contribute to the final preparation of the EU-China summit.

I also would like to appreciate that there was what I would describe as overall support for our approach to de-risk, but not to decouple in relations with China. It was very clearly the motto and the guideline principle that the Commission was following since the start of the mandate.

If you allow me a couple of words on de-risking, the honourable MEPs Lange, Maniatis, Eroglu, Kelleher, but especially Ms脗聽Bentele,脗聽highlighted how important it is to get better access to the critical raw materials.

I have, in particular, to thank Ms脗聽Bentele for exceptional work done on the critical raw materials report, because thanks to her, we opened new chapters in how to increase the economic security of the European Union. Based on that report, and with the new approach of the European Commission, we already completed the first selection process of more than 60 projects when it comes to critical raw materials 芒聙聯 47 in Europe and 13 outside.

I believe that this exercise, which will be repeated and which will be now tested, in getting these projects from on paper into real operation, will demonstrate how serious we are about being more independent when it comes to the supply of critical raw materials.

Coming back to China, I can also reassure you that all the issues you've been clearly highlighting 芒聙聯 be it the trade restrictions in the field of agriculture, cosmetics, pharmaceutical business or in public procurement 芒聙聯 are permanently raised on the technical level, but also by me in my regular interactions with the Chinese Trade Minister, Wang Wentao, and we are permanently pushing for the resolution of these issues.

But to conclude, I agree with all of you that what we need in Europe is to build stronger resilience, and for that, the policies which we charted together, like the Net-Zero Industry Act, and being stronger in getting critical raw materials to Europe, improving our competitiveness, would be the best responses to all the challenges which are ahead of us.

All the political issues in relation to human rights, to the war in Ukraine and to overall global geopolitics will for sure be raised by the leaders at the summit, as indicated by the President of the Commission and by the representative of the Presidency.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioners, thank you for a good debate. Many important topics were mentioned: imbalanced trade support for Ukraine, respect for human rights, partnerships on global issues such as climate, together with many other issues. EU-China relations are complex, as many of you said. We value the trade and economic relationship with China, but it must benefit both sides.

Mr Pascual de la Parte, from the EPP, Mr Maniatis from S&D and Mr Eroglu from Renew, together with many of your colleagues, all mentioned trade and control of earth materials, so I want to elaborate a little bit more on that issue. Our trade relations with China remain, unfortunately, unbalanced, both in terms of trade and investments. We have long-standing concerns about lack of level playing field and the significant asymmetry in our respective market openings.

In addition, China's economic model has led to systemic distortions with negative spillovers to trading partners. EU firms have continued to face forced localisation policies and restrictions on the access to the Chinese market, as well as pressure resulting from overcapacities. The deficit in trade in goods has been widening over the last decade, fuelled by illegal subsidies. Last year, the EU-China trade deficit reached EUR脗聽304.5 billion. A more recent headache is the export control measures on rare earth and related products introduced by China, which impact EU producers.

The EU will continue to reduce critical dependencies and vulnerabilities, including the supply chains, and will de-risk and diversify where necessary and appropriate. We do not intend to decouple or to turn inwards. A balanced relationship built on fairness and reciprocity in our common interest 芒聙聯 this will be the message that the EU will deliver to China at the summit, seeking to press China to concrete steps in addressing them.

As regards Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine, China has spoken out in favour of peace, but we need to see actions to follow these words. Engagement and cooperation remain essential, including on human rights, as mentioned by Mr Niinist脙露 from the Greens and by many others.

We are committed to working with China to improve bilateral relations and to address concerns through dialogue where possible. At the same time, we must remain firm in protecting our interests and our values. We need to find long-term solutions to tackle global challenges and ensure that the rules-based international order is upheld, with the UN Charter at the core.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 Comunico di aver ricevuto sei proposte di risoluzione a conclusione della discussione, conformemente all'articolo 136, paragrafo 2, del regolamento.

La discussione 脙篓 chiusa.

La votazione si svolger脙聽 gioved脙卢 10 luglio 2025.

Dichiarazioni scritte (articolo 178)

脗聽脗聽Katar脙颅na Roth Neve脛聫alov脙隆 (NI), p脙颅somne. 芒聙聯 Ver脙颅m, 脜戮e by sme sa mali sna脜戮i脜楼 viac budova脜楼 mosty ako ich p脙隆li脜楼. Potrebujeme partnerov, s ktor脙陆mi m脙麓脜戮eme obchodova脜楼 a s ktor脙陆mi sa vieme dohodn脙潞脜楼 na spolo脛聧n脙陆ch z脙隆ujmoch. U脜戮 dlho sa tu pretl脙隆脛聧a, 脜戮e m脙隆me nepriate脛戮ov a sme s nimi v obchodnej vojne. Jedn脙陆m z nich je pod脛戮a niektor脙陆ch z v脙隆s 脛聦脙颅na. Pod脛戮a m脜聢a je 脛聦脙颅na pr脙颅le脜戮itos脜楼ou. Ver脙颅m, 脜戮e nadch脙隆dzaj脙潞ci samit E脙職 芒聙聯 脛聦脙颅na vyu脜戮ijeme na zlep脜隆enie spolupr脙隆ce, komunik脙隆cie a otvorenie 脛聫al脜隆脙颅ch nov脙陆ch spolo脛聧n脙陆ch kapitol. 脛聦脙颅na je nielen v脙陆znamn脙陆 producent, ale aj 脜楼a脜戮脙颅 ve脛戮k脙漏 mno脜戮stvo surov脙颅n pre E脙職 d脙麓le脜戮it脙陆ch.

V tomto roku oslavujeme okr脙潞hle v脙陆ro脛聧ie spolo脛聧n脙陆ch diplomatick脙陆ch vz脜楼ahov a to je pr脙颅le脜戮itos脜楼ou na ich rev脙颅ziu a nov脙陆 脜隆tart. Moment脙隆lne obmedzenie v脙陆vozu niektor脙陆ch surov脙颅n z 脛聦脙颅ny je tie脜戮 odrazom na脜隆ich posledn脙陆ch polit脙颅k vo vz脜楼ahu k tejto krajine. Vyu脜戮ime mo脜戮nos脜楼 samitu na to, aby sme op脙陇脜楼 boli partnermi a vz脜楼ahy zlep脜隆ili. Spolupr脙隆ca s touto krajinou je v s脙潞脛聧asnej turbulentnej medzin脙隆rodnej situ脙隆cii a st脙隆le rast脙潞cej hrozbe ve脛戮k脙漏ho konfliktu k脛戮脙潞脛聧ov脙隆 na upokojenie a nastolenie medzin脙隆rodn脙漏ho poriadku a re脜隆pektu k medzin脙隆rodn脙漏mu pr脙隆vu. Spolo脛聧ne dok脙隆脜戮eme toho ove脛戮a viac. 脛聦脙颅nsky technologick脙陆 pokrok je obrovsk脙陆 a m脙麓脜戮eme sa u脛聧i脜楼 navz脙隆jom. Sme exportne orientovanou ekonomikou, a tak by n脙隆m na dobr脙陆ch vz脜楼ahoch s 脛聦脙颅nou malo naozaj z脙隆le脜戮a脜楼.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Susana Sol脙颅s P脙漏rez (PPE), in writing. 芒聙聯 Europe芒聙聶s dependence on China for critical raw materials is not new. It芒聙聶s structural. And it芒聙聶s becoming a strategic vulnerability. China controls a significant share of global CRM supply and has used this dominance to restrict exports, from rare earths in 2011 to gallium, germanium and graphite in 2023. This pattern exposes EU industries to bottlenecks, price shocks and loss of competitiveness.

From automotive to wind energy and defence, our key sectors rely on materials we import over 50脗聽% from China. This reality demands more than speeches. We need European-scale action.

We welcome the Commission芒聙聶s clear priorities: rebalancing our economic relationship with China, reducing strategic risks, and advancing cooperation on global issues like climate. But these goals require concrete tools.

Europe must invest in its own CRM value chains, from mining to recycling, and boost European-specific financing for our strategic projects. We also need to diversify supply through partnerships with reliable allies such as the recently signed trade agreements with Mexico and Canada.

Strategic autonomy is not a slogan. It芒聙聶s an industrial and geopolitical imperative. We must shift from dependency to resilience. From short-term fixes to long-term solutions. Our competitiveness and sovereignty depend on it.
