4. Presentation of the programme of activities of the Danish Presidency (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the debate on the presentation of the programme of activities of the Danish Presidency (2025/2538(RSP)).

Prime Minister Frederiksen, dear Mette, dear colleagues, on 1脗聽July, Denmark assumed the presidency of the Council of the European Union. Today, Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen joins us to present the Presidency's programme for the months ahead. So, Prime Minister, dear Mette, welcome to the European Parliament.

(Applause)

Nearly one year into this legislature, the Danish Presidency arrives at a decisive moment. In a world marked by uncertainty, we need a Europe that is stronger and that is safer. That means taking responsibility for our own security. It means scaling up efforts to address migration and tackling high energy costs. It means giving our defence industry a much-needed boost and remaining steadfast in our support for Ukraine. We know that a more secure Europe also depends on a strong and competitive economy, so we have accelerated in this House reforms and we are pushing ahead with our simplification agenda. But we also need to make full use of our single market and, crucially, unlock more capital.

Decisions must turn quickly into action. As I am sure you will hear, Prime Minister, in our debate today, companies need resources to grow and businesses need predictability to succeed. Denmark gets that. You understand that security starts with strength, and that strength is built through smart choices, and you are leading truly by example. Next week, the Commission will present its proposal for the next multiannual financial framework. The Danish Presidency will have a chance to show once again that commitment.

Here in Parliament, we are ready. We are already setting out our priorities, and we have done so in a resolution. Our expectations are clear: our long-term budget needs to meet today's challenges; it must be strategic; it must be responsible and flexible enough to adapt.

Dear Prime Minister, the Danish Presidency's motto 芒聙聯 'A strong Europe in a changing world' 芒聙聯 captures exactly what is needed and resonates deeply in this House. Security and competitiveness must remain the twin engines driving our work. As we work together to build a stronger, smarter and safer Europe, this Parliament stands alongside you as a partner and as a friend.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mette Frederiksen, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, 'Europe will be forged in crises and will be the sum of the solutions adopted for those crises'. These are the words of Jean Monnet, one of the pioneers and founding fathers of European cooperation. Today, ladies and gentlemen, these words seem truer than ever before.

Dear Members of the European Parliament, Madam President and Commissioners, thank you for inviting me today. For the next six months, Denmark will hold the Presidency of the European Union. We do so at a time where Europe is facing the greatest challenges since the 1940s: Russia's brutal war against Ukraine, pressure on Europe's external borders from migration, conflicts in the Middle East, risk of trade war and tariffs on the rise and, increasingly, competition on the future of strategic technologies. And on top of all this: the climate and biodiversity crisis.

Our security, our values, our economies and our way of living is under pressure. We need to address the challenges head on and we need to do it together. Members of the European Parliament and Member States: we share the responsibility to find the right compromises on the difficult questions ahead of us. Solutions that work for all of us; first of all, our citizens, but also our businesses, labour unions and civil society. That is why I am so pleased to be here today.

The threats may be many and they are indeed serious, but I have trust in Europe; trust that we can move our continent forward, build a more secure Europe, a greener and more competitive Europe, a new Europe of tomorrow with possibilities and welfare for our citizens. But only if we remember what we are capable of, what our societies are built upon and who we really are as Europeans.

Our strengths come from within. We are much more than the reach of our countries and the sum of our populations 芒聙聯 Europe is first and foremost an idea. It is the way we think: our fundamental values 芒聙聯 freedom, democracy, the rule of law; our firm belief in progress, science and common sense; the fact that we always seek to make tomorrow better than today; our social model; and that we are free to think as we want, believe what we want and speak as we see fit. These are the fundamental ideas on which our continent is built, and they run in the blood of all Europeans.

Europe is cooperation, discussions and difficult compromises. But, above all, Europe is a magnificent, rich and diverse continent that has managed to rise again and again.

In a time where our fundamental values are being questioned, the response from our side needs to be firm and to be clear: we must in every way possible be willing to protect our democracies, our freedom and rights, and our prosperity. Therefore, the Danish Presidency has two overall priorities: first of all, of course, a secure Europe, and secondly, a competitive and green Europe.

First of all, we must take responsibility for our own security. Cutting our defence spending in the past 30脗聽years was a huge mistake and we shall never repeat it. We cannot take the threat from the East lightly. Russia's military rearming means that they could, within two to five years, pose a credible military threat to Europe and NATO, and Russia is no longer working alone. We see it in Ukraine: North Korea providing soldiers and ammunition, missiles from Iran, and military technology from China.

We must act now to respond to this threat. We need to build a much stronger European defence industry 芒聙聯 we have to invest more. Strengthening Europe's defence industry is an absolute top priority and we have to be able to defend ourselves by 2030 at the latest. We support an ambitious roadmap to get us there, as outlined in the June European Council conclusions. We have made a ReArm Europe investment plan and the Danish Presidency will take forward the proposals on defence simplification and the European Defence Industry Programme.

At the same time, we have to learn from Ukraine. In a very short time, they have established an impressive defence industry, producing weapons and ammunition much faster than we can, developing advanced drones and weapons while fighting a war 芒聙聯 that is quite impressive. We have to continue our support to Ukraine in every way we can as Europeans, for as long as it takes.

(Applause)

Military support, ammunition, missiles, air defence systems with investments into Ukraine's defence industry, engaging in partnerships with Ukrainian companies and integrating the Ukrainian defence industry into the European, to our mutual benefit, and with financial, civil and, of course, political support. We need to look at our donations for Ukraine in a different way. Our donations are not a gift and they are not charity; they are a direct defence of Europe, our life and our freedom.

We also have to increase pressure on Russia with the toughest possible sanctions. We will drive forward the proposals to stop all imports of Russian gas.

(Applause)

All these measures are interlinked, so you have to applaud once more, because we have to take the next steps in the enlargement process for Ukraine, Moldova and the Western Balkans. By bringing these countries closer to Europe, we can help them flourish and grow and make the European Union even stronger.

Another part of our security as Europeans is migration. It is challenging Europe, affecting people's lives and the cohesion of our societies. We saw it very clearly in the European Parliament elections last year: migration was a top priority for many Europeans, including myself. Our citizens expect us politicians to find new solutions, with good reason, and European citizens have a right to feel safe in their own countries. That is why we need to strengthen our external borders. We have to lower the influx of migrants to Europe. We need to help stabilise Europe, neighbouring countries and make the process of returns easier and, of course, also more efficient.

(Applause)

What has been mainstream among our populations for many years is now mainstream for many of us politicians as well, finally. Maybe not in Parliament, but gladly, and I'm really happy about that, in the European Council. So of course, as the Presidency, we will prioritise the proposals on the table and also provide a much more effective response to Russia, who is using migration as a weapon at our eastern borders.

So that was a few words on security 芒聙聯 we also need a competitive and green Europe. As we all know, Draghi published his report last year; he painted quite a bleak picture of too much regulation, high energy costs, a significant innovation and investment gap and a shrinking workforce. Europe is now falling behind and we ourselves bear a large part of the responsibility. These issues have become a top priority and luckily there is a general understanding that we need to act with urgency already this year. It has to be a top priority for all of us and therefore we must simplify our rules. We have to reduce burdens on businesses, citizens and public authorities 芒聙聯 the Danish Presidency will aim for less red tape for European companies and individuals. Therefore, we must make progress on the Omnibus proposals and simplification agenda.

But, of course, we already know that more is needed. To increase the European Union's long-term competitiveness we need to boost our ability to innovate, develop and produce the technologies of tomorrow. We need to do it with urgency and ambition but, of course, without compromising what makes Europe special: our social balance, our democratic stability, our green ambitions.

Climate change remains one of the greatest challenges of the world right now. Billions of people around the world are already feeling the consequences 芒聙聯 extreme weather, fires. It is leading to migration, poverty and political instability. Europe needs to keep pushing for an ambitious global green transition. That is why we need to agree on an ambitious 2040 climate target.

(Applause)

I don't see the applause coming from the whole Parliament on the whole speech 芒聙聯 but this is important also to demonstrate that we take our climate leadership on our shoulders globally. We have to create a stable and clear political framework so businesses can be assured of our long-term direction. A just green transition, providing clean and affordable energy, new jobs and new opportunities. This means a better future for all of us 芒聙聯 this is a key part of our long-term competitiveness.

We have to build more of our own clean energy, more and better infrastructure, and work towards a stronger energy union: ensuring low and stable energy prices for citizens and our businesses, strengthening our security of supply and, of course, at the same time, getting out of our dependency on Russian energy. We can use our trade policy to diversify and make supply chains more robust and, while others build tariff walls, we should form new trade partnerships. Get the agreement with Mercosur over the line and push on with partnerships, enabling access to critical raw materials.

To enable the EU to deliver results, the Danish Presidency will help set an ambitious and responsible course for negotiating the EU's next long-term budget. We have to rethink the structure of our budget. We have to make it more flexible to manage unforeseen events. We have to make it simpler, mobilise more private capital and make it more focused on political priorities. For me, the status quo is not an option. Last time we called the long-term budget negotiations 'the most difficult' 芒聙聯 it was just after COVID-19 and Brexit. This time I believe they will be 'the most important'.

Dear friends in Parliament, Jean Monnet also once said 'anything is possible in exceptional moments, as long as you are ready'. We have to trust Europe 芒聙聯 a Europe that will no longer seek shelter behind others, Europeans that are ready to stand up for ourselves and to defend ourselves. We have to do it together. So we count on you, the Members of the European Parliament, directly representing European citizens as co-legislator, we cannot do it without each other, of course. I hope for great cooperation in the next six months; let's build a stronger Europe together.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maro脜隆 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, first and foremost, Madam Prime Minister, dear Mette, I would like to thank you for putting forward Denmark's priorities for your Presidency of the Council. They are very much in line with the priorities of the Commission.

When I checked this morning, this is already your eighth presidency, so you are bringing a lot of experience, a lot of energy into the Chairing and management of the European Union in very challenging times. But we are absolutely convinced in the Commission that, as your previous seven presidencies have been very successful, that this will be another one.

I also would like to start by welcoming the Danish Presidency's focus on security and defence. The Commission has already started working on the roadmap for readiness for the European Council in October, and for this we will identify capability gaps and work with the Member States on common European projects for joint procurement.

Defence readiness is, however, not just about how much we spend, but also about how we spend it. And here the Danish Presidency will play a central role by ensuring the implementation of SAFE, our EUR脗聽150脗聽billion joint procurement investment programme. Before the end of the year, we also aim to finalise the participation of close allies in SAFE, starting with the UK and Canada, to ensure the necessary strategic depth.

In addition, we look to the Danish Presidency to swiftly finalise the negotiations with this House on the European defence industry programme to further strengthen Europe's defence industrial base. We also count on the Danish Presidency to progress with the Defence Readiness Omnibus, which will lift barriers and build an EU-wide market for defence.

Beyond this, it is important to reiterate that the security of Ukraine is also the security of the European Union, so we must maintain our financial support to Ukraine across all fronts: militarily, economically and politically. In that regard, we need to accelerate discussions for financial support to Ukraine in 2026 and beyond at EU but also at the G7 level. At the same time, we continue to put pressure on Russia and we will deliver the 18th package of sanctions.

Furthermore, we must accelerate Ukraine's path towards EU membership by opening all negotiating clusters in 2025, once Ukraine meets the relevant conditions. We also trust that the Danish Presidency will ensure the swift endorsement by the Council of the recently concluded agreement with Ukraine on the liberalisation of trade in agri-food products.

On migration, the implementation of the Pact on Migration and Asylum is a top priority for us, and we look to the Danish Presidency to take forward work on legislative files currently on the table: the return proposal, the safe third country concept and the safe countries of origin.

Turning to the second priority 芒聙聯 a competitive and green Europe. Here, simplification is crucial as we seek to reduce administrative burdens. We have already put forward five omnibus proposals that we expect to bring over EUR脗聽8脗聽billion in cost savings. We have tackled some of the most pressing issues holding us back in the areas of sustainability requirements, investment, agriculture, small mid-caps and defence. Quick progress is needed to maintain the momentum and ensure that our omnibus proposals benefit our businesses. We welcome the strong emphasis that the Presidency is placing on simplification, and the aim to complete negotiations with this House on the first omnibus.

It is also worth reiterating that we must continue to improve the single market 芒聙聯 our biggest asset. We count on the Danish Presidency to further the implementation of the single market strategy and conclude the customs union. I am very glad to highlight that this afternoon, together with Danish Presidency, will have a first trilogue on the deepest, most important customs reform since the customs union of the EU was established.

At a time when entrepreneurs struggle to access the risk capital they need, advancing on our savings and investment union is also a must. It will serve as a vital catalyst for enhancing our economic competitiveness, as highlighted in the Competitiveness Compass. We also have to deliver on the clean industrial deal, our flagship strategy to make decarbonisation a driver of industrial growth. Action plans have been put forward for the steel, chemicals and automotive sectors, setting out tailored actions for their specific needs.

Our new State aid framework will accelerate renewables, industrial decarbonisation and clean tech manufacturing. Our efforts must be underpinned by access to affordable clean energy, so we need to progress on the REPowerEU proposal, while a European grid package will be put forward later this year to speed up, permitting, and expand green capacity. And I know that my colleague, the Commissioner for energy Dan J脙赂rgensen, is working very hard on all these files.

We will also work closely with all of you on the amendment to the European Climate Law to ensure that we stay on track with our climate agenda and keep targets realistic and achievable.

Third, honourable Members, dear Prime Minister, looking further afield, we will continue to boost trade with our partner and further diversify our supply chains and essentially grow our network of free trade agreements, which is already the world's largest. We increasingly see others around the world turning to Europe as a reliable, predictable and trustworthy partner who remains committed to rules-based, free and fair trade. We aim to finalise the deal with Mercosur and Mexico, and we hope to conclude negotiations with Indonesia and India before the end of the year. We also have made progress with the United Arab Emirates, and we are moving forward with the Philippines, Thailand and Malaysia.

At the same time, we continue to engage closely with our US counterparts on the tariffs placed on European goods. I want to assure you that we are working flat out to secure fair and mutually beneficial negotiated solutions, but we need to be prepared for all outcomes and be ready to rebalance if necessary.

It will also be very important to maintain an open and frank dialogue with China, which there has been a debate on this morning, because it's clearly to keep the view that we need to resolve systemic trade irritants, to rebalance our trade and investment relations.

And closer to home, we need to follow up on a successful EU-UK summit in May and we also support concluding trilogues on the Generalised Scheme of Preferences Regulation as quickly as possible, hopefully again under the Danish Presidency.

Finally, to continue delivering on our priorities in the coming years, next week we will present our proposal for a strong new Multiannual Financial Framework. Our aim is to strike the right balance between stability and adaptability, ensuring the EU budget can support key priorities while retaining the flexibility to respond to future crises. We are committed to managing repayments under NextGenerationEU responsibly without compromising our long-term investment capacity. It is also important to continue the discussion on the new own resources, which are essential for securing a sustainable EU budget without overburdening national contributions.

We welcome the Danish Presidency's intention to prepare the first negotiating box by the end of the year. A balanced approach will be key to preserving the integrity of the package and enabling constructive negotiations with this House.

Honourable Members, dear Prime Minister, with this, I would like to conclude. I would like to thank the Prime Minister for setting up the priorities and, of course, we are very much looking forward to our debate.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jeroen Lenaers, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, colleagues, dear Prime Minister, welcome. You have big shoes to fill after the success of the Polish Presidency, but the EPP is ready to cooperate with your Presidency at a crucial time for our European Union.

While it's no secret that we do not belong to the same political family, I am happy to see a socialist leader here responsible, pragmatic and ready to engage. Perhaps in that sense, the Danish Presidency could be a good bridge-building Presidency.

Because for the EPP, this is not about politics. It is about content. We particularly support the focus on three essential pillars.

On defence, our message is clear: Europe is at a turning point. Russian aggression continues and the world is becoming more dangerous. We need to strengthen our defence industry, invest in capabilities and deepen cooperation with our allies, especially in support of Ukraine. The EPP stands firmly behind this, just like we stand firmly behind Denmark on the issue of Greenland.

On competitiveness, colleagues, let's be honest. While other global players race ahead, Europe risks suffocating under its own regulation. We need to make it easier to do business, to innovate, to grow, to compete, especially for our SMEs and family businesses. We need less ideology and more common sense, and this will also be our focus during your Presidency.

Finally, on migration, the EPP has long called for a fair, firm and effective system. We must tackle illegal migration, protect our external borders, combat trafficking networks, and ensure that those in genuine need are supported, while returning those who are not.

Prime Minister, your priorities are closely aligned with those from the European Commission. We need less moral posturing and more real solutions.

On this issue, you will find in the EPP a partner you can work with and rely on, and we rely on you as well to engage and convince your colleagues 芒聙聯 also in this European Parliament, also those from your own political family, because, as you might have noticed, that part of the Chamber forgot to applaud that specific section of your speech.

President, we stand ready to work with the Danish Presidency to make Europe stronger, safer and more competitive.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Iratxe Garc脙颅a P脙漏rez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora primera ministra Frederiksen, bienvenida al Parlamento Europeo. Su presencia aqu脙颅 no es solo la de una jefa de Gobierno; es la de una l脙颅der valiente, europe脙颅sta y comprometida con la justicia social, una l脙颅der que ha demostrado que se puede gobernar desde la firmeza, con convicci脙鲁n y responsabilidad.

Bajo el lema 脗芦Una Europa fuerte en un mundo cambiante脗禄, Dinamarca asume su octava Presidencia en un momento en el que la historia nos est脙隆 observando. Cambian los equilibrios globales, se intensifican las amenazas externas y regresan fantasmas internos que cre脙颅amos superados. Pero Europa s脙鲁lo tiene un camino posible: m脙隆s unidad, m脙隆s solidaridad y m脙隆s ambici脙鲁n democr脙隆tica.

Por eso, apoyamos que esta Presidencia priorice una Europa segura, competitiva y verde. Porque entendemos la competitividad no como una carrera de desigualdades, sino como la capacidad de construir econom脙颅as que innovan sin excluir, que lideran sin contaminar, que protegen el empleo sin sacrificar la justicia social. Y sabemos que no hay justicia social posible si no garantizamos un derecho tan b脙隆sico como el de la vivienda. Por eso tambi脙漏n creemos que debe ser una prioridad durante este tiempo.

Europa tambi脙漏n necesita un presupuesto a la altura de los retos. Queremos un marco financiero justo, con recursos propios y con un Fondo Social Europeo Plus m脙隆s fuerte, aut脙鲁nomo y centrado en las personas. Lo decimos alto y claro: no hay defensa europea ni autonom脙颅a estrat脙漏gica si no garantizamos la cohesi脙鲁n territorial y la cohesi脙鲁n social. Es posible abarcarlo todo, es posible hacerlo todo: avanzar en los nuevos desaf脙颅os defendiendo tambi脙漏n la Europa que conocemos hoy.

Se脙卤ora Frederiksen, acogemos con esperanza su impulso a la Directiva sobre la igualdad de trato. Aunque haya sido retirada, usted ha decidido no rendirse. Porque la igualdad no se archiva. Porque la lucha contra la discriminaci脙鲁n no se aplaza. Porque construir una Europa sin racismo, sin homofobia y sin machismo es una urgencia democr脙隆tica.

Tambi脙漏n es urgente levantar la voz fuera de nuestras fronteras. En Ucrania, con la confiscaci脙鲁n de los activos congelados rusos frente a la masacre de Putin y, en Palestina, con la suspensi脙鲁n del Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n con Israel frente al genocidio de Netanyahu.

Se脙卤ora Frederiksen, la historia de Dinamarca en Europa es la historia de un pa脙颅s que ha sabido defender sus convicciones con coraje. Un pa脙颅s que no ha necesitado levantar la voz para hacerse o脙颅r. Un pa脙颅s que ha comprendido que la soberan脙颅a no se pierde en nuestra Uni脙鲁n: se comparte, se multiplica, se transforma en fuerza colectiva.

Recordemos las palabras de Jens Otto Krag, el primer ministro dan脙漏s que firm脙鲁 el Tratado de Adhesi脙鲁n, en su discurso en Bruselas: 脗芦Mi deseo es que las Comunidades persigan sus pol脙颅ticas internas con un esp脙颅ritu progresista de conciencia social脗禄.

Ese deseo tambi脙漏n es el nuestro y, con su Presidencia, se脙卤ora Frederiksen, tendremos una oportunidad real de hacerlo posible.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anders Vistisen, for PfE-Gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Ministre! Hvis Ursula von der Leyen skulle f脙楼 lyst til at udpege sin dr脙赂mmestatsminister, s脙楼 kunne valget meget vel falde p脙楼 dig, Mette Frederiksen. En statsminister, der siger 脙漏t til sine v脙娄lgere og g脙赂r det stik modsatte, n脙楼r Bruxelles ringer. Du kaldte f脙娄lles EU-g脙娄ld for en r脙赂d linje. Alligevel s脙楼 stemte den danske regering for EU-l脙楼n p脙楼 750 milliarder euro, og nu er st脙赂tten der til endnu mere g脙娄ld. Du sagde: "Vi skal have et mindre EU-budget", men nu arbejder den danske formand for det st脙赂rste EU-budget i historien og en gigantisk ekstraregning til de danske skatteydere. Og oven i det hele st脙赂tter man nu en k脙娄mpe 脙赂studvidelse med ekspresfart, uden den n脙赂dvendige omtanke eller reformer. Det vil f脙楼 konsekvenser: l脙赂ndumping, velf脙娄rdsturisme og organiseret 脙赂stkriminalitet. Det er hverken ansvarligt eller nationalt, det er f脙赂deralt, og det er farligt.

Men du n脙赂jes jo ikke med at svigte politisk, du har ogs脙楼 svigtet demokratisk. Mens vi i disse dage behandler Ursula von der Leyens Pfizer-gate-skandale, s脙楼 fandt du jo opskriften p脙楼 at undg脙楼 ansvaret. Da din regering under coronakrisen ulovligt gav ordre til minkaflivning, blev grundloven brudt. Men i stedet for en retssag og en unders脙赂gelse, s脙楼 fik vi slettede SMS'er og en midterregering, der lukkede hele sagen ned. Det er imponerende! Du sletter og slipper. Ursula, hun n脙娄gter og bliver h脙娄ngt ud. S脙楼 ja, Mette Frederiksen, du er virkelig "European by heart". I hvert fald hvis det d脙娄kker over en ukritisk opbakning til et f脙赂deralt monster, som EU har udviklet sig til med din hj脙娄lp. Og det danske lederskab er ikke lederskab, men underkastelse, og derfor er Ursula von der Leyen en glad kvinde i dag, hvor Danmark overtager EU-formandskabet.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kristoffer Storm, for ECR-Gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru formand! K脙娄re Mette! Som dansker med respekt for retssamfundet, s脙楼 g脙赂r det mig faktisk lidt trist at sidde og se dig sidde her i dag og nyde rampelyset, mens du stadig n脙娄gter at st脙楼 til ansvar for dine handlinger hjemme i Danmark. For som statsminister har du uden lovgrundlag lukket et helt erhverv. Du har gjort hundredvis af mennesker arbejdsl脙赂se, og du har sendt en regning p脙楼 omkring 30 milliarder danske kroner ud til skatteborgerne. Og da sp脙赂rgsm脙楼lene de kom, ja s脙楼 gemte du dig bag Moderaterne og Venstre, og udnyttede jeres spinkle flertal til at undg脙楼 at skulle stilles for en domstol. Du sagde i din tale, at Europas byggesten er demokrati og frihed. Men du er den f脙赂rste statsminister i nyere dansk historie, der har begr脙娄nset borgernes ytringsfrihed. Det gjorde du, da du indf脙赂rte Koranloven. Da valgte du at begr脙娄nse ytringsfriheden for helt almindelige mennesker og underkaste dig imamerne og lade dem diktere, hvad vi m脙楼 sige, og hvordan vi m脙楼 ytre os. Det er ikke alene et angreb p脙楼 ytringsfriheden, det er ogs脙楼 en oph脙赂jelse af islam i det danske samfund.

N脙楼r jeg ser p脙楼 regeringens arbejdsprogram for de n脙娄ste 6 m脙楼neder, s脙楼 ser jeg et arbejdsprogram uden konkrete l脙赂sninger, og som i princippet liges脙楼 godt kunne v脙娄re skrevet af et hvilket som helst andet medlemsland her i EU. Det er overfladisk, og der kommer ingen konkrete l脙赂sninger. En af de ting du dog n脙娄vner, som jeg syntes er positivt, er migration, men som sagt f脙赂r: Ingen l脙赂sninger, kun tomme ord. S脙楼 Mette, jeg vil gerne hj脙娄lpe dig lidt. Lad os rejse flere hegn og mure langs EU's gr脙娄nser. Lad os vende b脙楼dene i Middelhavet. Lad os s脙娄tte et stop for alt spontant asyl. Lad os give medlemslandene retten til at indf脙赂re permanent gr脙娄nsekontrol. Og lad os lave en plan for remigration, og lad os tage et opg脙赂r, med de konventioner, som sp脙娄nder ben for, at vi kan udvise u脙赂nskede og kriminelle indvandrere. Hvis du mangler flere gode id脙漏er, Mette, s脙楼 kan du altid skrive til mig. Jeg lover dig, at jeg sletter ikke mine SMS'er. Afslutningsvis vil jeg sige, at jeg er ikke i tvivl om, at du anser i dag og de kommende 6 m脙楼neder for 脙漏n lang jobsamtale for din fremtidige karriere. Hvis du syntes, at jeg har v脙娄ret lidt for h脙楼rd ved dig, s脙楼 har jeg kun 脙漏n ting at sige: Lev med det!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Billy Kelleher, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, first of all, I want to wish you, Prime Minister, and the Danish Presidency success over the next six months.

Of course, you're taking over at a very strategic time in Europe's destiny in terms of the uncertainty of international challenges, geopolitical challenges and, of course, the continuous threat of security and defence from Russia in Ukraine and our eastern borders. So, I do want to wish you and your government well.

Of course, the key issues that our citizens face on a daily basis are the issues that you refer to: competitiveness, the Green Deal, energy costs and ensuring that we have sustainable policies around housing. All very key and important areas.

Renew, as you know, Prime Minister, visited Copenhagen recently, where we met with your government and engaged with them. Renew will always be a bridge芒聙聭builder. In fact, we will work as effectively as we can between the S&D and the EPP. At times, they do make it challenging, but we will continue to ensure that we have a pragmatic majority in this Parliament that can address the fundamental needs of the citizens.

Very often, Prime Minister, the difficulty is that we come to challenges with ideological baggage. What we need to do is look at things in a practical, clear sense.脗聽So, the Draghi report, for example, is a template that has been handed to us that we must address with urgency 芒聙聯 not just talk about the report itself, but actually implement what it says it must do in terms of competitiveness, the Green Deal, deepening the single market, addressing energy costs and ensuring that we remain competitive and start engaging internationally in multilateralism as well. These are the key fundamental issues that are facing citizens.

One issue, Prime Minister, where I think you might look at it is single European skies. We have a situation where, continually, industrial disputes bring aviation to a standstill across Europe. It is a matter that affects jobs, competitiveness, citizens on holidays and business. I believe it is one key area that, if it was addressed, would send out a strong signal that Europe works for citizens, it works for competitiveness. So, I just hope that you take that specific issue on in terms of the single European skies, along with, obviously, the larger challenges that face the European Union.

I wish you, your government and your people the best.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kira Marie Peter-Hansen, for Verts/ALE-Gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Tak for talen. Jeg har gl脙娄det mig til i dag, og det er jeg sikker p脙楼, at I ogs脙楼 har, for det danske formandskab st脙楼r over for nogle store udfordringer. I skal levere p脙楼 et sikkert, et konkurrencedygtigt og et gr脙赂nt Europa. Lad mig starte med at tage fat i det sidste, for jeg har nemlig store forventninger til Danmark. Is脙娄r fordi ministre jo gennem tiden, n脙楼r der har v脙娄ret et milj脙赂problem, de ikke selv vil l脙赂se, har kigget til EU. Det g脙娄lder for eksempel et forbud mod PFAS og evighedskemikalier, et stop for eksport af giftige pesticider og spr脙赂jtemidler oppe fra Cheminova og beskyttelse af vores havmilj脙赂. Nu har I muligheden for at g脙赂re ord til handling, og det haster nemlig, for vandet, vi drikker, bliver stadig mere og mere forurenet, vores evne til at f脙楼 b脙赂rn p脙楼virkes af fertilitetsskifte, og vores havmilj脙赂 ligger stend脙赂dt.

I SF og den gr脙赂nne gruppe, der er vi klar, og det h脙楼ber jeg ogs脙楼, at den danske regering er, for det m脙楼 efterh脙楼nden st脙楼 klart for enhver, at kloden, den br脙娄nder. S脙楼 sent som i sidste uge s脙楼 vi hedeb脙赂lger, der l脙娄gger gaderne 脙赂de, vi s脙楼 oversv脙赂mmelser, der sender mennesker fra hus og hjem, og vi s脙楼 en landbrugsproduktion i kn脙娄. Derfor er det ogs脙楼 helt afg脙赂rende, at Danmark bruger formandskabet til at komme videre i den gr脙赂nne omstilling og holde det gr脙赂nne h脙楼b i live. Og vi er heldigvis beviset p脙楼, at det godt kan lade sig g脙赂re 芒聙聯 at klima, sikkerhed, en st脙娄rk 脙赂konomi og et trygt velf脙娄rdssamfund kan g脙楼 h脙楼nd i h脙楼nd. Derfor er det ogs脙楼 s脙楼 vigtigt, at Danmark lander en st脙娄rk 2040 plan, der reelt reducerer CO2 med 90 procent, uden klimakreditter og uden fikse regnemetoder. Det er for mig et af de helt store succeskriterier for det danske formandskab.

Det n脙娄ste halve 脙楼r sidder Danmark i formandsstolen. Det forpligter. Det forpligter ikke kun over for EU, men ogs脙楼 over for en verden, der bliver mere og mere usikker. Jeg bliver stolt, n脙楼r Danmark g脙楼r forrest i den milit脙娄re st脙赂tte til Ukraine. Jeg bliver ogs脙楼 stolt, n脙楼r vi st脙楼r p脙楼 de iranske kvinders side, og n脙楼r vi forsvarer retten til at leve i et demokrati. Men desv脙娄rre har vi en situation i Gaza og p脙楼 Vestbredden, der er ude af kontrol, hvor civile og b脙赂rn sl脙楼s ihjel, hvor n脙赂dhj脙娄lp bruges som et v脙楼ben, og hvor familier stadig venter p脙楼 at f脙楼 deres k脙娄re hjem. Det n脙娄ste 脙楼r er det Danmark, der har bolden. Jeg h脙楼ber, I vil tage det ansvar p脙楼 jer.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Per Clausen, for The Left-Gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Det kunne v脙娄re fristende at holde en valgtale m脙楼lrettet mod danskerne, men jeg vil faktisk fokusere p脙楼 det program, som det danske formandskab har fremlagt. Der er nemlig rigeligt at kritisere: det er for lidt, det er for d脙楼rligt, og der er for meget copy/paste. Det synes jeg, er den bedste beskrivelse af prioriteringerne. Hvis man ser p脙楼 de prioriteringer, der er kommet frem, s脙楼 er der tale om en ukritisk Kommission, hvad den konservative EU-kommissionsformand allerede har sagt. S脙楼 jeg forst脙楼r i grunden godt EPP's begejstring. Men hvor er de reelle ambitioner for at tackle klimakrisen? Hvor er den m脙楼lrettede indsats mod den gr脙娄nseoverskridende sociale dumping, vi gang p脙楼 gang ser eksempler p脙楼? Hvor er indsatsen for de svageste og mest s脙楼rbare i Europa? Hvor er den reelle indsats mod PFAS og den kemi, der forurener b脙楼de mennesker, dyr og milj脙赂? Og m脙楼ske ikke mindst, hvorfor kan man ikke klart og utvetydigt sige fra over for Donald Trump i USA? Hvorfor skal vi fortsat v脙娄re afh脙娄ngige af USA? Og kan du svare mig p脙楼, hvorfor respekt for international ret ikke er i centrum for det danske EU-formandskab? For ja, EU skal st脙赂tte Ukraine, EU skal sige klart fra over for diktatoren Putin, s脙楼 langt er vi 100脗聽% enig. Men burde vi ikke g脙赂r det samme over for den krigsforbryder til Netanyahu, som regerer i Israel? Burde vi ikke g脙赂re det over for Saudi-Arabien, Qatar eller andre diktaturer, som undertrykker befolkningerne? Det er nu 芒聙聯 for at sige det p脙楼 godt nordjysk 芒聙聯 skuffende. Den gode nyhed er, at det stadig er muligt at rette op p脙楼 de manglende ambitioner. Den d脙楼rlige er, at jeg tvivler p脙楼, at du vil g脙赂re det, men hvis du vil, s脙楼 vil vi i Venstrefl脙赂jsgruppen gerne hj脙娄lpe dig. Og som du ved, Mette, s脙楼 kan du altid regne med mig og os, n脙楼r du g脙赂r det rigtige. Det er kun, n脙楼r du g脙赂r det forkerte, at det g脙楼r galt. S脙楼 kom nu ind i kampen! I Venstrefl脙赂jsgruppen mener vi, at b脙楼de Danmark og EU fortjener bedre end det her.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mary Khan, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Ein starkes Europa in einer sich wandelnden Welt. Unter diesem Motto 脙录bernimmt D脙陇nemark die Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft. Ein starkes Europa, das bedeutet f脙录r mich ein Europa der Vaterl脙陇nder, ein Europa, das auf Sicherheit, Souver脙陇nit脙陇t und Traditionen baut, nicht auf offene Grenzen, Fremdbestimmung oder Ideologie. Doch es ist kein Zufall, dass D脙陇nemark diesen Sonderweg in Sachen Migration gehen kann. Denn es ist nicht Teil des Gemeinsamen Europ脙陇ischen Asylsystems. Und genau deshalb hat D脙陇nemark Handlungsspielr脙陇ume, von denen wir in Deutschland nur tr脙陇umen k脙露nnen. W脙陇hrend Berlin an Br脙录sseler Quoten gebunden ist, kann Kopenhagen seine Grenzen sch脙录tzen, R脙录ckf脙录hrungen konsequent umsetzen und 脙录ber Asylzentren in Drittstaaten verhandeln. Deutschland hingegen zahlt Milliarden f脙录r ein System, das die Kontrolle l脙陇ngst verloren hat. 脙聹berforderte Kommunen, steigende Kriminalit脙陇t und Frust in der eigenen Bev脙露lkerung 芒聙聯 all das ist die Realit脙陇t offener Grenzen. Migration ist kein Schicksal, sie ist eine Frage des politischen Willens. Deshalb fordern wir auch von der AfD, aus dem gemeinsamen europ脙陇ischen Asylsystem auszutreten. Und vielleicht k脙露nnen Sie das in Ihrer Zeit auch Friedrich Merz ...

(Die Pr脙陇sidentin entzieht der Rednerin das Wort.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Niels Flemming Hansen (PPE). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Mange tak for talen, Mette. Normalt ser jeg mig selv som en glad mand, og i torsdags blev jeg endnu gladere. Markeringen af det danske formandskab var en folkefest ud over det s脙娄dvanlige. Der var fyldte gader, koncerter, debatter, diskussioner og sammenhold 芒聙聯 dansk politik n脙楼r det er bedst. Du, Mette, holdt selv en fremragende tale, hvor vi blev mindet om et m脙赂rkt kapitel i verdenshistorien, men ogs脙楼 om, hvordan Aarhus' borgere gik pragmatisk til v脙娄rks og samlede jern fra hele landet for at bygge det r脙楼dhus, der stadig st脙楼r midt i byen. Den handlekraft har vi virkelig brug for igen. Denne gang g脙娄lder det forsvaret af Europa, den gr脙赂nne omstilling og 脙赂konomisk v脙娄kst. Jeg h脙楼ber, at den handlekraft vil pr脙娄ge det danske formandskab. Men for at det kan ske, s脙楼 kr脙娄ver det mere end godt samarbejde i R脙楼det. Det kr脙娄ver s脙娄rligt et frugtbart partnerskab her i Parlamentet. Is脙娄r mellem din gruppe og den gruppe, jeg repr脙娄senterer. Som en klog mand engang sagde, s脙楼 er ideologi noget bras, og vi er n脙赂dt til at smitte Europa med den danske model. Modellen om, at ligegyldigt hvem der har magten i landet, s脙楼 fors脙赂ger vi at lave l脙赂sninger sammen. Ingen f脙楼r alt, men alle f脙楼r noget 芒聙聯 den konservative model. Tiden er ikke til politiske drillerier, s脙楼 lad os sammen bygge bro over forskelle og sikre, at Europa kommer styrket ud af det danske formandskab. Lad os vise verden, at n脙楼r det g脙娄lder Europas fremtid, s脙楼 st脙楼r vi skulder ved skulder.

(Taleren accepterede at besvare et bl脙楼t kort-sp脙赂rgsm脙楼l)

脗聽脗聽Petras Gra脜戮ulis (ESN), pak脛聴lus m脛聴lyn脛聟j脛聟 kortel脛聶 pateiktas klausimas. 芒聙聯 Nor脛聴jau J脜芦s脜鲁 paklausti, ar ne per daug 脛炉vairiausi脜鲁 institucij脜鲁 Europos S脛聟jungoje? 脗聽脗聽Niels Flemming Hansen (PPE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 I have to say that I think that the gentleman speaking to me is completely wrong. Of course, it is important with the institution of a presidency that goes divided from country to country.

From the Danish Presidency, our biggest effort here is to put the European alliance on the right track, so that we keep moving forward and we need presidency and we need Danish leadership to do that.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christel Schaldemose (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! K脙娄re Mette. Tak for din tale. Et st脙娄rkt Europa i en usikker verden. Det er mottoet for det danske formandskab. Det er godt, det skal ogs脙楼 blive vores virkelighed. Og til det har jeg tre bem脙娄rkninger. For det f脙赂rste: Ukrainerne st脙楼r ved frontlinjen. De k脙娄mper ogs脙楼 for vores frihed, demokrati og vores v脙娄rdier. Derfor er det utrolig vigtigt, som du ogs脙楼 sagde, at vi tager kampen op og hj脙娄lper ukrainerne i den kamp. Lad os v脙娄re 脙娄rlige. De er med til at forsvare Europa, og vi kan ikke tale om solidaritet med det ukrainske folk om mandagen og s脙楼 betale til Putins krigskasse om tirsdagen. Vores st脙赂tte m脙楼 ikke vakle. Ikke i viljen, ikke i v脙楼ben og ikke i vedholdenhed. S脙楼 tak til det danske formandskab. For det andet: Du taler om konkurrencedygtighed og gr脙赂n omstilling. Det er nemlig fuldst脙娄ndig rigtigt. Gr脙赂n omstilling st脙楼r ikke i mods脙娄tning til konkurrenceevne. De er hinandens foruds脙娄tninger, de er ikke hinandens mods脙娄tninger. Lad os k脙娄mpe for, ogs脙楼 at f脙楼 sat gang i den gr脙赂nne omstilling. Det er en af m脙楼derne, men selvf脙赂lgelig skal vi ogs脙楼 forenkle. S脙楼 tak for at have begge dele med. Og endelig til sidst: Kampen for Europas fremtid f脙赂res ikke kun p脙楼 slagmarken og i produktionshallerne. Det er ogs脙楼 i vores b脙赂rns v脙娄relser, bag sk脙娄rme og ogs脙楼 p脙楼 m脙楼der, vi voksne ikke forst脙楼r. Jeg h脙楼ber ogs脙楼, at det danske formandskab, vil v脙娄re med til at k脙娄mpe for, at b脙赂rn ikke bliver overladt til algoritmer, misinformation og misbrug online. Tak for ogs脙楼 at tage det med, selv om det ikke blev n脙娄vnt i dag. Vi skal k脙娄mpe sammen for Europas fremtid, og Danmark g脙楼r i forvejen med et godt eksempel.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jean-Paul Garraud (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, le Danemark assure 脙聽 pr脙漏sent la pr脙漏sidence du Conseil de l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne. Si votre gouvernement reste marqu脙漏, Madame le Premier ministre, par des politiques de gauche, il faut reconna脙庐tre qu芒聙聶en mati脙篓re migratoire vous avez infl脙漏chi votre position dans le bon sens. Mais rendons justice 脙聽 ceux qui ont pr脙漏par脙漏 cette 脙漏volution. Ce sont nos alli脙漏s du Parti populaire danois qui, les premiers, ont d脙漏nonc脙漏 les effets d脙漏vastateurs de l芒聙聶immigration massive. Leur combat a fait bouger les lignes.

Vous avez d脙漏sormais reconnu ce que les peuples r脙漏p脙篓tent depuis des ann脙漏es: sans ma脙庐trise de l芒聙聶immigration, il n芒聙聶y a ni s脙漏curit脙漏, ni coh脙漏sion, ni justice sociale, et les co脙禄ts li脙漏s 脙聽 l芒聙聶immigration de masse sont consid脙漏rables pour la collectivit脙漏. 脙聙 pr脙漏sent que vous pr脙漏sidez le Conseil, il vous revient d芒聙聶impulser cette m脙陋me fermet脙漏 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏chelle europ脙漏enne, car les peuples attendent des actes: r脙漏duction des flux, durcissement du droit d芒聙聶asile, fin des abus et retour 脙聽 la souverainet脙漏. Nos pays doivent garder la ma脙庐trise de leur avenir et de leur destin: cela commence par la politique migratoire. Vous le faites au Danemark. 脙聙 pr脙漏sent, il faut aussi le faire pour l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicola Procaccini (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, Primo ministro, onorevoli colleghi, il nostro compito, oggi, 脙篓 valutare il vostro programma alla luce delle nostre diverse sensibilit脙聽 politiche.

Ovviamente, apprezzo i Suoi propositi sulla lotta all'immigrazione illegale, sulla protezione delle frontiere esterne e sulla necessit脙聽 di agevolare i rimpatri di chi non ha diritto all'asilo. Lei non sa da quanto tempo noi conservatori aspettavamo queste parole da una leader socialista. In tutti questi anni, solo per aver usato le Sue stesse parole, siamo stati insultati, dileggiati e combattuti dalle sinistre in questo Parlamento. Quindi, finalmente.

Purtroppo, in altre nazioni europee i socialisti continuano a perseguire una folle dottrina immigrazionista no borders. Penso alla Spagna o all'Italia, dove gli esponenti del Partito democratico rischierebbero di svenire sentendo un Suo脗聽ministro definire "incredibilmente positivo" il protocollo Italia芒聙聭Albania.

Purtroppo, le buone notizie nel Suo programma per noi finiscono qui. Le cattive notizie iniziano con la volont脙聽 di modificare al rialzo la tassazione europea sull'energia, quindi, tasse pi脙鹿 alte per famiglie e imprese nel nome della transizione green.

Purtroppo, qui si rivela la matrice di sinistra del Suo governo, che interpreta l'ambientalismo come un surrogato del socialismo, che impone, tassa, vieta, obbliga e comprime la neutralit脙聽 tecnologica e la libert脙聽 delle persone.

Tutto questo senza mai citare neppure una volta la parola "nucleare", rivelando l'avversione per questa fonte energetica da parte del Commissario danese per l'Energia.

Primo ministro Frederiksen, converr脙聽 con me che 脙篓 il bello della democrazia e della politica confrontarsi tra opinioni diverse. Noi lo faremo come sempre: cortesi nei toni, ma molto decisi nel rispetto dei nostri valori.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Morten L脙赂kkegaard (Renew). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! K脙娄re Mette! Forleden indledte Danmark sit formandskab for EU, og vi havde en fest i Aarhus. En stor fest, hvor vi som danskere hyldede det europ脙娄iske lederskab midt i en brydningstid med kommissionsformand von der Leyen i spidsen. Det var dejligt at se, at danskerne bakkede s脙楼 meget op, som de gjorde, og kontrasten til i g脙楼r her i salen var til at f脙楼 脙赂je p脙楼. Her handlede det netop om mistillid, politiske spilf脙娄gterier og fors脙赂g p脙楼 at 脙赂del脙娄gge det europ脙娄iske sammenhold, midt i en global krisetid. Misforst脙楼 mig ikke, selvf脙赂lgelig skal Kommissionens g脙赂ren og laden kunne t脙楼le kritik og transparens. Vi i Renew er ogs脙楼 kritiske p脙楼 den m脙楼de. Men for nu at sige det lige ud: Timingen stank. Mens vi st脙楼r og sk脙娄ndes her, forts脙娄tter krigen i Ukraine, USA er ikke l脙娄ngere en sikker allieret, og risikoen for handelskonflikter og protektionisme stiger for hver eneste dag. Det kalder p脙楼 lederskab og p脙楼 et formandskab, der st脙楼r fast, n脙楼r det bl脙娄ser. Jeg har personligt ikke nogen som helst tvivl om, at det kan det danske formandskab leve op til, og vi i Renew er i hvert fald klar til at yde vores. Det skal v脙娄re budskabet herfra. Specielt ogs脙楼 p脙楼 den dagsorden, som handler om at g脙赂re hverdagen lettere, gribe ind, der hvor frugterne h脙娄nger lavt, forenkle reglerne, fjerne bureaukrati, skabe bedre vilk脙楼r for dem, der hver dag f脙楼r Europa til at h脙娄nge sammen, og det er jo som bekendt borgere og virksomheder. Der kan vi noget i Danmark. Og af samme grund roste von der Leyen ogs脙楼 Danmark for sin effektivitet. Jeg er sikker p脙楼, at vi kan leve op til det. Jeg er i hvert fald hundrede procent sikker p脙楼, at det er den tilgang, Europa har brug for lige nu.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasmus Andresen (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! K脙娄re statsminister! I en tid, hvor had og nationalisme for alvor har indtaget scenen, er det vigtigt at st脙楼 sammen. Men som mindretalsdansker og borger i det dansk-tyske gr脙娄nseland synes jeg, at EU og skiftende regeringer p脙楼 begge sider af gr脙娄nsen har svigtet os. Permanent og ulovlig gr脙娄nsekontrol har gjort hverdagen for mange tusinder af mennesker i vores region langt mere besv脙娄rlig. Gr脙娄nsependlere og skoleelever, som krydser gr脙娄nsen hver eneste dag, turister og almindelige borgere, som har familie, venner eller en l脙娄ge p脙楼 den anden side af gr脙娄nsen. Men det er ikke det eneste. Siden permanent gr脙娄nsekontrol blevet genindf脙赂rt i 2016, er det gr脙娄nseoverskridende samarbejde blevet indefrosset, og vi frygter, at der i det nye EU-budget ikke ville v脙娄re plads til gr脙娄nseoverskridende projekter og samarbejde. Det er, som om vi ikke bliver h脙赂rt hverken i Bruxelles, Berlin eller K脙赂benhavn. De europ脙娄iske mindretal har gang p脙楼 gang opfordret Kommissionen til at s脙娄tte mindretalsrettigheder p脙楼 dagsordenen, og der er desv脙娄rre ikke blevet lyttet. S脙楼 jeg har et simpelt budskab til dig og det danske formandskab: Glem os ikke!

(Taleren accepterede at besvare et bl脙楼t kort-sp脙赂rgsm脙楼l)

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Deputado Rasmus Andresen, queria perguntar-lhe se, na perspetiva dos Verdes, o programa da Presid脙陋ncia que foi hoje apresentado corresponde efetivamente 脙聽 alternativa de que os povos precisam.

脗聽脗聽Rasmus Andresen (Verts/ALE), bl脙楼t-kort-svar. 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Tak for sp脙赂rgsm脙楼let, og jeg vil da bare referere til, hvad vores n脙娄stformand for vores gruppe lige f脙赂r sagde i sin indledende tale for vores gruppe. Jeg tror, det bliver vigtigt i de n脙娄ste par m脙楼neder at f脙楼 et EU-budget p脙楼 plads, hvor vi b脙楼de har plads til den gr脙赂nne omstilling og plads til sociale sp脙赂rgsm脙楼l, men selvf脙赂lgelig ogs脙楼 med en styrket indsats for sikkerhedspolitik, som fylder mere, end den har gjort tidligere for EU. Og dette er i hvert fald den position, vi er meget enige om i den gr脙赂nne gruppe. Og s脙楼 syntes jeg til geng脙娄ld ogs脙楼, vi kan g脙赂re noget mere for at beskatte f.eks. de store techvirksomheder. Og jeg h脙楼ber p脙楼, at Kommissionen kommer med nogle konkrete forslag i n脙娄ste uge.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Giuseppe Antoci (The Left). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, accogliamo con interesse il programma della Presidenza danese.

Certo, non ne condividiamo l'accelerazione verso il riarmo, mentre condividiamo l'urgenza di rafforzare la fiducia nello Stato di diritto.

Ma non pu脙虏 esserci una vera Europa dei diritti senza una vera Europa della legalit脙聽. Per questo, 脙篓 inaccettabile che, dopo mesi di lavoro e negoziato sulla nuova direttiva anticorruzione, ci troviamo oggi con un dossier bloccato da un'esigua minoranza di Stati membri. La stragrande maggioranza dei paesi dell'Unione prevede gi脙聽 nei propri codici penali il reato di abuso d'ufficio.

Abbiamo perso tempo prezioso. Qui c'脙篓 la nostra relatrice che si 脙篓 impegnata tantissimo e la corruzione non aspetta nessuno. Nel frattempo, risorse pubbliche, anche europee, continuano a essere sottratte nell'impunit脙聽.

Alla Presidenza danese chiediamo coraggio. Serve una forte leadership e determinazione per superare i veti isolati, molto isolati e dotare l'Unione, finalmente, di un'arma efficace e condivisa contro la corruzione.

Il tema non 脙篓 solo politico, ma prioritariamente morale.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Jongen (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Sehr geehrte Ministerpr脙陇sidentin Frederiksen! Viele B脙录rger in Deutschland schauen mit Neid, aber auch mit Hoffnung nach D脙陇nemark, denn Sie haben geschafft, wozu unsere Regierung bisher nicht f脙陇hig war. Mit Ihrer konsequenten Migrationspolitik sch脙录tzen Sie die nationale Identit脙陇t und den sozialen Zusammenhalt Ihres Landes. Dieses Modell muss Vorbild f脙录r Deutschland und ganz Europa werden, um unsere Kultur und unsere Sicherheit zu bewahren脗聽芒聙聯 aller Respekt unserer ESN-Fraktion daf脙录r.

Doch wir h脙露ren auch Beunruhigendes. D脙陇nemarks Pl脙陇ne zur EU芒聙聭weiten Chatkontrolle und Bek脙陇mpfung sogenannter Falschinformationen sind ein Angriff auf die b脙录rgerlichen Freiheiten. Ihr d脙陇nisches Islamgesetz war eine Unterwerfung, und 脙聹berwachung privater Chats und Zensur unliebsamer Meinungen bedrohen die Demokratie in ihrem Kern. Kinderschutz ja, aber unter diesem Deckmantel d脙录rfen unsere Grundrechte nicht ausgehebelt werden.

Sorgen Sie bitte daf脙录r, dass neben der Sicherheit auch die Freiheit ein Leitstern Ihrer Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft wird. Dann w脙录nschen wir Ihnen auch viel Erfolg daf脙录r.

脗聽

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fernand Kartheiser (NI). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Premi脙篓re ministre, l芒聙聶administration Trump, le secr脙漏taire d芒聙聶脙聣tat Rubio, le g脙漏n脙漏ral脗聽Kellogg d脙漏clarent ouvertement que la guerre en Ukraine est une guerre par procuration, une guerre de coalition men脙漏e par les 脙聣tats-Unis et l芒聙聶OTAN contre la Russie. Gr脙垄ce au New York Times,脗聽nous savons qu芒聙聶elle est command脙漏e depuis Wiesbaden. En m脙陋me temps, le pr脙漏sident Trump nous dit que cette guerre est celle du pr脙漏sident Biden et que, si lui avait 脙漏t脙漏 pr脙漏sident, elle n芒聙聶aurait jamais commenc脙漏.

Ainsi, les Am脙漏ricains contredisent aujourd芒聙聶hui la propagande, qui est pourtant toujours la n脙麓tre, d芒聙聶une guerre d芒聙聶agression unilat脙漏ralement russe. Ils n芒聙聶admettent plus cette terminologie dans leurs d脙漏clarations officielles. Tirons-en les cons脙漏quences. Soyons aussi honn脙陋tes que les Am脙漏ricains: mettons fin 脙聽 cette guerre! Changeons notre politique 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏gard de la Russie et aidons les Ukrainiens 脙聽 se reconstruire politiquement et 脙漏conomiquement! Nous esp脙漏rons que la pr脙漏sidence danoise agira dans ce sens.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Angelika Niebler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Vizepr脙陇sident, Herr Kommissar, Frau Ministerpr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen, liebe Kollegen! Worauf kommt es jetzt an? Europa muss f脙录r die Sicherheit seiner B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger sorgen. Wir m脙录ssen unsere Verteidigungsf脙陇higkeit sicherstellen. Wir m脙录ssen die Ukraine weiter verteidigen. Wir m脙录ssen gegen illegale Migration vorgehen, und unsere Unternehmen m脙录ssen entlastet werden. Wir m脙录ssen B脙录rokratie abbauen, unseren europ脙陇ischen Standort st脙陇rken, uns gute Rahmenbedingungen schaffen, damit die Arbeitspl脙陇tze bei uns hier in der Europ脙陇ischen Union bleiben. Nat脙录rlich m脙录ssen Klimaschutz und Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit Hand in Hand gehen. Um all das will sich in den n脙陇chsten sechs Monaten die d脙陇nische Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft k脙录mmern. Verehrte Frau Ministerpr脙陇sidentin, Ihnen und Ihrem ganzen Team w脙录nsche ich gutes Gelingen, w脙录nsche ich eine gl脙录ckliche Hand bei Ihrer Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft und kann auch 芒聙聯 wie meine Kollegen aus der Fraktion 芒聙聯 nur gute Zusammenarbeit mit der EVP anbieten.

Gerne w脙录rde ich ein Thema noch einmal ansprechen, das Sie auch priorit脙陇r angehen wollen, das ist das Thema Entlastung, B脙录rokratieabbau. Ich w脙录nsche mir sehr, dass der erste Omnibus, das erste gro脙聼e Entlastungspaket noch unter Ihrer Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft unter Dach und Fach kommen kann. Wir brauchen eine massive Entlastung unserer mittelst脙陇ndischen Betriebe. Der erste Omnibus zur Nachhaltigkeit ist ja noch unter der polnischen Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft weitestgehend beraten worden. Also viel Gl脙录ck, und ich hoffe sehr, dass es Ihnen gelingt, wirklich das zuzumachen. Das ist das Signal, was gerade unsere Betriebe brauchen, dass wir es ernst meinen mit dem B脙录rokratieabbau.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alex Agius Saliba (S&D). 芒聙聯 Sinjura President, Prim Ministru, ma setax ikun hemm mument a脛搂jar sabiex id-Danimarka, wie脛搂ed mill-aktar pajji脜录i di脛隆italizzati, tie脛搂u f'idejha l-presidenza tal-Unjoni Ewropea. Id-di脜录gwid dwar Greenland wera li d-Danimarka, li pajji脜录hom u l-Unjoni Ewropea huma wisq dipendenti fuq it-teknolo脛隆iji Amerikani. Huwa l-mument f'waqtu li nie脛搂du passi konkreti u nsa脛搂脛搂u s-sovranit脙聽 tag脛搂na fejn jid脛搂lu t-teknolo脛隆iji. Il-kompetittivit脙聽 fil-qasam di脛隆itali tirrikjedi li l-Ewropa tibni l-kapa脛聥it脙聽 tag脛搂ha f'teknolo脛隆iji di脛隆itali ewlenin. L-innovazzjoni tinkiseb b'investiment li jsostni soluzzjonijiet di脛隆itali alternattivi. Irridu no脛搂olqu alternattivi Ewropej li jkissru l-mudelli finanzjarji ineti脛聥i ta'脗聽data harvesting u profiling. Huwa biss permezz ta' dawn l-alternattivi li nistg脛搂u neg脛搂lbu l-isfidi li nipprote脛隆u 脜录-脜录g脛搂a脜录ag脛搂 minn kontenut abbu脜录iv, inwaqqfu l-algoritmi vizzju脜录i u nillimitaw u脜录u e脛聥脛聥essiv ta' a脛隆脛隆e脛隆脛隆i di脛隆itali. Naqbel li b'mod ur脛隆enti je脛搂tie脛隆 li jkollna strate脛隆ija g脛搂al start-ups, scaleups u infrastrutturi tat-teknolo脛隆ija sabiex insa脛搂脛搂u l-ekosistemi teknolo脛隆i脛聥i tag脛搂na. Jiena kunfidenti li d-Dani脜录i g脛搂andhom il-konoxxenza sabiex nag脛搂mlu l-progress fl-a脛隆enda di脛隆itali tag脛搂na.

脗聽脗聽Ern脜聭 Schaller-Baross (PfE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! Tisztelt Minisztereln脙露k Asszony! Tisztelt Miniszter Asszony! A soros eln脙露ks脙漏get bet脙露lt脜聭 tag脙隆llam felel脜聭ss脙漏ge 脙鲁ri脙隆si. Els脜聭dleges feladata, hogy a tag脙隆llamok k脙露z脙露tti koordin脙隆ci脙鲁t seg脙颅tse akkor is, ha szocialista korm脙隆nyokr脙鲁l besz脙漏l脙录nk. M脙隆r a d脙隆n eln脙露ks脙漏g kezdet脙漏n politikai alap脙潞 脙隆ll脙隆sfoglal脙隆sok sz脙录lettek az 脙聳n脙露k r脙漏sz脙漏r脜聭l, melyek k脙录l脙露n脙露sen haz脙隆mat, Magyarorsz脙隆got fenyegetik. Nem 脙漏rtem. Eur脙鲁p脙隆nak most b脙漏k脙漏re, val脙鲁di versenyk脙漏pess脙漏gre lenne sz脙录ks脙漏ge. 脙聳n脙露k egy h脙隆bor脙潞ban 脙隆ll脙鲁 orsz脙隆got k脙颅v脙隆nnak gyors脙颅tva felvenni az uni脙鲁ba. A magyar emberek erre egy脙漏rtelm脜卤en nemet mondtak.

脙聳n脙露k ezzel a l脙漏p脙漏ssel az eur脙鲁pai biztons脙隆got, gazdas脙隆got 脙漏s agr脙隆riumot d脙露nten脙漏k romba, mik脙露zben Magyarorsz脙隆g szabadon v脙隆lasztott korm脙隆ny脙隆t 脙漏s v脙隆laszt脙鲁it zsarolj脙隆k. A magyarok 2015-ben m脙隆r egyszer nemet mondtak a fenyeget脙漏s脙录kre, az ok脙颅t脙隆sukra, nemet mondtak az illeg脙隆lis migr脙隆ci脙鲁ra, az integr脙隆ci脙鲁s neh脙漏zs脙漏gekre, az import脙隆lt terrorizmusra 脙漏s antiszemitizmusra, a t脙露bbs脙漏gi nyelv elsorvad脙隆s脙隆ra iskol脙隆inkban, int脙漏zm脙漏nyeinkben. Ez lehet a br脙录sszeli 脙潞t, lehet az 脙聳n脙露k 脙潞tja, de mi nem k脙漏r脙录nk bel脜聭le, ahogy most sem k脙漏r脙录nk az ok脙颅t脙隆sb脙鲁l, a h脙隆bor脙潞s usz脙颅t脙隆sb脙鲁l 脙漏s az eur脙鲁pai gazdas脙隆g romba d脙露nt脙漏s脙漏b脜聭l.

K脙漏rem a d脙隆n eln脙露ks脙漏get, tartsa tiszteletben honfit脙隆rsaim, a magyar emberek demokratikus d脙露nt脙漏s脙漏t.

脗聽脗聽Charlie Weimers (ECR). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! 脙聞rade statsminister Frederiksen! I m脙楼nga 脙楼r har Danmark varit en f脙露rebild f脙露r oss svenskar. Er pragmatism, er stolthet 脙露ver nationen och er vilja att st脙楼 upp f脙露r danska intressen inspirerade. Ni sa nej till massinvandring l脙楼ngt innan alla andra gjorde det. Ni kr脙陇vde en nykter EU-budget. Det var bra.

Men nu ser vi en annan bild. Ni omfamnar en st脙露rre EU-budget. Ni tar den enkla v脙陇gen. Att spendera andras pengar 脙陇r nog det enklaste som finns. Verkligt ledarskap handlar om att prioritera och g脙露ra upp med Bryssels ineffektivitet.

Fru statsminister! For 6 脙楼r siden kaldte De EU-budgettet fuldst脙娄ndig gak. Helt rigtigt. Omtrent som tidligere bruger jeg udtrykket kompromisvillig som en svensker. Landet, der altid stemmer ja, aldrig nedl脙娄gger veto 芒聙聯 Europas dommere. Kompromisvillig som en svensker, ville De virkelig ogs脙楼 huskes for det, fru statsminister?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Stine Bosse (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Prime Minister, dear Commissioners, for the next half year, Denmark bears a great responsibility, and what an important time to do so. The challenges before us are numerous, urgent and complex.

Europe is facing a busy agenda, from ensuring the security and defence of our borders to addressing the multifaceted threats we confront. We in the north and east must support our southern neighbours along the Mediterranean in strengthening our outer borders and fight illegal migration, while Europeans in the south and west must recognise the urgency of protecting our eastern borders against the rising threats from Putin's Russia.

At the core, we must defend our fundamental values, enabling our businesses, big and small, to thrive and grow, transforming our single market into a truly united one.

Simultaneously, we must simplify our processes without compromising on vital priorities: food security, environmental health, workplace safety and combating the devastating climate crisis, which is more urgent than ever. In other words, we need to work hard and work together. But I truly believe that, through dedication and determination, we can make a real difference for ourselves and for future generations.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinier Van Lanschot (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Prime Minister, Denmark has great stories to tell. It gave Ukraine all its artillery so they could fight back. It launched the Danish model for defence investments to help Ukraine win. Now Denmark is targeting Orb脙隆n's veto so Ukraine can take steps towards its European future.

Denmark holds the rotating presidency for the next six months, and your Minister of European Affairs has said she wants to take away Orb脙隆n's veto and voting rights. A unique opportunity to blast through Europe's paralysis with a little Danish dynamite.

It's time to do what millions of Hungarians, tens of millions of Ukrainians and hundreds of millions of Europeans have asked for: stop this wannabe dictator. The least corrupt country on the planet going after the most corrupt leader in Europe. Isn't that a story we would all like to hear?

脗聽脗聽Arno Bausemer (ESN). 芒聙聯 Sehr geehrte Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Frau Ministerpr脙陇sidentin. Ein starkes Europa in einer Welt im Wandel脗聽芒聙聯 so lautet also das Motto der d脙陇nischen Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft. Ein starkes Europa w脙陇re ein Europa mit sicheren Au脙聼engrenzen und weiteren sichtbaren Ma脙聼nahmen gegen illegale Migration. Hier haben die Sozialdemokraten in D脙陇nemark bereits einige positive Akzente gesetzt, deshalb w脙陇re es zu hoffen, dass es hier endlich positive Impulse zur Remigration geben wird. Die anhaltende Fl脙录chtlingsbegeisterung, vor allem bei deutschen Sozialdemokraten und Gr脙录nen, zeigt aber, dass hier wenig Grund zur Hoffnung besteht.

Verehrte Frau Ministerpr脙陇sidentin, lassen Sie sich bei der Migrationspolitik nicht bremsen. Zeigen Sie bei St脙露rfeuern von au脙聼erhalb dieselbe Entschlossenheit wie der Trainer Ihrer so erfolgreichen Handballnationalmannschaft! Als im Januar ein Klimaaktivist ein Spiel von D脙陇nemark bei der Weltmeisterschaft mit seinem Protest als B脙录hne missbrauchen wollte, da bef脙露rderte Trainer Nikolaj Jacobsen diesen seltsamen 脙聳kofaschisten kurzerhand selbst vom Spielfeld. Er wollte, dass es ohne St脙露rungen erfolgreich weitergeht.

Wie w脙陇re es denn ersatzweise mit diesem Motto f脙录r die d脙陇nische Pr脙陇sidentschaft? Ein erfolgreiches Europa ohne St脙露rungen von au脙聼erhalb.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Siegfried Mure脜聼an (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Prime Minister, welcome back to the European Parliament. Denmark is taking over the Council Presidency at a very important moment. Setting the right priorities for the Union for the next six months is essential because, Prime Minister, your success will be our success.

Truth be told, we are seeing autocrats inside the European Union and outside the European Union cooperating closely, clearly wanting to weaken our democracy, weaken our security, weaken our prosperity.

Let me say clearly: we will only be able to defend our democracy and our prosperity if we fight for it. This is why setting security and defence on the one side, and competitiveness, prosperity, defending our way of life, our high social standards in Europe, are essential priorities for the next six months. They should be priorities of the Danish Presidency and they are our priorities for the next years.

We should also put our money where our mouth is. As the Prime Minister has said, as the Vice-President of the Commission has said, the European Commission will make their proposal on the multiannual financial framework of the Union on 16脗聽July.

We should work closely together to make sure that that will be a budget that will be adopted in time, that will give certainty to beneficiaries of EU funds, to researchers, to farmers, to the regions, to students.

It should be a budget that makes Europe safer and more competitive. It should be a budget that prioritises and doesn't neglect traditional priorities. Farmers are important, regions are important and it should be a European budget, not a sum of 27 different national interests. For this, the European Parliament will fight together.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gabriele Bischoff (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Premierministerin! In der Tat, ein entscheidender Zeitpunkt Europas, wo die d脙陇nische Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft 脙录bernimmt. In einer Zeit, wo geopolitische Unsicherheiten wachsen, aber auch in unserer Gesellschaft sich immer mehr Menschen abgeh脙陇ngt f脙录hlen脗聽芒聙聯 und gleichzeitig eben nicht sicher genug. Deswegen geh脙露rt zur Sicherheit f脙录r uns essenziell die soziale Sicherheit. Und ich m脙露chte sagen, heute haben wir hier viele Beispiele geh脙露rt, wo alle gerne d脙陇nisch werden m脙露chten.

Ich m脙露chte ein anderes Beispiel nennen, wo D脙陇nemark f脙录r uns ein Rollenmodell ist脗聽芒聙聯 und das ist die 脙露ffentliche Auftragsvergabe. Sie stellen sicher, dass es nicht das billigste Angebot ist, sondern tats脙陇chlich, dass da, wo gute Arbeit geschaffen wird durch 脙露ffentliche Auftr脙陇ge脗聽芒聙聯 und hier wollen wir von Ihnen lernen, wir machen gerade einen Initiativbericht, denn wir wollen gute Arbeit und auch faire und freie Mobilit脙陇t脗聽芒聙聯 hier ist D脙陇nemark ein Rollenmodell.

Wir brauchen digitale Methoden hier, um sicherzustellen, dass wir wirklich auch eine faire Mobilit脙陇t in Zukunft haben und ein Punkt der (unverst脙陇ndliche Worte) Erweiterung und Reform der EU zusammen zu denken.

脗聽脗聽P脙隆l Szekeres (PfE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! Tisztelt Minisztereln脙露k Asszony! A d脙隆n eln脙露ks脙漏g nagyon sokat besz脙漏l a z脙露ld 脙隆t脙隆ll脙隆sr脙鲁l, de a program szinte teljesen mell脜聭zi a t脙隆rsadalmi szempontokat. Hol vannak bel脙录l azok az int脙漏zked脙漏sek, amelyek megv脙漏den脙漏k az eur脙鲁pai csal脙隆dokat az energia脙隆rak dr脙隆mai emelked脙漏s脙漏t脜聭l? A z脙露ld 脙隆tmenet hossz脙潞 t脙隆v脙潞 c脙漏ljai sz脙漏pen hangzanak, de addig is emberek milli脙鲁inak kell kifizethetetlen sz脙隆ml脙隆kkal szemben脙漏zni脙录k. A statisztikai adatok azt mutatj脙隆k, hogy az uni脙鲁s polg脙隆rok egy脙露t脙露d脙漏nek gondot okozott a t脙漏li f脜卤t脙漏ssz脙隆ml脙隆k kifizet脙漏se.

Ezzel szemben Magyarorsz脙隆gon a rezsics脙露kkent脙漏s rendszere vil脙隆gos 脙漏s c脙漏lzott v脙隆lasz. V脙漏di a csal脙隆dokat, kisz脙隆m脙颅that脙鲁v脙隆 teszi a k脙露lts脙漏geket, 脙漏s enyh脙颅ti az infl脙隆ci脙鲁s nyom脙隆st is. Amit szint脙漏n hi脙隆nyolok a d脙隆n eln脙露ks脙漏gi programban: a lakhat脙隆s k脙漏rd脙漏se nem el脙漏g hangs脙潞lyos, pedig az eur脙鲁pai fiatalok sz脙隆m脙隆ra ez 脙漏get脜聭en fontos. Magyarorsz脙隆g nem feledkezik meg a fiatalokr脙鲁l. Az els脜聭 saj脙隆t lak脙隆shoz jut脙隆s 脙漏rdek脙漏ben a korm脙隆ny h脙隆romsz脙隆zal脙漏kos hitellel t脙隆mogatja 50 milli脙鲁 forint 脙漏rt脙漏kben, 25 脙漏ves futamid脜聭vel. Ez egy val脙鲁di, k脙漏zzelfoghat脙鲁 seg脙颅ts脙漏g. 脙聳n脙露k is maradjanak a realit脙隆s talaj脙隆n.

脗聽脗聽Tomas Tob脙漏 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commission, Prime Minister, first, I'd like to say that you have my deepest respect for your leadership on Ukraine. From a Swede to a Dane, mange tak.

I would also like to state that the EPP have very high expectations of the Danish Presidency. We need a stronger defence 芒聙聯 S&D Group, please, your Prime Minister is here, welcome to this debate 芒聙聯 from the EPP Group, we need a stronger defence. We need to tackle and combat organised crime. We need to take back control of migration and we need to make Europe more competitive with more growth instead of decline. Therefore, we need to deliver on the omnibus proposals and also make sure that we sign more free trade agreements.

Prime Minister, if you deliver on this, you will have an ally in the EPP. We will support you and we will work with you. And I would say that I hope that the voice of the Danish Government, the Danish S&D, would also be the voice of S&D in this House, because then we could really get things done. We wish you luck and we would like to cooperate with you. Good luck.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hel脙漏ne Fritzon (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! K脙陇ra Mette, jag v脙陇lkomnar det danska ordf脙露randeskapet. Mitt dejlige grannland. V脙陇lkommen!

I en tid d脙楼 v脙陇rlden skakar beh脙露ver Europa ett ledarskap som st脙楼r stadigt f脙露r v脙楼r s脙陇kerhet, f脙露r klimatet och f脙露r v脙楼r demokrati. Mette, du 脙陇r en stark ledare. Du 脙陇r en statsminister som st脙楼r p脙楼 medborgarnas sida, och det beh脙露ver vi i Europa i dag.

Danmarks prioriteringar 脙陇r mycket tydliga. EU ska forts脙陇tta ta ledartr脙露jan med en ambiti脙露s klimatpolitik. En gr脙露n omst脙陇llning med gr脙露na investeringar som driver Europas konkurrenskraft och tillv脙陇xt 芒聙聯 ja, det 脙陇r v脙陇gen fram脙楼t.

Men d脙楼 kr脙陇vs ocks脙楼 ett tydligt m脙楼l f脙露r 2040. Jag s脙陇ger minst 90脗聽%, med m脙楼let 95, f脙露r det s脙陇ger vetenskapen. Det h脙陇r handlar om v脙楼ra barns och barnbarns framtid, s脙楼 jag 脙露nskar er all framg脙楼ng med ordf脙露randeskapet. Lycka till!

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pierre-Romain Thionnet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Premi脙篓re ministre, et si la pr脙漏sidence danoise 脙漏tait celle d芒聙聶une pr脙漏f脙漏rence europ脙漏enne enfin assum脙漏e? Le Danemark a l芒聙聶occasion de montrer la voie. En mati脙篓re d芒聙聶industrie de la d脙漏fense, vous h脙漏ritez des n脙漏gociations du programme EDIP, et il est essentiel que l芒聙聶ambition de ce texte ne soit pas an脙漏antie par des d脙漏rogations qui prolongeraient la d脙漏pendance 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏gard des 脙聣tats-Unis.

En mati脙篓re de politique migratoire, le Danemark est source d芒聙聶inspiration. Vous nous montrez que la coh脙漏sion culturelle et sociale d芒聙聶une nation n芒聙聶est possible qu芒聙聶脙聽 l芒聙聶aide d芒聙聶un strict contr脙麓le des fronti脙篓res et d芒聙聶une r脙漏gulation des 脙漏quilibres d脙漏mographiques.

D脙漏fense, immigration: ce ne sont l脙聽 que deux enjeux, mais peut-脙陋tre les plus essentiels et les plus embl脙漏matiques du nouveau monde qui se dessine sous nos yeux. Les deux sont intimement li脙漏s. L芒聙聶Europe n芒聙聶aura pas de destin libre si elle n芒聙聶a pas les moyens militaires de dissuader les nouvelles pr脙漏tentions imp脙漏riales. Et l芒聙聶Europe ne sera pas cr脙漏dible 脙聽 l芒聙聶ext脙漏rieur si son identit脙漏 n芒聙聶est pas garantie 脙聽 l芒聙聶int脙漏rieur.

Gardien des d脙漏troits nordiques, le Danemark a aujourd芒聙聶hui la possibilit脙漏 de diriger l芒聙聶isthme europ脙漏en vers une nouvelle orientation: celle de sa renaissance.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Henrik Dahl (PPE). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! K脙娄re kolleger! K脙娄re statsminister! Velkommen til Europa-Parlamentet og Strasbourg og tak for en god og vigtig tale. Vi har ikke brug for eksperimenter. Vi har brug for samling af vores f脙娄lles v脙娄rdier, frihed, fornuft og folkestyre. Og jeg synes, det danske formandskab prioriteringer afspejler pr脙娄cis den alvor og ansvarlighed, der er brug for. Den danske statsminister er kendt for at kunne sige tingene klart, n脙楼r situationen og alvoren kr脙娄ver det. Og den evne h脙楼ber jeg, vores statsminister vil bruge i en helt afg脙赂rende sag: Ukraine. For sandheden er, at Europa tidligere har svigtet Ukraine. I 2008 afviste Tyskland og Frankrig at tilbyde Ukraine en vej ind i NATO af hensyn til Rusland. Det har haft store konsekvenser. I dag bakker Europa op om Ukraine i ord, men det kniber stadig med handling. I Danmark st脙赂tter vi med cirka to procent af BNP. Tyskland, Frankrig, Italien og Spanien giver langt mindre. Hvis alle st脙赂ttede som Danmark, havde Ukraine for l脙娄ngst vundet over Rusland. Derfor opfordrer jeg statsministeren til at tale klart med de lande, der endnu ikke har forst脙楼et alvoren.

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Szczerba (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Panie Komisarzu! Wysoka Izbo! Przede wszystkim wyra脜录amy wielkie zadowolenie, 脜录e po prze脜聜omowej polskiej prezydencji skoncentrowanej na kwestii bezpiecze脜聞stwa Dania przejmuje prezydencj脛聶 r脙鲁wnie脜录 z mocnym postanowieniem: bezpiecze脜聞stwo musi by脛聡 w centrum naszej uwagi.

Dania otrzymuje t脛聶 prezydencj脛聶 z instrumentem SAFE, kt脙鲁ry daje 150 mld euro na uzbrojenie Europy, ale r脙鲁wnie脜录 w bardzo wa脜录nym momencie, jakim b脛聶d脛聟 negocjacje nad wieloletnimi ramami finansowymi. Unia Europejska musi mie脛聡 solidny bud脜录et, by sprosta脛聡 wyzwaniom, tak脜录e w obszarze bezpiecze脜聞stwa. I tutaj Fundusz Konkurencyjno脜聸ci b脛聶dzie bardzo istotnym wsparciem. Chcieli脜聸my 脜录yczy脛聡 du脜聞skiej prezydencji wszystkiego dobrego. Mo脜录ecie polega脛聡 na polskich przyjacio脜聜ach.
