脗聽脗聽Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Prime Minister, your programme calls for a strong Europe in a changing world. But it would be good to enshrine one word: a strong social Europe in a changing world. And I trust the Danish Social Democratic Government, because you can do your best, strengthening the social dimension in the EU, because a social dimension means possibilities to convince European people to have much stronger reforms.

We need a more integrated Europe, we need a more effective Europe, and we need more attention in next multiannual financial framework, defending cohesion funds, defending cooperation, defending agricultural funds and not cutting those investments.

And of course: enlargement, enlargement, and once again, enlargement! It's one important tool for defending Ukraine and defending peace in Europe. Peace in Europe means support in Ukraine and peace is our common value.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Prime Minister, I would like to congratulate Denmark on the Council Presidency. I am delighted to see your programme addressing key EU challenges 芒聙聯 tackling illegal migration and securing external borders, as well as continuing the unwavering support for Ukraine and the pressure on Russia.

Countering issues like migrant smuggling and instrumentalised migration are not only vital for the safety of the victims of these practices, but also for the internal security of Europe.

The recent incitements to Islamic violence in Europe by Iran, the ongoing threat posed by uncontrolled migration, the threat of Russia 芒聙聯 this is not an easy time for Europe.

When it comes to migration policy, many European conservatives have envied strict Danish practices that have had vast support in your country. I hope those strict practices will become the future of Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raquel Garc脙颅a Hermida-Van Der Walle (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Madam Prime Minister, I fully agree with you 芒聙聯 the European Union should have security and competitiveness as priorities. But there will not be real security as long as foreign governments can corrupt our public officials. And there will not be competitiveness in a corrupt system. That is why we need a strong anti-corruption directive.

And it is a disgrace that Member States are blocking this at this moment. They are playing political games in a dirty tit for tat: 'If you support my opposition to an anti-corruption directive, I will support your opposition to better green legislation.' We cannot allow this to happen.

Citizens are watching, and I hope, I really hope that under your leadership, the Danish Presidency will manage to convince Member States of the need for a new anti-corruption directive. I wish you all the luck.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maro脜隆 脜聽ef脛聧ovi脛聧, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Prime Minister, honourable Members, first and foremost, I know the time is really, really flying, but I think that also this debate was so clear that it allowed me to be very brief because the discussion among the honourable MEPs clearly confirmed strong, across-the-board support for the Danish Presidency's priorities.

I believe that there is a clear recognition of the challenging times we are living and therefore, in the Commission, we very much appreciate that the Danish Presidency's priorities keep focus on how to tackle these challenges and how to deal with them.

I think the debate clearly reflected strong underlying support for Ukraine, the need to work more for a secure Europe and promote a common defence, not to repeat the mistakes of complacency we committed over the last decade, as it was rightly pointed out by the Prime Minister in her opening remarks.

The second sectorial priority, which was reflected in many interventions of the honourable Members of the European Parliament, is the importance of the implementation of the Pact on Migration and Asylum, where we look forward to advancing legislative files which are currently on the table together with our Danish colleagues.

Third, most highlighted remarks have been focusing on the importance of competitiveness and simplification. And I agree with those MEPs who see it as absolutely crucial to respond to the current challenges. I believe that the programme of the Danish Presidency is clearly reflecting this priority as well.

As you know, Denmark is traditionally a strong supporter and ally in promoting the agenda of free and fair trade. And clearly there is a huge agenda there. And I can tell you, as Commissioner for Trade, that it is a good feeling to know that you have the support of the presidency and that we share common high ambition in this area.

To conclude, Madam President, I just would like to refer to the common Slovak proverb which, if translated into English, says 'good start means good conclusion', and we indeed need a good start on multiannual financial framework negotiations, because we know how difficult they are.

But I believe that with the ambition of the Danish Presidency to propose negotiating books already by the end of the year, we will get there, we will do that, and we will have a good start.

So, Madam Prime Minister, good luck. Lots of success to your presidency. Thank you very much.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much, Commissioner. Now, dear colleagues, we will hear from the Prime Minister.

Dear Mette, you have seen quite a broad version of views for the past one hour and a half. Thank you for sitting through them. If you can try to answer most of them, that would be good.

Dear colleagues, can we please listen to the Prime Minister of Denmark as the sitting presidency.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mette Frederiksen, formand for R脙楼det. 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Tak for mange gode indl脙娄g. Jeg er enig i det meste. Der var nogle, der var lidt besynderlige, dem vender jeg tilbage til. Der var en af jer, der sagde 芒聙聯 en dansker 芒聙聯 at en af mine forg脙娄ngere p脙楼 et tidspunkt har sagt, at ideologi er noget bras. Det er jeg meget, meget uenig i. Hvis ikke man som politiker kender sin retning, s脙楼 bliver man bl脙娄st omkuld. Og hvis vi som europ脙娄ere ikke ved, hvor vi vil hen nu, s脙楼 er der rigtig mange fjender i den her verden, der har t脙娄nkt sig at bl脙娄se Europa omkuld. S脙楼 vi bliver n脙赂dt til at kende vores retning. Og n脙楼r vi har den, s脙楼 skal vi v脙娄re pragmatiske, s脙楼 skal vi finde kompromiserne, og s脙楼 skal vi v脙娄re villige til at finde l脙赂sningerne.

For mig er der kun 脙漏n vej frem nu, og det er Europa 芒聙聯 Europa, Europa, Europa. Det siger jeg, m脙楼ske ogs脙楼 henvendt den her vej... (afbrudt af klapsalver) ja, men jeg skal se, om jeg kan f脙楼 Parlamentet til at klappe p脙楼 det samme tidspunkt i dag, for jeg vil gerne g脙赂re det meget klart, at min foruds脙娄tning for at kunne sige Europa, det er, at jeg selv er meget, meget stolt af mit f脙娄dreland. Jeg er dansker, f脙赂r jeg er noget andet. Jeg tror p脙楼 nationalstaten. Jeg tror ogs脙楼 p脙楼 gr脙娄nsekontrol. Jeg tror p脙楼, at vi skal have lov til at styre, hvem der kommer ind i vores land, og hvem der kommer ind p脙楼 vores kontinent. Men foruds脙娄tningen for, at jeg kan passe p脙楼 mit land 芒聙聯 det Danmark, jeg elsker 芒聙聯 det er... (afbrudt af klapsalver) det er... (afbrudt af tilr脙楼b og klapsalver) nej, nej, nej, I klapper alt, alt, alt for tidligt. I klapper alt for tidligt i denne her proces, fordi...

Yes, that's a good start, but...

Egentlig er jeg glad for at st脙楼 i midten i dag, fordi der er ikke nogen 芒聙聯 beklager 芒聙聯 politisk gruppe i det her parlament, jeg synes har svaret p脙楼 alle udfordringer p脙楼 samme tidspunkt, og derfor vil min appel v脙娄re: l脙娄g m脙楼ske partigrupperne lidt til side, og hav nu kun 脙漏n ting for 脙赂je: Hvad er det f脙娄lles bedste for hele vores Europa? 1) Vi skal have ambiti脙赂se klimam脙楼l. Venner, det er uholdbart, at vi betaler for Putins krigsmaskine i et europ脙娄isk land, det bliver n脙赂dt til at slutte. (Klapsalver i den ene side af salen). Nu glemmer I at klappe i den her side af parlamentssalen. Men 1) at finansiere en russisk krig i Europa er forkert, 2) vi er ogs脙楼 n脙赂dt til at sige, at det er uholdbart, at vi som politikere ikke kan skaffe vores befolkninger og vores virksomheder ren, gr脙赂n og billig energi. Derfor: hold fast i ambitionerne, og s脙赂rg for, at den gr脙赂nne transformation bliver til virkelighed. Og 2) krigen i Ukraine har aldrig handlet kun om Ukraine. Krigen i Ukraine handler om et Rusland, der 脙赂nsker at bygge et st脙赂rre Rusland, og de er villige til at sl脙楼 vores europ脙娄iske naboer ihjel. Derfor skal vi forts脙娄tte, ikke alene vores st脙赂tte og vores opbakning til Ukraine. Ukraine B脙聵R blive medlem af NATO. De SKAL v脙娄re medlem af EU. Vi bliver n脙赂dt til at se vores opbakning til Ukraine som forsvaret af os selv, og aldrig... (afbrudt af klapsalver) og aldrig nogensinde igen, m脙楼 vi bringe Europa i en situation, hvor Europa ikke kan forsvare sig selv. Vi skal ikke ligge i l脙娄 af andre. Europa skal nu ranke ryggen. Vi skal inden 2030 kunne forsvare os selv i t脙娄tte alliancer med andre, men det er vores opgave 芒聙聯 os, der er her - sammen med vores kolleger at passe p脙楼 vores eget kontinent. Derfor bliver vi n脙赂dt til at f脙楼 industrien til at k脙赂re.

We are no longer at peacetime. Europe is no longer at peacetime. Therefore, we have to rearm ourselves.

Og m脙楼 jeg s脙楼 til sidst bare lige sige et par enkelte ord omkring migration. Det er jo netop fordi jeg er socialdemokrat, at jeg tror p脙楼 og k脙娄mper for en stram udl脙娄ndingepolitik. For de, der betaler prisen ved den alt for h脙赂je kriminalitet i rigtig mange europ脙娄iske hovedst脙娄der lige nu 芒聙聯 de, der betaler prisen for den usikkerhed, som den ukontrollerede indvandring har medf脙赂rt til vores lande, det er de d脙楼rligst stillede europ脙娄ere. S脙楼 det er netop fordi, jeg er socialdemokrat, at jeg st脙楼r for den udl脙娄ndingepolitik, jeg g脙赂r... (afbrudt af klapsalver) I klapper herovre ... jeg skal nok slutte nu ... I klapper stadigv脙娄k for tidligt, fordi der er for mange i den her side af Parlamentet, der bliver ved med at p脙楼st脙楼 over for europ脙娄erne, at I kan passe p脙楼 Europa uden os andre. Intet europ脙娄isk land er i dag stort nok til at passe p脙楼 sig selv og deres befolkninger. Derfor 芒聙聯 og det skal v脙娄re min afsluttende kommentar 芒聙聯 jeg er glad for, at vi har en st脙娄rk EU-Kommission lige nu. Jeg er glad for, at det er Ursula von der Leyen, der st脙楼r i spidsen for det europ脙娄iske samarbejde. Vi har brug for samarbejde, vi har brug for handlekraft. Et splittet Europa er den bedste gave, I kan give Rusland, og derfor f脙楼r I aldrig nogensinde lov til, at det kommer til at ske. Vi bliver n脙赂dt til at st脙楼 sammen. Vi skal passe p脙楼 vores befolkninger. Vi skal passe p脙楼 vores v脙娄rdier. Vi skal passe p脙楼 Europa. Og i 脙赂vrigt tak for opbakningen til hele kongeriget Danmark inklusive Gr脙赂nland. Jeg gl脙娄der mig til samarbejdet. Det bliver et sv脙娄rt halvt 脙楼r, men tag ikke fejl, Europa har st脙楼et over for langt st脙赂rre udfordringer end dem, vi st脙楼r over for i dag. Og st脙楼r vi sammen i Europa, kan vi l脙赂se selv de sv脙娄reste opgaver. Jeg gl脙娄der mig til samarbejdet med jer allesammen.

7.1. Bulgaria's adoption of the euro on 1 January 2026 (A10-0113/2025 - Eva Maydell) (vote)

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Our second point on the agenda. I have received a request for adjournment of the vote on the report on the adoption by Bulgaria of the euro on 1 January 2026. Pursuant to Rule 206(4), a political group or Members reaching at least a low threshold may present a motion calling for a vote to be adjourned. This is the case.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rada Laykova, on behalf of the ESN Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, we move to adjourn the vote on the Maydell report until the Advisory Committee has made its ruling based on Rule脗聽206(4). There is an active investigation into a serious conflict of interest because the rapporteur is from the same party that governs Bulgaria, the very government she is expected to evaluate impartially. This report asks one thing: have the convergence criteria been met? These exist to protect the citizens and not the interests of governments, and here the governing party is evaluating itself.

The Commission and the Bulgarian Government have already had their exchange, and now it's Parliament's job to scrutinise and not to rubber stamp.脗聽This conflict goes to the heart of institutional integrity 芒聙聯 we filed a complaint weeks ago and there has been no ruling yet. If it comes after the vote, this could seriously undermine this Parliament's credibility. We still have the chance to act responsibly. This is not just a technical matter 芒聙聯 it's about trust and democratic scrutiny and our role as guardians of public interest.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jeroen Lenaers, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, so the EPP is against this request to adjourn the vote on Bulgaria's adoption of the euro.

Let's also be frank about what this is really about: Ms Laykova is not interested in the Rules of Procedure or some fantasy allegations on conflict of competence, especially coming from a shadow rapporteur with the same nationality on the same file.

What this is really about is that Ms Laykova and her friends in the ESN Group would rather have Bulgaria join the rouble than the euro.

So, all the correct procedures have been followed. This House is ready to vote, so let's do so.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I put the request to a vote by roll call.

(Parliament rejected the request)

We continue with the votes.

The first vote is on Bulgaria's adoption of the euro on 1 January 2026脗聽(see minutes, item 7.1).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

脨聻脩聜 脨戮脨陆脨碌脨路脨赂, 脨潞脨戮脨赂脩聜脨戮 脨卤脩聫脩聟脨掳 脨陆脨掳 脨驴脨禄脨戮脩聣脨掳脨麓脨掳 脨驴脩聙脨碌脨路 1989 脨鲁. 脨赂 脨录脨赂脩聙脨陆脨戮 脩聛脨虏脨掳脨禄脨赂脩聟脨掳 脨潞脨戮脨录脩聝脨陆脨赂脩聛脩聜脨赂脩聡脨碌脩聛脨潞脨赂脩聫 脩聙脨碌脨露脨赂脨录, 脨驴脩聙脨碌脨路 脨戮脨陆脨碌脨路脨赂, 脨潞脨戮脨赂脩聜脨戮 脨赂脨路脨鲁脨禄脨掳脨麓脩聝脨虏脨掳脩聟脨掳 脨虏脨掳脨禄脩聝脩聜脨陆脨掳脩聜脨掳 脨潞脩聙脨赂脨路脨掳 脨陆脨掳 1997 脨鲁. 脨麓脨戮 脨戮脨陆脨碌脨路脨赂 脨录脨禄脨掳脨麓脨赂 脩聟脨戮脩聙脨掳, 脨潞脨戮脨赂脩聜脨戮 脨麓脨陆脨碌脩聛 脨陆脨掳脩聙脨赂脩聡脨掳脩聜 脨聲脨虏脩聙脨戮脨驴脨掳 脩聛脨虏脨戮脩聫 脨麓脨戮脨录. 脨聼脩聙脨赂脨碌脨录脨掳脨陆脨碌脩聜脨戮 脨陆脨掳 脨碌脨虏脩聙脨戮脩聜脨戮 脨碌 脨驴脨戮脩聛脩聜脨赂脨鲁脨掳脨陆脨碌 脨陆脨掳 脨碌脨虏脩聙脨戮脨驴脨碌脨鹿脩聛脨潞脨掳脩聜脨掳 脨录脨碌脩聡脩聜脨掳 脨路脨掳 脩聜脩聫脩聟 脨虏 脩聠脩聫脨禄脨掳脩聜脨掳 脩聺 脨驴脩聤脨禄脨陆脨戮脩聜脨掳. 脨聵 脨陆脨赂脨碌 脨麓脩聤脨禄脨露脨赂脨录 脨陆脨掳 脨虏脩聛脨赂脩聡脨潞脨赂 脩聜脩聫脩聟 脨戮脨麓脨戮脨卤脩聙脨碌脨陆脨赂脨碌脩聜脨戮 脨陆脨掳 脩聜脨戮脨路脨赂 脨麓脨戮脨潞脨禄脨掳脨麓. 脨聭脩聤脨禄脨鲁脨掳脩聙脨赂脩聫 脨碌 脨鲁脨戮脩聜脨戮脨虏脨掳, 脨聲脨虏脩聙脨戮脨驴脨掳 脨碌 脨鲁脨戮脩聜脨戮脨虏脨掳. 脨聺脨碌脨潞脨掳 脨陆脨掳脨驴脩聙脨掳脨虏脨赂脨录 脩聜脨掳脨路脨赂 脨潞脩聙脨掳脩聡脨潞脨掳 脨路脨掳脨碌脨麓脨陆脨戮.

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 It is adopted. Congratulations, Bulgaria!

7.3. Temporary derogation from certain provisions of Regulations (EU) 2017/2226 and (EU) 2016/399 (A10-0082/2025 - Assita Kanko) (vote)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next vote is on the temporary derogation from certain provisions of Regulations (EU) 2017/2226 and (EU) 2016/399 (see minutes, item 7.3).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 Before the vote:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Assita Kanko, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Waarom hebben wij het Entry/Exit System (EES) nodig? Wij hebben EES nodig om paspoortcontroles van burgers van derde landen accurater en effici脙芦nter te kunnen uitvoeren. We hebben EES脗聽nodig om op elk moment te kunnen zien welke onderdanen van derde landen zich in de EU bevinden en of zij zich nog aan de termijnen van hun verblijf houden. We hebben EES nodig omdat het de hoeksteen is van de Europese grensarchitectuur en omdat in de toekomst andere belangrijke veiligheidssystemen op EES gebouwd gaan worden. We hebben EES nodig omdat het een stukje is van de puzzel om onze burgers veilig te houden. Enkel door de Schengenbuitengrenzen streng te bewaken, zullen wij de binnengrenzen open kunnen houden. Dat is van cruciaal belang voor onze burgers en bedrijven.

Collega芒聙聶s, ik ben blij dat we vandaag kunnen stemmen over het Entry/Exit System. Het is alweer van het jaar 2017 geleden, na de aanslagen in Brussel en Parijs, dat dit systeem door Raad en Parlement is goedgekeurd. Acht jaar later wachten wij nog altijd op de invoering. Als we de aanpassingen aan het systeem vandaag goedkeuren, kunnen de lidstaten dit najaar echt van start gaan. Zij hebben dit nu ook voor het eerst unaniem toegezegd.

Ik roep u dus allemaal op om hier nu voor te stemmen. Ik dank onze medewerkers, de Commissie, het Poolse Raadsvoorzitterschap en alle schaduwrapporteurs voor hun constructieve houding. Ik zie nu uit naar een positieve stemming die ons harde werken van de afgelopen maanden bevestigt en Schengen versterkt.

脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 Before the vote on the motion for a resolution:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Emma Fourreau, rapporteure. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, l芒聙聶Arctique se r脙漏chauffe quatre fois plus vite que le reste du globe. C芒聙聶est le signal d芒聙聶alarme que lancent les scientifiques. Cette r脙漏gion du monde, pourtant essentielle dans la lutte contre le d脙漏r脙篓glement climatique, en est d芒聙聶ores et d脙漏j脙聽 la premi脙篓re victime. Le Groenland ne fait pas exception, et sa population souffre des effets du d脙漏r脙篓glement climatique, lesquels touchent directement les lieux et les modes de vie. La p脙陋che, qui repr脙漏sente le plus gros secteur 脙漏conomique avec plus de 15脗聽% des emplois et 90脗聽% des exportations, en est affect脙漏e, notamment par la fonte des glaces et par l芒聙聶acidification de l芒聙聶oc脙漏an.

Dans ce contexte, l芒聙聶accord de p脙陋che que nous votons aujourd芒聙聶hui est crucial en ce qu芒聙聶il constitue un important m脙漏canisme de soutien financier et un cadre de coop脙漏ration entre l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne et le Groenland. Si cet accord est imparfait, notamment pour ce qui est de la surexploitation de certaines populations de poissons ou du manque de donn脙漏es scientifiques, je vous invite 脙聽 l芒聙聶adopter, car il est indispensable que l芒聙聶Europe affirme haut et fort son soutien au Groenland.

Pourquoi aujourd芒聙聶hui plus que jamais? Parce qu芒聙聶une puissance 脙漏trang脙篓re, les 脙聣tats-Unis d芒聙聶Am脙漏rique, et son pr脙漏sident, Donald脗聽Trump, menacent un territoire membre des pays et territoires d芒聙聶outre-mer de l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne; parce qu芒聙聶une puissance 脙漏trang脙篓re promet d芒聙聶envahir un pays constitutif du royaume du Danemark. Le manque de r脙漏action de la part de la Commission face 脙聽 des menaces d芒聙聶emploi de la force est inacceptable. Nous devons envoyer un message fort au peuple groenlandais: nous vous soutenons et jamais nous ne baisserons la t脙陋te, jamais nous ne c脙漏derons devant l芒聙聶imp脙漏rialisme am脙漏ricain et 脙漏tasunien.

C芒聙聶est pourquoi je vous invite 脙聽 voter en faveur de mon amendement de soutien face aux d脙漏clarations de l芒聙聶administration Trump. Cet accord va dans le bon sens, car la contribution financi脙篓re de l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne a 脙漏t脙漏 augment脙漏e. Le protocole et la l脙漏gislation groenlandaise pr脙漏voient 脙漏galement des garanties visant 脙聽 prot脙漏ger l芒聙聶environnement marin, les communaut脙漏s de p脙陋cheurs c脙麓tiers et les populations autochtones.

Aussi, en signe de soutien au peuple groenlandais, afin qu芒聙聶il conserve des relations de partenariat avec l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne, et pour renforcer nos liens face 脙聽 la menace 脙漏tasunienne, je vous invite 脙聽 voter pour l芒聙聶accord et sa r脙漏solution.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 Before the vote on Amendment 9:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, I propose to push the European Investment Bank to align its activities with the recent report of UN Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese regarding the role certain corporations play in violations of international law.

The text of the amendment would be the following: 'urgently calls on the European Investment Bank to stop providing support to companies involved in sustaining the illegal Israeli occupation and its ongoing genocidal campaign in Gaza'.

You can, of course, oppose this, but it would mean putting the EIB outside international law.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Parliament did not agree to put the oral amendment to the vote)

脗聽

7.8. Security of energy supply in the EU (A10-0121/2025 - Beata Szyd脜聜o) (vote)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

芒聙聯 Before the vote:

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Beata Szyd脜聜o, Sprawozdawczyni. 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Za chwil脛聶 b脛聶dziemy g脜聜osowa脛聡 nad sprawozdaniem dotycz脛聟cym bezpiecze脜聞stwa dostaw energii, a to oznacza, 脜录e b脛聶dziemy g脜聜osowa脛聡 nad sprawozdaniem dotycz脛聟cym bezpiecze脜聞stwa naszych kraj脙鲁w, bezpiecze脜聞stwa Unii Europejskiej. Bo dzisiaj bezpieczne dostawy energii to jest w脜聜a脜聸nie gwarancja bezpiecze脜聞stwa Unii Europejskiej i naszych suwerennych pa脜聞stw.

Je脜录eli wyrazimy wszyscy zgod脛聶 na to, aby to sprawozdanie zosta脜聜o przyj脛聶te, Mamy szans脛聶, 脜录e b脛聶dzie to nasz wsp脙鲁lny europejski g脜聜os w sprawie bezpiecze脜聞stwa dostaw energii i b脛聶dzie on jedynym takim g脜聜osem. Poniewa脜录 przypomn脛聶, 脜录e wcze脜聸niej zosta脜聜y odrzucone konkluzje Rady dotycz脛聟ce tego tematu. Niestety, z przykro脜聸ci脛聟 stwierdz脛聶, 脜录e przez r脙鲁wnie脜录 si脜聜y, kt脙鲁re s脛聟 w tym Parlamencie, a rzecz dotyczy oczywi脜聸cie wsp脙鲁脜聜pracy z Rosj脛聟.

I nawi脛聟脜录脛聶 tutaj do s脜聜脙鲁w pani premier Danii, kt脙鲁ra b脛聶dzie w tej chwili przewodniczy脜聜a europejskiej prezydencji. Nie mo脜录na wsp脙鲁脜聜pracowa脛聡 z agresorem, a bezpiecze脜聞stwo powinno by脛聡 ponad podzia脜聜ami politycznymi, bez wzgl脛聶du na to, po kt脙鲁rej stronie sali siedzimy. Pani premier wywodzi si脛聶 z ugrupowa脜聞 zasiadaj脛聟cej po lewej stronie sali Parlamentu Europejskiego. Ja jestem tutaj po stronie prawej. Ale podobnie jak ona, r脙鲁wnie脜录 z w脜聜asnych do脜聸wiadcze脜聞 wiem, 脜录e konsensus i wsp脙鲁脜聜praca dotycz脛聟ca bezpiecze脜聞stwa jest naszym wielkim obowi脛聟zkiem i nasz脛聟 wsp脙鲁ln脛聟 spraw脛聟.

Chc脛聶 podzi脛聶kowa脛聡 wszystkim kontrsprawozdawcom, z kt脙鲁rymi przez wiele miesi脛聶cy wsp脙鲁脜聜pracowali脜聸my nad tym projektem. To jest dobry konsensus. R脙鲁脜录nimy si脛聶 politycznie, mamy r脙鲁脜录ne pogl脛聟dy, ale uda脜聜o nam si脛聶 znale脜潞脛聡 porozumienie, kt脙鲁re gwarantuje bezpiecze脜聞stwo Unii Europejskiej.

7.9. Preserving the memory of the victims of the post war communist period in Slovenia (B10-0322/2025) (vote)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I would like to remind Members who are on the speakers' list for the first debate in the afternoon to remain in the Chamber. The debate will start immediately after the votes.

On the last point of the day, I have received a request for adjournment of the vote on the resolution on preserving the memory of the victims of post-war communist period in Slovenia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandra G脙鲁mez L脙鲁pez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, solicitamos que se posponga la votaci脙鲁n sobre la base de los apartados脗聽1, 4 y脗聽12 de la propuesta de Resoluci脙鲁n, porque se aprob脙鲁 en la Comisi脙鲁n de Peticiones infringiendo las normas, ya que cont脙鲁 con el rechazo de cuatro grupos pol脙颅ticos, porque no es una petici脙鲁n ciudadana 芒聙聰la diputada Romana Tomc tuvo la desfachatez de escribir en sus redes sociales que ella misma hab脙颅a escrito la petici脙鲁n y la Resoluci脙鲁n, lo que es una prueba m脙隆s de la politizaci脙鲁n que hace la derecha de la Comisi脙鲁n de Peticiones芒聙聰, pero, sobre todo, porque criminaliza a las personas que lucharon contra el fascismo y presenta como posibles v脙颅ctimas a posibles nazis y colaboradores, lo que es un insulto a los valores de la Uni脙鲁n Europea, pero, sobre todo, a la memoria de las personas que construyeron esta Europa.

Quiero dirigirme a los populares: su compa脙卤era Ursula von der Leyen dijo que no dej脙隆ramos que la extrema derecha reescribiera la historia. Ustedes solo los se脙卤alan, excepto cuando les prestan sus votos. No nos tomen por est脙潞pidos. No insulten nuestra inteligencia y dejen de alimentar al monstruo, porque no quiere pactos, quiere devorarles, y este jueves se lo va a dejar bien claro.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomas Tob脙漏, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, colleagues, in Slovenia, the Government have abolished the national remembrance day of the victims of communism. Now, some colleagues on the left refuse to support this resolution.

This is not about facts. This is about choosing party affiliation instead of respecting the victims of communism in Slovenia. The horrible mass graves are real. This is not only disrespectful for the victims, I would say that it is dangerous.

I would also like actually to turn to my dear colleagues in Renew. Please take a hard look at Slovenia, where your Prime Minister now is even considering taking Slovenia out of NATO. Put your focus on that instead of helping him sweep history under the carpet.

Mr脗聽President, the EPP will reject the postponement and we will ask for a roll call vote.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bogdan Rzo脜聞ca, przewodnicz脛聟cy komisji PETI. 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Chc脛聶 zapewni脛聡 wszystkich pa脜聞stwa, 脜录e wszystko to, co dzia脜聜o si脛聶 na posiedzeniu Komisji Petycji, dzia脜聜o si脛聶 bardzo transparentnie, zgodnie z regulaminem Parlamentu Europejskiego, zgodnie z regulaminem komisji, ka脜录dej komisji. Ka脜录dy mia脜聜 mo脜录liwo脜聸脛聡 dyskutowania o tre脜聸ci tej rezolucji, a tre脜聸脛聡 rezolucji nie jest prywatn脛聟 w脜聜asno脜聸ci脛聟 nikogo. Tylko autorami tej rezolucji s脛聟 europos脜聜owie z Komisji Petycji.

I o to dzisiaj prosz脛聶, 脜录eby脜聸my po prostu zag脜聜osowali niezw脜聜ocznie za t脛聟 rezolucj脛聟, poniewa脜录 ofiary komunizmu na S脜聜owenii nie mog脛聟 le脜录e脛聡 w workach foliowych, tylko ich szcz脛聟tki i prochy musz脛聟 by脛聡 godnie pochowane i uszanowane. Je脜聸li kto脜聸 nie respektuje warto脜聸ci chrze脜聸cija脜聞skich, to niech zagl脛聟dnie do 芒聙聻Antygony芒聙聺 Sofoklesa, i tam si脛聶 dowie, jak w脜聜a脜聸nie uczono jeszcze w staro脜录ytno脜聸ci respektu i szacunku do ka脜录dego cia脜聜a.

Uwa脜录am wi脛聶c, 脜录e trzeba po prostu za t脛聟 rezolucj脛聟 zag脜聜osowa脛聡, bo to jest wiara naszego humanitaryzmu.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽

9. State of play of implementation of the European Media Freedom Act in the Member States (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pre脜聼edinte. 芒聙聯 Urm脛聝torul punct de pe ordinea de zi este dezbaterea privind Declara脠聸iile Consiliului 脠聶i Comisiei referitoare la stadiul punerii 脙庐n aplicare a Regulamentului european privind libertatea mass-mediei 脙庐n statele membre (2025/2785(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members and Commissioners, freedom of expression and information is a fundamental human right. Free and pluralistic media are crucial elements of a vibrant and resilient democracy. To uphold these principles, the European Media Freedom Act is one of the flagship initiatives we agreed upon, and which will start to apply as of 8脗聽August.

Member States have been working on the implementation. The Commission is formally tasked with overseeing the implementation of the legislation. But the Council maintains a dialogue between Member States and the Commission to facilitate the implementation.

This has proven very useful in exchanging on the implementation challenges faced by Member States. The Commission has also facilitated the process from their end by organising a workshop with the relevant authorities of the Member States.

The Danish Presidency is committed to continuing this dialogue in the working group within the Council.

The Council also looks forward to the Commission's announced guidelines on the implementation of particular provisions, such as on Article脗聽18, on the content of media service providers on very large online platforms.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Lu脙颅s Albuquerque, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear Minister脗聽Bjerre, honourable Members, thank you for scheduling today's debate on the European Media Freedom Act. Media freedom and pluralism are at the heart of our European democracy. The debate today is an important opportunity to restate our joint commitment to upholding democratic values and supporting journalists across the continent.

A strong and independent media is essential to inform citizens and to make our democracies work. It serves as a watchdog, holding those in power accountable, exposing injustices, combatting disinformation and giving a voice to those who otherwise cannot be heard. Upholding media pluralism and media freedom, and supporting and protecting independent media and journalists are important priorities for this Commission. The swift and effective implementation of the European Media Freedom Act will be critical for achieving these priorities.

As you all know, we are approaching an important milestone as most of the provisions of the European Media Freedom Act will become applicable in exactly one month from today. As of 8脗聽August, we will also be able to rely on more safeguards for the independent functioning of public service media providers and media ownership transparency. These rules will enable citizens to have access to a plurality of information and opinions, while being aware of who is behind the news that they consume.

The entry into application of the European Media Freedom Act on 8脗聽August will also bring benefits to media service providers. They will enjoy fairer and more transparent allocation of state advertising expenditure and more predictability in media market concentrations, making it easier for them to expand their operations across the European internal market.脗聽Media companies will also be able to benefit from fair competition and better return on investment in the digital environment through, for example, the audience measurement transparency rules and new safeguards concerning content removals on very large online platforms.

As regards individual journalists, flagship rules on protection of journalistic sources and confidential communications will become directly enforceable. This includes provisions protecting journalists from being targeted by spyware.

The European Media Freedom Act includes several provisions where action from Member States in the form of specific implementing measures are expected. This includes setting up different mechanisms, bodies and procedures 芒聙聯 if not yet in place 芒聙聯 that will allow direct enforceability of the rules. National media regulatory authorities may also need to be officially granted new powers to give effect to the new rules.

For this purpose, the Commission has been engaging with Member States to ensure that the implementation of the European Media Freedom Act is on track ahead of the August deadline. Earlier this year, the Commission sent to all Member States a questionnaire regarding their national laws and implementation efforts. The questionnaire was followed by meetings with all the national administrations to gain more insight into specific national issues.

In the meantime, all Member States have submitted their responses to the questionnaire, and we are currently analysing the responses to ensure that we have a clear picture of the situation in Member States. We have remained at the disposal of Member States to support as needed so that, come 8脗聽August, the European Media Freedom Act can be effectively applied.

We continue to monitor any relevant developments in the Member States while providing support to the independent European Board for Media Services, which was established in February. The board carries out the important task of ensuring the consistent application of the act and the wider EU media law framework.

Before concluding, I would like to recall that media freedom and pluralism are one of the four pillars of the rule of law report. Today, the Commission should adopt the sixth edition of the annual rule of law report. With the implementation of the provisions of the European Media Freedom Act underway, the report provides a useful support mechanism to Member States.脗聽A correct application of the European Media Freedom Act will also help address a number of the recommendations made in this year's rule of law report, in particular those on public media independence, certain aspects of the protection of journalists, and the transparency and fairness in the allocation of state advertising.

Finally, I would like to express my gratitude for the support of the Danish Presidency for facilitating progress in the implementation of the European Media Freedom Act and, more broadly, for their commitment to strengthening information integrity and media freedom. I also encourage the European Parliament, you honourable Members, to continue to promote the effective implementation of this regulation so that we can reap the benefits that it promises.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sabine Verheyen, im Namen der PPE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, sehr geehrte Frau Kommissarin, sehr geehrte Vertreterin der d脙陇nischen Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Der European Media Freedom Act ist ein gro脙聼er Schritt 芒聙聯 ein Meilenstein auf dem Papier. Doch ein Gesetz alleine reicht nicht. Wir alle erinnern uns: Als der EMFA vorgestellt wurde, war die Begeisterung gro脙聼. Endlich ein europ脙陇isches Gesetz, das die journalistische Unabh脙陇ngigkeit sch脙录tzen soll. Ein klares Signal: Die EU steht an der Seite der freien Medien. Doch was wir jetzt sehen, ist ein bekanntes Muster. Der Gesetzentwurf wird gefeiert, aber wenn es um die Umsetzung geht, wird es pl脙露tzlich still. Dann reden wir 脙录ber Ausnahmen, 脙聹bergangsfristen, nationale Befindlichkeiten, und ich sage ganz klar: Das reicht nicht! Was wir brauchen, ist kein Applaus f脙录r Absichtserkl脙陇rungen. Was wir brauchen, ist politischer Mut, auch dann, wenn es unbequem wird. Denn es geht hier nicht um ein Detail der Regulierung. Es geht um die demokratische Grundarchitektur Europas. Eine vielf脙陇ltige, unabh脙陇ngige und freie Medienlandschaft ist kein nice to have. Sie ist das Fundament jeder freiheitlichen Demokratie. Und ja, Medien d脙录rfen unbequem sein, sie m脙录ssen es sogar. Eine Regierung, die damit nicht leben kann, stellt sich nicht gegen die Medien, sondern sie stellt sich gegen die Demokratie selbst.

Was wir derzeit beobachten, auch in der EU, ist beunruhigend. Journalisten werden nach wie vor 脙录berwacht, verklagt, unter Druck gesetzt, und in manchen Mitgliedstaaten hinkt die Umsetzung des EMFA weit hinterher. Gerade in den Teilen, die nicht wie eine Verordnung formuliert sind, sondern wo aktives Handeln in den Mitgliedstaaten notwendig ist. Gleichzeitig sehen wir, in einigen Staaten fehlt schlicht der politische Wille, wirklich etwas zu tun und die Medien unabh脙陇ngig zu machen. Deshalb mein Appell: Wir d脙录rfen nicht zulassen, dass sich die Regierungen f脙录r ihre Zustimmung zum Gesetz feiern lassen und sich dann aus der Verantwortung stehlen, wenn es ernst wird und es in die Umsetzung geht. Es braucht konsequente Umsetzung, klare Kontrolle und wenn n脙露tig, Verfahren gegen Mitgliedstaaten, die sich nicht an die gemeinsamen Regeln halten. Der 8.脗聽August ist nicht mehr fern und eigentlich ist das das Datum, wenn die Gesetzgebung wirken und umgesetzt sein soll. Nicht anfangen, umzusetzen und nicht anfangen, Strukturen aufzubauen. Das ist die Deadline gewesen. Wenn wir wirklich etwas f脙录r Medien machen wollen, dann m脙录ssen wir endlich konsequent und vor allem auch stark und mutig handeln.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandro Ruotolo, a nome del gruppo S&D. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, grazie on. Verheyen per le Sue parole di verit脙聽.

Tra un mese, l'8脗聽agosto, entrer脙聽 in vigore il Media Freedom Act.

Sono tanti i paesi che non hanno ancora "registrato" i loro regolamenti. A Cipro, per esempio, la bozza di legge rende pi脙鹿 facile spiare i giornalisti; della Slovacchia 脙篓 meglio non parlare; a Malta non si sa nemmeno quale ministero se ne occupi; nell'Ungheria di Orb脙隆n la volont脙聽 politica non esiste; e in Italia i vertici della Rai sono nominati dal governo, in contrasto con il Media Freedom Act.

La politica, ancora una volta, ha eletto il consiglio di amministrazione del servizio pubblico, ma non il presidente, perch脙漏 si 脙篓 divisa. Nessuna riforma della governance all'orizzonte: il controllo 脙篓 nelle mani della maggioranza. Dirigenti della Rai hanno rivendicato pubblicamente l'appartenenza al partito della premier Meloni. E, per finire, quattro giornalisti italiani sono spiati illegalmente con uno spyware, Graphite, venduto solo agli Stati da Paragon, azienda israeliana con fondi americani.

Basta alibi. I governi sanno cosa devono fare: mettersi in regola con il Media Freedom Act. Bruxelles ha il dovere di pretenderlo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gerolf Annemans, namens de PfE-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter! Zou Big Brother van George Orwell ook een Big Sister hebben kunnen zijn? We kennen inmiddels het antwoord:脗聽ja, natuurlijk! Emily O芒聙聶Reilly, de vorige ombudsvrouw van de Europese Unie, heeft het fenomeen beschreven. Een oppermachtige, onverkozen en ontransparante bedrijfscultuur aan de top van de Europese Commissie maakt de ondoorzichtigheid steeds erger. Ik was steeds, zegt ze, op mijn ongemak met de 芒聙聹machtige consiglieri芒聙聺 uit het kabinet van de voorzitster van de Commissie (een woord waarmee men maffia-adviseurs omschrijft). Alles komt van boven, topdown. Informatie wordt achtergehouden op bevel van boven. D脙聽t is de cultuur: dat achterhouden en de terughoudendheid bij het overhandigen van informatie is, ik citeer nog altijd, verontrustend.

We weten dus dat er een Big Sister, omringd door 芒聙聹powerful consiglieri芒聙聺, aan het hoofd staat van een Europa dat al lang geen samenwerking meer is, maar een imperium, een Big Sister imperium naar het model van Orwell.

Vandaag voegen wij daar andermaal een afschuwwekkend hoofdstuk aan toe: de Verordening 1083.脗聽De oprichting van een zogenaamde Europese Raad voor Mediadiensten. De centralisering van de macht over de media is Orwell copy paste. Dit wordt 100% zeker het toekomstige Ministerie van de Waarheid. Met onduidelijke definities en bevoegdheden die erop gericht zijn de oppositie te neutraliseren. Bovenaan staat werkelijk een sublieme versie van de typisch Orwelliaanse leugen. De verordening is gericht op, ik citeer, de bescherming van de vrijheid en de pluriformiteit van de media.

Ik concludeer, deze Europese Unie verwordt tot een totalitair regime. Moge onze kleinkinderen ons vergeven dat we het zo uit de hand hebben laten lopen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 (Protests from the gallery)

Excuse me. You do not have the right to protest, and I ask the ushers to take care.

We thank you for your message, but this is not the way to do it. I ask you to quietly leave the room. I am sorry for your pain, but this is not the way to protest in this Parliament.

We continue with Piotr M脙录ller on behalf of the Conservatives and Reformists Group of Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Piotr M脙录ller, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo, chcecie rozmawia脛聡 o tym, jak wygl脛聟da wolno脜聸脛聡 medi脙鲁w, ale chcia脜聜bym zacz脛聟脛聡 te脜录 od tego, w jaki spos脙鲁b ona jest realizowana tu, w tej Izbie, ale r脙鲁wnie脜录 u pa脜聞stwa przyjaci脙鲁脜聜 i lider脙鲁w Europejskiej Partii Ludowej.

Donald Tusk, jeden z lider脙鲁w Europejskiej Partii Ludowej, nie wpuszcza na konferencje prasowe w Polsce, na konferencj脛聶 premiera polskiego rz脛聟du, najwi脛聶kszej stacji informacyjnej - Telewizji Republika. Po prostu fizycznie zabrania wej脜聸cia na ich konferencje prasowe. To jeden z przyk脜聜ad脙鲁w.

Ostatnio, gdy dyskutowali脜聸my tutaj wczoraj o odwo脜聜aniu przewodnicz脛聟cej von der Leyen, zastosowali脜聸cie takie sztuczki, 脜录eby pos脜聜owie nie mogli si脛聶 wypowiedzie脛聡, dlaczego chcemy tego odwo脜聜ania. Chcieli脜聸cie tylko kr脙鲁tkich o脜聸wiadcze脜聞.

Wi脛聶c ja wykorzystam t脛聶 szans脛聶, aby to powiedzie脛聡: dlatego 脜录e prowadzicie niebezpieczn脛聟 polityk脛聶 dotycz脛聟c脛聟 migracji i paktu migracyjnego. Dlatego, 脜录e polityka Zielonego 脜聛adu powoduje ubo脜录enie spo脜聜ecze脜聞stwa i zagro脜录enie jako脜聸ci 脜录ycia oraz 脜录e umowa z Mercosurem, kt脙鲁r脛聟 chcieli脜聸cie utajni脛聡, powoduje, 脜录e polscy i europejscy rolnicy strac脛聟 na tej umowie.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I'm sorry. Was there a point of order? OK. Thank you so much.

I will take note, and I'll look back at the minutes of the speech. We continue with Hristo Petrov.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Diana Riba i Giner, en nombre del Grupo Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, aprobar el Reglamento Europeo sobre la Libertad de los Medios de Comunicaci脙鲁n fue un acto de victoria democr脙隆tica. Por primera vez, Europa cuenta con un Reglamento que garantiza el derecho de la ciudadan脙颅a a recibir informaci脙鲁n libre y plural. Pero hay un riesgo: tener un Reglamento hist脙鲁rico que no se cumpla. La organizaci脙鲁n Liberties ya nos ha alertado: hay Estados miembros que no est脙隆n preparados para aplicarlo o directamente no tienen intenci脙鲁n de hacerlo. Porque s脙颅, hoy en la Uni脙鲁n Europea hay Gobiernos que han convertido los medios p脙潞blicos en aparatos de propaganda y que utilizan el spyware contra periodistas, y lo m脙隆s c脙颅nico de todo es que son los mismos que hoy aqu脙颅 se atreven a hablar de censura.

Miren, si la extrema derecha se incomoda con este Reglamento, es que algo estamos haciendo muy bien. Pero la estela antidemocr脙隆tica se extiende: el Partido Popular espa脙卤ol aprueba leyes en Galicia para controlar la TVG, y en Valencia, con Vox, hacen leyes de lo mismo con 脙聙 Punt, lo que vulnera un Reglamento europeo que ellos mismos aprobaron en este Parlamento. 脗驴C脙鲁mo se puede firmar el Reglamento con una mano y romperlo con la otra? Por eso, lo que reivindicamos es muy simple: vigilancia efectiva por parte de la Comisi脙鲁n y que el periodismo libre y plural no vuelva a estar solo. Necesitamos el impulso de las fuerzas democr脙隆ticas 芒聙聰esas que vimos en Budapest芒聙聰 tambi脙漏n para defender la libertad de prensa, para defender la democracia.

脗聽脗聽Ewa Zaj脛聟czkowska-Hernik, w imieniu grupy ESN. 芒聙聯 Europejski akt o wolno脜聸ci medi脙鲁w. Zawsze, gdy w jakiej脜聸 unijnej dyrektywie, rezolucji albo rozporz脛聟dzeniu s脜聜ysz脛聶 termin wolno脜聸脛聡, to cierpnie mi sk脙鲁ra. Bo wolno脜聸脛聡 wed脜聜ug Komisji Europejskiej to cenzura, to reglamentacja tre脜聸ci, to nadmierna kontrola i kary za to, 脜录e kto脜聸 ma inn脛聟 definicj脛聶 wolno脜聸ci. Wy ju脜录 dawno zapomnieli脜聸cie, czym jest wolno脜聸脛聡. Przede wszystkim czym jest wolno脜聸脛聡 samodzielnego my脜聸lenia. Nie potraficie my脜聸le脛聡 samodzielnie i przeszkadza wam to, gdy kto脜聸 inny potrafi.

I 脜录eby t脛聶 wolno脜聸脛聡 w waszym mniemaniu wprowadzi脛聡, tworzycie narz脛聶dzia, kt脙鲁re w rzeczywisto脜聸ci s脜聜u脜录脛聟 do cenzurowania przestrzeni medialnej. DSA b脛聶dzie cenzurowa脜聜o Internet, europejski akt o wolno脜聸ci medi脙鲁w: radio, telewizj脛聶 i pras脛聶. Zawarli脜聸cie w tym akcie zbi脙鲁r pobo脜录nych 脜录ycze脜聞, 脜录eby teoretycznie przeciwdzia脜聜a脛聡 koncentracji medi脙鲁w, ale tylnym wej脜聸ciem wprowadzacie 脜聸rodki do wp脜聜ywania na dostawc脙鲁w us脜聜ug medialnych.

芒聙聻Rok 1984芒聙聺 Orwella mia脜聜 by脛聡 ostrze脜录eniem, a nie poradnikiem. To jest wasz model dzia脜聜ania. Tworzycie narz脛聶dzia o pi脛聶knych nazwach, by nas, zwyk脜聜ych ludzi, po prostu gn脛聶bi脛聡. Robicie brudne interesy i potrzebujecie medi脙鲁w, by o tym nie wspomina脜聜y, tworz脛聟c z medi脙鲁w polityczne narz脛聶dzie. A jak co脜聸 si脛聶 wam wymyka spod kontroli, to wprowadzacie cenzur脛聶 prewencyjn脛聟. Wydajecie pieni脛聟dze na lobbing np. za politycznym Zielonym 脜聛adem. Przekupuje dziennikarzy i organizacje pozarz脛聟dowe. I macie problem z tym, gdy to wychodzi na jaw i w脜聜a脜聸nie robi medialne zasi脛聶gi. Demokracja dla was jest wtedy, gdy kontrolujecie co, kto i o czym m脙鲁wi. To nie jest ta Unia, do kt脙鲁rej Polska wchodzi脜聜a w 2004 roku.

脗聽脗聽Pre脜聼edinte. 芒聙聯 Nu 脠聶tiu dac脛聝 am 脙庐n脠聸eles bine c脛聝 a脠聸i spus c脛聝 colegii nu au capacitatea de a g脙垄ndi liber. Cred c脛聝 toat脛聝 lumea are capacitatea de a g脙垄ndi liber 脙庐n aceast脛聝 sal脛聝. Urmeaz脛聝 domnul Zolt脙隆n Tarr.

脗聽脗聽Zolt脙隆n Tarr (PPE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k 脙職r! Tisztelt Miniszter Asszony! Tisztelt K脙漏pvisel脜聭t脙隆rsaim! A m脙漏diaszabads脙隆gr脙鲁l sz脙鲁l脙鲁 eur脙鲁pai rendelet eur脙鲁pais脙隆gunk 脙漏s nemzeti identit脙隆sunk meg脜聭rz脙漏s脙漏nek egy nagyon fontos eleme. A kevesebb mint egy h脙鲁nap m脙潞lva hat脙隆lyba l脙漏p脜聭 rendelkez脙漏s a m脙漏dia szabads脙隆g脙隆t biztos脙颅t脙鲁 keretek minimum脙隆t r脙露gz脙颅ti. A m脙漏dia szabads脙隆g脙隆nak 脙录gye nem csup脙隆n jogszab脙隆lyi k脙漏rd脙漏s, hanem egy eg脙漏szs脙漏ges, demokratikus t脙隆rsadalom m脙漏rc脙漏je is. Magyarorsz脙隆gr脙鲁l j脙露v脙露k. Megtapasztalom, milyen az, amikor a korm脙隆ny r脙隆telepszik a k脙露zm脙漏di脙隆ra, 脙漏s a hirdet脙漏si piac torz脙颅t脙隆s脙隆val befoly脙隆solja a sajt脙鲁t.

Azzal is tiszt脙隆ban vagyok, mivel naponta 脙隆t脙漏lem, hogy milyen az, amikor a hatalom a m脙漏di脙隆t 脙漏s annak munkat脙隆rsait fegyverk脙漏nt haszn脙隆lja, hogy hamis k脙漏rd脙漏sekkel er脜聭ltesse r脙隆nk saj脙隆t narrat脙颅v脙隆j脙隆t. Pontosan ez az, amit ez a szab脙隆lyoz脙隆s nem enged. Ez a rendelet ugyanis arr脙鲁l sz脙鲁l, hogy a k脙露zszolg脙隆lati m脙漏dia ne egyetlen politikai t脙隆bor sz脙鲁cs脙露ve legyen, hanem val脙鲁ban a k脙露zt szolg脙隆lja, 脙漏s t脙隆j脙漏koztasson, teremtsen lehet脜聭s脙漏get a vit脙隆ra. Ez a m脙漏dia feladata. Aki a hatalom birtok脙隆ban van, de f脙漏l a m脙漏dia k脙漏rd脙漏seit脜聭l, az val脙鲁j脙隆ban a saj脙隆t n脙漏p脙漏t脜聭l f脙漏l. 脙聣n abban hiszek, hogy a szabad t脙隆j脙漏koztat脙隆s a nemzet 脙漏ltet脜聭 ereje. Ez脙漏rt t脙隆mogatjuk mi is ezt a jogszab脙隆lyt.

脗聽脗聽Hannes Heide (S&D). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Frau Kommissarin, Frau Ministerin! Ohne freie Medien gibt es keine freie Gesellschaft. Und das Medienfreiheitsgesetz ist ein Meilenstein f脙录r den Schutz von Medienfreiheit, Unabh脙陇ngigkeit und Pluralismus. Es sch脙录tzt Journalistinnen und Journalisten vor politischer Einflussnahme und schafft notwendige Transparenz bei Eigent脙录merstrukturen und Finanzierung von Medien脗聽芒聙聯 damit sch脙录tzt es direkt unsere liberale Demokratie. Und in exakt einem Monat, am 8.脗聽August, sind alle Bestimmungen in den Mitgliedstaaten anzuwenden.

Aber wo stehen wir? In einigen Mitgliedstaaten fehlt jede sichtbare Vorbereitung脗聽芒聙聯 es wird 芒聙聯 teilweise politisch motiviert 芒聙聯 verschleppt und verz脙露gert. Gleichzeitig nimmt der politische Druck zu. Journalistinnen und Journalisten werden mit Spionagesoftware 脙录berwacht, regierungskritische Medien werden finanziell von Regierungen ausgehungert.

Die Kommission darf hier nicht zuschauen, sie muss f脙录r Klarheit sorgen! Das 脙露ffentliche Bewusstsein f脙录r den einen verst脙陇rken, den Druck auf s脙陇umige Mitgliedstaaten erh脙露hen und hart bei Verst脙露脙聼en und Vers脙陇umnissen umgehend handeln. Es darf und kann keine Ausnahmen geben: Medienfreiheit ist kein Verhandlungsgegenstand.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jorge Buxad脙漏 Villalba (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, con su Reglamento Europeo sobre la Libertad de los Medios de Comunicaci脙鲁n nunca quisieron promover medios de comunicaci脙鲁n independientes ni fomentar el periodismo libre: ustedes buscaban crear un entorno medi脙隆tico sumiso, obediente, censurador y limitado, donde solo quepan los relatos de la oligarqu脙颅a dominante, se proh脙颅ba el disenso y los discursos que cuestionen su autoridad y legitimidad, y se someta la verdad al inter脙漏s pol脙颅tico del poderoso. Por eso persiguen las redes sociales: porque ah脙颅 sus medios subvencionados no tienen el control de la informaci脙鲁n.

Comisaria, vamos a hacer un juego: 脗驴qu脙漏 Gobierno persigue a periodistas de investigaci脙鲁n por destapar casos de corrupci脙鲁n de sus ministros?, 脗驴qu脙漏 Gobierno pretende prohibir a periodistas entrar en el Congreso de los Diputados?, 脗驴qu脙漏 presidente del Gobierno hace ruedas de prensa sin periodistas o solo con aquellos que 脙漏l elige?, 脗驴qu脙漏 Gobierno ha batido el r脙漏cord de publicidad institucional a los medios? Pues, mire, el Gobierno socialista de Pedro S脙隆nchez. Lo digo porque la bancada socialista ni siquiera aparece: s脙颅, el l脙颅der de su Internacional Socialista. Y ustedes con la matraca de la extrema derecha, pero 脗驴qu脙漏 extrema derecha? Son ustedes, populares, socialistas y todo el resto, los que no se merecen el respeto del pueblo espa脙卤ol que ans脙颅a una Espa脙卤a nueva y decente.

脗聽脗聽Laurence Farreng (Renew). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Ministre, Madame la Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, le 8脗聽ao脙禄t, le r脙篓glement sur la libert脙漏 des m脙漏dias entrera en vigueur. Cette loi, la premi脙篓re 脙聽 donner un cadre clair pour la structuration de la presse europ脙漏enne et pour la protection des journalistes, est d脙漏sormais un pilier de notre d脙漏mocratie. Pourquoi? Parce qu芒聙聶elle permet 脙聽 la Commission europ脙漏enne d芒聙聶agir de fa脙搂on d脙漏terminante sur la concentration des m脙漏dias. Cela est essentiel, parce que la libert脙漏 d芒聙聶opinion n芒聙聶est pas la libert脙漏 de d脙漏sinformation et que seul le pluralisme garantit la libert脙漏 d芒聙聶opinion.

Voil脙聽 pourquoi il faut emp脙陋cher Viktor脗聽Orban d芒聙聶assujettir au pouvoir l芒聙聶ensemble des m脙漏dias hongrois, de m脙陋me que Vincent脗聽Bollor脙漏, en France, d芒聙聶脙漏tendre ind脙漏finiment son empire m脙漏diatique par la seule force de l芒聙聶argent. Encore une fois, c芒聙聶est la garantie du pluralisme qui doit pr脙漏valoir. Il faut donc une application claire et rapide du r脙篓glement sur la libert脙漏 des m脙漏dias, qui devra 脙陋tre confort脙漏 par le futur bouclier d脙漏mocratique. Et il n芒聙聶y aura pas de bouclier d脙漏mocratique sans volont脙漏 d芒聙聶investir massivement dans nos m脙漏dias europ脙漏ens, ce que nous exigerons dans le futur budget.

脗聽脗聽Nela Riehl (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Was bedeutet es, wenn die Medienfreiheit im Herzen Europas erodiert? Die EU hat sich mit dem EMFA klar der Wahrung der Medienfreiheit verpflichtet. Aber laut Reporter ohne Grenzen stehen Journalistinnen und Journalisten in Deutschland zunehmend unter Beschuss. Sie erleben Angriffe auf offener Stra脙聼e von rechts, sie erleben Zensur, sie erleben politischen Druck und das gerade dann, wenn sie 脙录ber den Genozid in Gaza berichten. Kritische Berichterstattung wird nicht gesch脙录tzt, sondern gezielt verhindert. Erst diese Woche wurde ein Fall von einer Journalistin an mich herangetragen, die versucht hat, einen sehr kritischen Artikel bei sehr etablierten und eigentlich auch f脙录r kritischen Journalismus bekannten Zeitungen zu platzieren. Er wurde 脙录berall abgelehnt bis auf beim Freitag, vermutlich aus Angst vor Gegenreaktionen. Das grenzt an Selbstzensur und das ist h脙露chst alarmierend. Der Bericht der Reporter ohne Grenzen muss bei uns die Alarmglocken l脙陇uten. Medienfreiheit ist nicht nur in der Peripherie bedroht, sie br脙露ckelt im Kern. Die Glaubw脙录rdigkeit der EU h脙陇ngt jetzt davon ab, ob wir bereit sind, bei uns zu Hause die Medienfreiheit zu verteidigen und sonst auch 脙录berall in ganz Europa.

脗聽脗聽Gaetano Pedulla' (The Left). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, in Italia una legge, come sapete, assegna al governo il potere di scegliersi i vertici della TV pubblica. Ecco perch脙漏 per la Rai 脙篓 stato coniato il termine "TeleMeloni".

Sempre in Italia si registra il numero pi脙鹿 alto d'Europa di querele temerarie, e giornalisti scomodi, come Francesco Cancellato e Sigfrido Ranucci, sono spiati o minacciati dallo stesso servizio pubblico.

Sempre in Italia, pochi imperi mediatici controllano quasi tutta l'informazione: Angelucci, un deputato della maggioranza; Caltagirone, favorito dal governo nel risiko bancario; la famiglia Berlusconi, proprietaria di Forza Italia. E sempre la destra tiene in ostaggio e blocca da mesi la commissione parlamentare di vigilanza sulla Rai. I risultati si vedono: nella classifica sulla libert脙聽 di stampa, l'Italia si colloca al 48o脗聽posto, peggio del Gabon e della Macedonia.

Dall'8脗聽agosto prossimo, il Media Freedom Act sar脙聽 integralmente applicabile, ma nessuno 芒聙聯 n脙漏 il governo, n脙漏 i partiti della maggioranza 芒聙聯 ha cominciato a occuparsene. Uno schiaffo alla legge e anche a questo Parlamento europeo per garantire le fake news della propaganda delle destre italiane.

Per questo noi diciamo "Europa svegliati", per questo noi diciamo "basta".

脗聽脗聽Sun脛聧ana Glavak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, po脜隆tovana povjerenice, kolegice i kolege, pa danas zapravo stojimo, rekla bih, pred trenutkom istine za europsku demokraciju. Europski akt o slobodi medija nije samo zakon, on je i alat autoritarnim re脜戮imima. Slobodni mediji nisu ukras politi脛聧kog sistema. I ako lijeva ili krajnje desna ili krajnje lijeva strana nisu zadovoljne, ako smo negdje u sredini, pa onda mora da radimo dobar posao. A sad 脛聡u re脛聡i i za脜隆to. Ako te vrijednosti ne脜隆to zna脛聧e, onda EMFA nije opcija, ve脛聡 nu脜戮nost. Dolazim iz Hrvatske i 脜戮elim se samo referirati 脜隆to se doga脛聭a trenutno u mojoj zemlji. Kre脛聡e u javno savjetovanje i prijedlog zakona koji 脛聡e omogu脛聡iti implementaciju Europskog akta o slobodi medija. Kolegice i kolege, za脜隆titimo novinare, za脜隆titimo njihovu neovisnost i pru脜戮imo im i onu financijsku neovisnost.

脗聽脗聽Katarina Barley (S&D). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! 芒聙聻Demokratie stirbt in der Dunkelheit芒聙聹 hat der US芒聙聭Journalist und Pulitzerpreistr脙陇ger Bob Woodward gesagt. Das Licht, das die Demokratie sch脙录tzt, das sind die freien Medien. Ohne Journalistinnen und Journalisten und unabh脙陇ngige Journalistinnen und Journalisten gibt es keine wirksame Kontrolle von Politik und Wirtschaft. Ohne sie verschwimmt die Grenze zwischen Fakt und Fake bis zur Unkenntlichkeit. Vor allem in Zeiten, in denen viele glauben, dass man Informationen ja gratis bekommen kann. Bei jungen Leuten sind TikTok und YouTube die wichtigsten Informationskan脙陇le geworden, wo man nat脙录rlich etwas bekommt. Ob man das 脙录berhaupt noch Information nennen kann, das steht auf einem anderen Blatt.

Der European Media Freedom Act ist ein gro脙聼er Schritt zu mehr Transparenz und mehr Unabh脙陇ngigkeit. Ganz besonders stolz bin ich als Mitglied des Pegasus芒聙聭Untersuchungsausschusses darauf, dass wir es geschafft haben, auch den Schutz vor Sp脙陇hsoftware wie Pegasus darin zu verankern. Heute in einem Monat tritt das Medienfreiheitsgesetz endlich in Kraft. Es kommt darauf an, dass die Mitgliedstaaten es anwenden und die Kommission bei Verst脙露脙聼en hart durchgreift. Denn noch nie war eine freie, unabh脙陇ngige Presse so wichtig wie heute.

脗聽脗聽Irena Joveva (Renew). 芒聙聯 Gospod predsednik!脗聽Evropski akt o svobodi medijev je eden najve脛聧jih na脜隆ih dose脜戮kov iz prej脜隆njega mandata. A te脜戮ava je, kot je to pogosto, da se zakon ne izvr脜隆uje tako, kot bi se moral. 脜聽e to脛聧no mesec je do dneva, ko zakon v polnosti stopi v veljavo, a se dr脜戮ave 脛聧lanice prepo脛聧asi pripravljajo na to.

Zato vas pristojne v Komisiji pozivam, nemudoma spro脜戮ite postopke pred Sodi脜隆脛聧em in uveljavite zakon. Ker 脜戮e od sprejetja je medijska svoboda marsikje po Uniji pravzaprav nazadovala, merim predvsem na Slova脜隆ko, mediji pa niso imuni pred napadi oblasti nikjer. Pri svobodi medijev je enako kot pri katerikoli svobodi. Ko jo zadu脜隆i脜隆, jo te脜戮ko vzpostavi脜隆 nazaj. In pomena svobode se zave脜隆 脜隆ele, ko jo izgubi脜隆.

V besedilu zakona je ogromno pomembnih 脛聧lenov, ki omogo脛聧ajo za脜隆脛聧ito medijev pred nedopustnimi dr脜戮avnimi pritiski, zasebnimi interesi ali tehnolo脜隆kimi velikani. Manjka le pogum, predvsem pogum Komisije, da se tem pritiskom zoperstavi.

脗聽脗聽David Cormand (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, les menaces contre la libert脙漏 de la presse s芒聙聶accumulent. C芒聙聶est d芒聙聶ailleurs pourquoi nous avions mis en place ce r脙篓glement europ脙漏en sur la libert脙漏 des m脙漏dias.

L芒聙聶ind脙漏pendance de la presse est remise en question, certes, par des r脙漏gimes illib脙漏raux qui la musellent 脙聽 l芒聙聶int脙漏rieur m脙陋me de l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne. Elle est mise en danger par des oligarques 脙漏trangers 脙聽 l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne, qui essaient de prendre le contr脙麓le de m脙漏dias europ脙漏ens. Elle est aussi menac脙漏e par des milliardaires d芒聙聶extr脙陋me droite, en Europe 芒聙聯脗聽comme dans mon pays, la France, avec le groupe Bollor脙漏. C芒聙聶est pourquoi, d芒聙聶ailleurs, les services publics de m脙漏dias sont une arme absolue pour lutter contre ce danger.

En France, encore, h脙漏las, on a une ministre de la Culture qui veut d脙漏sormais cr脙漏er une grande holding des m脙漏dias publics pour pouvoir mieux les contr脙麓ler. Je demande que l芒聙聶article脗聽5, qui pr脙漏voit l芒聙聶ind脙漏pendance des m脙漏dias publics, soit r脙漏ellement mis en 脜聯uvre pour que cette menace cesse de s芒聙聶脙漏tendre dans nos pays et que nous puissions compter sur des m脙漏dias publics de qualit脙漏 qui informent nos concitoyennes et nos concitoyens.

脗聽脗聽Bogdan Andrzej Zdrojewski (PPE). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Obserwuj脛聶 t脛聶 debat脛聶 i musz脛聶 powiedzie脛聡, 脜录e najbardziej krytyczni wobec tego aktu s脛聟 ci, kt脙鲁rzy obawiaj脛聟 si脛聶 wp脜聜yw脙鲁w obcych i wrogich Europie mocarstw, je脜录eli chodzi o rozpowszechnianie w脜聜asnej propagandy. Natomiast co jest najistotniejsze? Ot脙鲁脜录 wolno脜聸脛聡 medi脙鲁w, musimy pami脛聶ta脛聡, to nie tylko prawo, to nasza wsp脙鲁lna odpowiedzialno脜聸脛聡. Kluczowe jest promowanie szacunku dla r脙鲁脜录norodnych pogl脛聟d脙鲁w, rozwoju 脜聸wiadomo脜聸ci obywatelskiej i takiej edukacji medialnej, kt脙鲁ra pozwoli odbiorcom lepiej weryfikowa脛聡 脜潞r脙鲁d脜聜a informacji.

W mojej ocenie europejski akt o wolno脜聸ci medi脙鲁w wyra脜录a jednoznaczne stanowisko. Wolno脜聸脛聡 s脜聜owa jest fundamentem demokracji. Nie jest przywilejem, lecz obowi脛聟zkiem. Pa脜聞stwa cz脜聜onkowskie maj脛聟 obowi脛聟zek zapewni脛聡, aby dostawcy publiczni us脜聜ug medialnych byli niezale脜录ni pod wzgl脛聶dem redakcyjnym i funkcjonalnym oraz aby zapewniali swoim odbiorcom pluralizm informacji i opinii. Z nadziej脛聟 patrz脛聶 na powo脜聜anie tego nowego unijnego organu, Europejskiej Rady Us脜聜ug Medialnych, cho脛聡 oczywi脜聸cie nie bez pewnego krytycyzmu. Pami脛聶tam rozmaite do脜聸wiadczenia w tej materii, kt脙鲁re nie zawsze ko脜聞czy脜聜y si脛聶 dobrze.

Pami脛聶tajmy, co jest w tym akcie: ochrona niezale脜录no脜聸ci redakcyjnej, niestosowanie oprogramowania szpiegowskiego wobec medi脙鲁w, przejrzysto脜聸脛聡 reklam, ochrona tre脜聸ci w Internecie, szacunek wobec prawa autorskiego. Jestem przekonany, 脜录e jeste脜聸my w stanie tym aktem dokona脛聡 pewnego post脛聶pu.

脗聽脗聽Joanna Scheuring-Wielgus (S&D). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Pani Komisarz! Przys脜聜uchiwa脜聜am si脛聶 tej debacie i temu, co m脙鲁wi prawa strona. I w zasadzie te ich krzyki i nawo脜聜ywania do tego, 脜录e ten akt im si脛聶 nie podoba, 脜聸wiadcz脛聟 tylko i wy脜聜脛聟cznie o tym, 脜录e dobrze, 脜录e ten akt w og脙鲁le powsta脜聜.

Ale Pani Komisarz, w ostatnim raporcie organizacji Libert脙漏 czytamy, 脜录e bardzo du脜录o kraj脙鲁w, w tym m脙鲁j kraj, Polska, nie przygotowa脜聜o si脛聶 do wdro脜录enia tego aktu. I uwa脜录am, 脜录e w swojej dzia脜聜alno脜聸ci Komisja musi doprowadzi脛聡 do implementacji i do wymagania od pa脜聞stw narodowych w Unii Europejskiej tego, 脜录eby ten akt by脜聜 wdro脜录ony tak, jak nale脜录y.

Bardzo cz脛聶sto powtarzamy tutaj takie has脜聜a jak wolno脜聸脛聡 s脜聜owa, wolno脜聸脛聡 informacji, m脙鲁wimy o prawdzie, m脙鲁wimy o faktach. Naszym obowi脛聟zkiem jako parlamentarzyst脙鲁w, ale te脜录 obowi脛聟zkiem Komisji jest zadba脛聡 o to, aby ten akt by脜聜 wdro脜录ony, ale r脙鲁wnie脜录 脜录eby脜聸my ju脜录 my脜聸leli o tym, jak sobie poradzi脛聡 z t脛聟 dezinformacj脛聟 i manipulacj脛聟, kt脙鲁ra jest w przestrzeni cyfrowej.

脗聽脗聽Pascale Piera (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, je me permets de rappeler que le r脙篓glement dont on parle sur la pr脙漏tendue libert脙漏 des m脙漏dias est en r脙漏alit脙漏 l芒聙聶un des piliers de la politique liberticide de l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne. En s芒聙聶imposant comme unique organe de r脙漏gulation en mati脙篓re de contr脙麓le des m脙漏dias, l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne se dote d芒聙聶un outil dangereux, qui muselle les opinions qui la d脙漏rangent, comme en Hongrie ou en Roumanie. Ce syst脙篓me orwellien viole tous les principes de libert脙漏, fondement de nos soci脙漏t脙漏s d脙漏mocratiques: la libert脙漏 d芒聙聶expression, la libert脙漏 de la presse, la pluralit脙漏 d芒聙聶opinion. Dans toutes les soci脙漏t脙漏s v脙漏ritablement lib脙漏rales, le seul r脙漏gulateur d芒聙聶un contenu m脙漏diatique ne peut 脙陋tre que le lecteur, et certainement pas les technocrates censeurs de Bruxelles.

Je crois en fait que vous avez peur. Votre obsession d芒聙聶instaurer votre v脙漏rit脙漏 dans le d脙漏bat public a pour objectif unique de cr脙漏er une machine 脙聽 penser europ脙漏iste et mondialiste. 脙聙 ce titre, votre comit脙漏 europ脙漏en pour les services de m脙漏dias, pilot脙漏 par la Commission, est en r脙漏alit脙漏 un v脙漏ritable minist脙篓re de la propagande. Face 脙聽 ce consensus liberticide d脙漏fendu par la gauche, par le centre, par la droite, nous, nous d脙漏fendrons toujours la libert脙漏.

(L'oratrice accepte une question carton bleu)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 In accordance with Rule 178(10), the last sentence of the Rules of Procedure, I would also ask for your permission to react to the answer that I will get from my colleague.

Thank you, colleague, for accepting the blue card 芒聙聯 you talked a lot about the dangers of one unified administrative body that regulates the media and the press. My question would be, do you think that media and press should be fully free without any oversight, being able to also spread lies and disinformation? And if not, what body can control this? Where can we put a safeguard in place?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pascale Piera (PfE), r脙漏ponse carton bleu. 芒聙聯 Cher coll脙篓gue, merci pour votre question. Je n芒聙聶ai qu芒聙聶une seule r脙漏ponse 脙聽 vous faire: le seul r脙漏gulateur, c芒聙聶est le lecteur. Et voyez-vous, en France comme ailleurs, les m脙漏dias traditionnels, s芒聙聶ils n芒聙聶avaient pas de subventions, ne vivraient pas. Pourquoi? Parce que leur contenu, qui est uniforme et totalement orient脙漏 politiquement, n芒聙聶int脙漏resse plus les lecteurs. C芒聙聶est la raison pour laquelle ce sont les m脙漏dias qui sont sur Internet, les m脙漏dias alternatifs par exemple, qui aujourd芒聙聶hui captent cette audience, et c芒聙聶est tr脙篓s heureux.

Je vous sugg脙篓re de relire le livre de George Orwell脗聽1984, dans lequel se trouve le minist脙篓re de la V脙漏rit脙漏.

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Ms脗聽Piera, Mr脗聽Lukas is using a point of order, and he can ask a following question.

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 No point of order. No.

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Yes, but according to the Rules, you can continue your second question.

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 It's not a point of order; it's a reaction.

Thank you, dear colleague. I can just tell you two things when you talk about the fact that the only corrective that is needed is the reader 芒聙聯 I advise you to research a little bit about the effect that the state-controlled media of the communist German Democratic Republic had or, on the other hand, the state-controlled media of the Nazi regime in Germany. I can tell you from my national experience, the idea that the only corrective is the reader is very, very na脙炉ve and you should think about this again.

脗聽脗聽Pascale Piera (PfE). 芒聙聯 Merci pour vos suggestions, mon cher coll脙篓gue, merci. Je vous signale que je suis une sp脙漏cialiste de la libert脙漏 d'opinion sous le r脙漏gime sovi脙漏tique. Je pourrais vous en parler des heures, et, de ce point de vue l脙聽, ce n'est pas le lecteur qui faisait le m脙漏dia. Heureusement qu'il y avait des gens comme Soljenitsyne, qui 脙漏taient cach脙漏s, et qui 脙漏crivaient, et qui disaient la v脙漏rit脙漏, et qui trouvaient leur lectorat.

脗聽脗聽Milan Zver (PPE). 芒聙聯 Gospod predsednik!脗聽Akt o svobodi medijev se je torej za脛聧el izvajati. 脛聦e nepristransko pogledamo, da ga ne bomo samo hvalili ali kritizirali, lahko re脛聧emo, da prina脜隆a tudi nekaj pozitivnega. Mislim zlasti na ve脛聧ji lastni脜隆ki preglednosti, ki je predvsem problem v nekaterih postkomunisti脛聧nih dr脜戮avah, kjer so si stare biv脜隆e strukture zagotovile odlo脛聧ilen vpliv tudi v medijih in ga 脜隆e imajo.

Po drugi strani pa si je s tem aktom politi脛聧na levica na neki na脛聧in kar v precej脜隆nji meri uzurpirala nadzor nad mainstream mediji in pa tudi spodbujanje cenzure, kar je v nasprotju z na脛聧elom svobode govora, na katerem temelji na脜隆a civilizacija. Mimogrede, vesel sem, da se Musk in Zuckerberg na svojih platformah odpovedujeta cenzuri.

Prepre脛聧iti moramo, da se akt o svobodi medijev ne bo politi脛聧no zlorabljal, kot se je v Sloveniji. Spomnimo, tu je slovenska levica z aktivno podporo biv脜隆e komisarke lomastila po medijski svobodi. Kultura, katere sestavni del so mediji, potrebujejo svobodo, ne regulacije ne cenzure ne nadzora. Nenazadnje, kdo bo nadziral nadzornike? To je klju脛聧no vpra脜隆anje.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, I want to start by saying to the far right here in our Parliament that free and independent media doesn't mean attacking them when it doesn't fit your ideology.

Actually, your ideology is an ideology of hatred. We have laws in our Union to ensure you don't import 1930s Germany to 2025. So stop attacking our democracies and go back to the 1930s and stay there, where your ideology belongs.

For us who believe in fully fledged democracies, we have a duty to defend free and independent media. We have a duty to strengthen them. It is every Member State's duty to implement the Media Freedom Act 芒聙聯 not if, but when, and the time is now.

脗聽脗聽Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, manipular, impor, silenciar e vulnerar direitos n脙拢o acontece s脙鲁 na Hungria, mas tamb脙漏m no meu pa脙颅s: a Galiza.

脗聽脗聽Volker Schnurrbusch (ESN). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, meine Damen und Herren! Der European Media Freedom Act ist ein trojanisches Pferd, eine Mogelpackung. 脗聽脗聽Vicent Marz脙聽 Ib脙隆脙卤ez (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, va a tener mucho trabajo si de verdad quiere que se implemente este Reglamento Europeo sobre la Libertad de los Medios de Comunicaci脙鲁n, entre otros, en mi pa脙颅s, en el Pa脙颅s Valenci脙聽.

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, la libertad de expresi脙鲁n y la libertad de difundir y recibir informaci脙鲁n veraz son derechos fundamentales consagrados en el art脙颅culo脗聽11 de脗聽la Carta de los Derechos Fundamentales de la Uni脙鲁n Europea. La libertad de expresi脙鲁n es el derecho del p脙潞blico a saber y el derecho a decir lo que alg脙潞n poderoso no quiere o脙颅r. Pero estas libertades, que tienen como corolario el pluralismo informativo, afectan tambi脙漏n a los medios de comunicaci脙鲁n. Y, por eso, el Reglamento Europeo sobre la Libertad de los Medios de Comunicaci脙鲁n exige a los medios de comunicaci脙鲁n daci脙鲁n de cuentas y transparencia de su titularidad, de su financiaci脙鲁n y de su publicidad institucional, exactamente por el derecho del p脙潞blico a saber.

La Uni脙鲁n Europea permanece as脙颅 fiel al est脙隆ndar m脙隆s alto del mundo, que se complementa con esa intervenci脙鲁n, a trav脙漏s del Reglamento de Servicios Digitales, sobre el modelo de negocio de las plataformas digitales, exactamente para proteger el derecho de la ciudadan脙颅a europea a saber qu脙漏 es lo que hay detr脙隆s tambi脙漏n de los medios de comunicaci脙鲁n, c脙鲁mo se financian y, por tanto, cu脙隆l es la intervenci脙鲁n de los poderes privados en los medios de comunicaci脙鲁n.

脗聽脗聽Maria Lu脙颅s Albuquerque, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, I believe that this debate has shown how crucial it is that media companies are supported and media freedom is protected in our Union. Together, we are in the right place to properly enforce the newest tool in our toolbox: the European Media Freedom Act.

In the period leading up to 8脗聽August, the Commission will continue to encourage Member States to intensify their efforts to facilitate the timely adoption of the necessary measures where needed. The European Media Freedom Act is a landmark legal instrument supporting the internal market for media services, while also protecting media freedom and editorial independence.

The act has a task no less than addressing pressing challenges identified in today's debate, while standing as a clear example of the EU's unwavering resolve to strengthening democratic values and upholding fundamental rights.

Rakstiski pazi脜聠ojumi (178. pants)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, Commissioners, thank you for putting this important issue on the agenda. As Sabine Verheyen from the EPP started saying, law on paper is not enough 芒聙聯 it needs to be implemented. We agree about that; European legislation has to be implemented.脗聽I noticed some concern about the legislation, but I also noted a lot of support, for instance from Zolt脙隆n Tarr, from the EPP, and from many others.

Now the legislation is adopted and now we need to implement it. I hope that today's exchange can help the relevant, competent authorities of the Member States in the implementation of the regulation. We also trust that the Commission will continue to help and guide the Member States in its implementation. From our side, the Danish Presidency will continue facilitating exchange and sharing of information and good practices. In this context, I will also mention that my colleague, the Danish Minister of Culture, has proposed draft Council conclusions to follow up on access to reliable news.

脗聽脗聽Joachim Stanis脜聜aw Brudzi脜聞ski (ECR), na pi脜聸mie. 芒聙聯 Od czasu, gdy w Polsce zacz脛聟脜聜 rz脛聟dzi脛聡 ponownie Donald Tusk, nie mo脜录emy ju脜录 m脙鲁wi脛聡 o wolno脜聸ci medi脙鲁w. No, chyba 脜录e t脛聶 wolno脜聸脛聡 脜聜amie ich partyjny kolega, to wtedy najwidoczniej dla brukselskiego establishmentu wszystko jest w porz脛聟dku. Tak jak premier Tusk, tak Komisja Europejska coraz bardziej chce kontrolowa脛聡 to co si脛聶 m脙鲁wi w mediach, a prawo do wolnej wypowiedzi i informacji jest coraz bardziej zagro脜录one. Coraz 脜聜atwiej jest komu脜聸 zarzuci脛聡, 脜录e pos脜聜uguje si脛聶 na przyk脜聜ad tzw. mow脛聟 nienawi脜聸ci. I znowu walka z t脛聟 mow脛聟 nienawi脜聸ci ma by脛聡 pretekstem do cenzury na coraz szersz脛聟 skal脛聶: w internecie, w telewizji czy w prasie. Prosz脛聶 pa脜聞stwa 芒聙聯 bro脜聞my wolno脜聸ci medi脙鲁w 芒聙聯 ale nie tak wybi脙鲁rczo jak w chwili obecnej!

脗聽脗聽Vasile D脙庐ncu (S&D), 脙庐n scris. 芒聙聯 Libertatea presei este coloana vertebral脛聝 a unei democra脠聸ii func脠聸ionale. European Media Freedom Act nu este doar un instrument legislativ, ci o garan脠聸ie a pluralismului, a transparen脠聸ei 脠聶i a protec脠聸iei jurnalismului independent 脙庐n fa脠聸a ingerin脠聸elor politice 脠聶i economice.

Din p脛聝cate, implementarea acestui act 脙庐n statele membre este inegal脛聝 脠聶i, 脙庐n unele cazuri, blocat脛聝. Vedem cum persist脛聝 concentrarea excesiv脛聝 a propriet脛聝脠聸ii media, intimidarea jurnali脠聶tilor, presiunile comerciale asupra redac脠聸iilor locale, dar 脠聶i lipsa de transparen脠聸脛聝 a algoritmilor care decid vizibilitatea con脠聸inutului 脙庐n online.

Fac un apel ferm c脛聝tre Comisie: s脛聝 instituie un mecanism de monitorizare clar, public 脠聶i periodic privind aplicarea acestui act 脙庐n fiecare stat membru, s脛聝 condi脠聸ioneze finan脠聸area european脛聝 pentru digitalizare 脠聶i comunicare de respectarea real脛聝 a libert脛聝脠聸ii presei 脠聶i s脛聝 intervin脛聝 acolo unde autorit脛聝脠聸ile na脠聸ionale e脠聶ueaz脛聝 脙庐n protejarea presei libere.

Nu putem ap脛聝ra democra脠聸ia f脛聝r脛聝 s脛聝 ap脛聝r脛聝m jurnali脠聶tii care o sus脠聸in prin munc脛聝 zilnic脛聝, adesea 脙庐n condi脠聸ii ostile.

脗聽

10. Need for the EU to scale up clean technologies (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Priek脜隆s脛聯d脛聯t脛聛js. 芒聙聯 N脛聛kamais darba k脛聛rt脛芦bas punkts ir debates par Komisijas pazi脜聠ojumu par nepiecie脜隆am脛芦bu ES v脛聯rien脛芦g脛聛k izv脛聯rst t脛芦ras tehnolo脛拢ijas (2025/2787(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dan J脙赂rgensen, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, it is an honour to discuss with you the need to scale up clean technologies in Europe. In times of energy transition, ever-mounting geopolitical tensions and fierce global competition, Europe must do its utmost to keep agency over its industrial destiny. This is our firm belief.

It is a sovereignty issue, but it is also a business issue. And the good news is that today the EU is a global leader and a trendsetter in these technologies. We must capitalise on this competitive edge, but to succeed we must go faster and act more boldly.

To clean tech project promoters, innovators and investors, I say: choosing Europe means access to a strong industrial base, a unique talent pool and a political commitment to climate neutrality. It's also accessing our single market. But as you know, some of Europe's clean tech value chains are under immense pressure due to unfair global competition. If we don't act decisively, we risk becoming mere assemblers, if not simply just buyers. If we want to have cars made in Europe that are electric and with own cost-competitive production of batteries and components, we need to take the next steps.

This is precisely the aim of the clean industrial deal. It brings together legislation, financing and simplification 芒聙聯 not as abstract goals, but as tools for delivery, because time is of the essence.

Just last week, the Commission announced EUR脗聽850脗聽million of funding dedicated to six projects to boost the EU's battery manufacturing industry. We also need to simplify the way we provide investment support from research and development to commercialisation. And we need to create real demand for EU-made batteries, solar panels and wind turbines.

The implementation of the Net-Zero Industry Act and the Critical Raw Materials Act is in full swing. Across the EU, single points of contacts are being set up to fast track permitting and to coordinate net-zero strategic projects. The concept of net-zero acceleration valleys is a prime example of how the Net-Zero Industry Act is already being translated into concrete action at a regional and local level, where it matters the most.

At the same time, the Net-Zero Industry Act's non-price criteria 芒聙聯 essential to biodiversity and avoid excessive import dependence 芒聙聯 will become mandatory from 1脗聽January脗聽2026. We are actively supporting national authorities to apply these rules consistently. The early signals are encouraging in some sectors, like wind. Chinese firms have started sourcing EU-made components, driving demand and production here in Europe.

With the forthcoming Industrial Decarbonisation Accelerator Act, the provision of public procurement to follow, and greening corporate fleets, we will go a step further, creating lead markets for decarbonisation and competitive EU industries. Furthermore, we will step up our game in the use of trade defence instruments such as anti-dumping, anti-subsidy or safeguard measures.

Now, of course, beyond the sectoral considerations, cost competitiveness, financing, and public and private investments remain an overarching challenge, which we must address forcefully. At EU level, we are mobilising a toolbox, for example, the Innovation Fund, InvestEU and Step. Besides, the new Clean Industrial State Aid Framework gives Member States more flexibility. In the next MFF, the European Competitiveness Fund, tightly connected to the framework research programme, will strengthen the EU's strategic investment capacity.

But public funding alone is not enough. We must also unlock private investments at scale. That is why we are advancing the savings and investments union.

Finally, simplification: with a series of omnibuses, the Commission is focused on creating an enabling regulatory environment, ensuring that Europe is the best place to invest and produce. If Europe wants to lead, if we want to lead the clean tech transition and not just import, we must act now and we must act together. The tools are there. More is coming. The time for delivery is now, and I'm counting on your commitment to help us all achieve this.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ra脙潞l de la Hoz Quintano, en nombre del Grupo PPE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or comisario, es evidente, estamos todos de acuerdo en que Europa necesita acelerar la implantaci脙鲁n de tecnolog脙颅as limpias, un proceso que es absolutamente necesario para la consecuci脙鲁n de los objetivos que nos hemos marcado en esta legislatura: competitividad industrial, soberan脙颅a energ脙漏tica y, por supuesto, la consecuci脙鲁n de los objetivos clim脙隆ticos.

Pero, para abordar este proceso, adem脙隆s de muy buenas palabras, de muy buenas intenciones 芒聙聰es evidente y usted lo ha dicho芒聙聰, hace falta pragmatismo, hace falta determinaci脙鲁n y, sobre todo, ambici脙鲁n. Y no solo eso, hace falta tambi脙漏n dejar de lado los viejos clich脙漏s dogm脙隆ticos y algunos prejuicios ideol脙鲁gicos que han demostrado ser tan perjudiciales en el pasado. Adem脙隆s, hay que decirlo claramente y, en consecuencia, actuar.

La transici脙鲁n energ脙漏tica en Europa es imposible sin la energ脙颅a nuclear. Si queremos de verdad una soberan脙颅a energ脙漏tica en Europa, necesitamos la energ脙颅a nuclear. Si queremos de verdad precios asequibles en la Uni脙鲁n Europea, necesitamos la energ脙颅a nuclear. Necesitamos, por tanto, que la energ脙颅a nuclear sea tratada en condiciones de igualdad con las energ脙颅as renovables. Necesitamos que desaparezcan de la legislaci脙鲁n europea los sesgos contrarios a la energ脙颅a nuclear que permanecen en este momento.

Tenemos el ejemplo de Alemania, de su error y de su rectificaci脙鲁n posterior. Actuemos en esa direcci脙鲁n. Atendamos la carta que doce Gobiernos europeos han hecho a la Comisi脙鲁n recientemente, en la que se le pide脗聽avanzar en el desarrollo de la energ脙颅a nuclear, y ay脙潞dennos 芒聙聰concluyo con esto芒聙聰 a impedir que el Gobierno de mi pa脙颅s, contra los tiempos y contra toda l脙鲁gica econ脙鲁mica, social y medioambiental, siga adelante con su plan de desmantelamiento de las centrales nucleares que existen en nuestro pa脙颅s, 脙潞nica y exclusivamente por capricho...

(el presidente retira la palabra al orador)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mohammed Chahim, namens de S&D-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, toen eind 19e eeuw het nieuwe Philips naar een nieuwe thuisbasis zocht, kwamen ze in Helmond terecht. Een stad, mijn stad, met heel veel economische activiteit. Het innovatieve Philips stond te popelen om hun gloeilampen vanuit Helmond aan de man te brengen.

Er was echter een probleem: de gevestigde bedrijven in Helmond zagen Philips niet zitten. Zij overtuigden het stadsbestuur dat zo芒聙聵n nieuwkomer alleen maar onrust zou veroorzaken. Ze beloofden zekerheid, werkgelegenheid en wilden geen verandering. En Philips? Die vertrok naar Eindhoven.

Bijna tachtig jaar later kreeg het stadsbestuur een keiharde reality-check. De industrie in Helmond was grotendeels vertrokken, terwijl Philips in Eindhoven maar bleef groeien en uitbreiden. Om de Helmondse werkloosheidscrisis tegen te gaan, vroeg het stadsbestuur Philips alsnog een fabriek te openen in Helmond. En de dominante industrie uit het verleden? Daar blijft maar weinig van over. Die lieten de stad en heel veel Helmonders achter. Helmond had helaas de boot gemist en eigenlijk is er niks zo speculatiefs als denken dat de grote spelers van vandaag er over vijftig jaar weer zijn. De geschiedenis bewijst dat keer op keer.

Dus laten wij als Europa die fout voorkomen, zodat we niet jaren later er alsnog alles aan moeten doen om de bedrijven hiernaartoe te halen. Dat kan nu. Door ons te richten op de toekomst en niet op bepaalde of enkele belangen. Dat betekent een lagere energierekening, de deuren niet langer openhouden voor fossiel, betere investeringscapaciteit, maar vooral een blik op de toekomst. Een blik naar voren.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Knotek, za skupinu PfE. 芒聙聯 Pane p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, pane komisa脜聶i, opakujete stejn脙漏 chyby, stanovuje nere脙隆ln脙漏 c脙颅le. Sna脜戮脙颅te se lidem jako jedinou mo脜戮nost vnutit drah脙漏 a n脛聸kdy nespolehliv脙漏 脛聧ist脙漏 technologie a v脜炉bec v脙隆s nezaj脙颅m脙隆, 脜戮e o n脛聸 zna脛聧n脙隆 脛聧脙隆st ve脜聶ejnosti nem脙隆 z脙隆jem. M脙颅sto naslouch脙隆n脙颅 ve脜聶ejnosti rad脛聸ji regulujete, zdra脜戮ujete anebo zakazujete.

A j脙隆 V脙隆m n脛聸co pov脙颅m, pane komisa脜聶i, zdra脜戮en脙颅m star脙陆ch, 脛聧asto fosiln脙颅ch technologi脙颅 sice urychl脙颅te jejich konec, ale rozhodn脛聸 t脙颅m nezajist脙颅te odpov脙颅daj脙颅c脙颅 n脙隆r脜炉st pod脙颅lu 脛聧ist脙陆ch technologi脙颅. V脙陆roba v脛聧etn脛聸 emis脙颅 se p脜聶esune mimo Evropu, Evropan脙漏 je脜隆t脛聸 v脙颅ce zchudnou a klima t脙颅m nezachr脙隆n脙颅te. Tak脜戮e se, pros脙颅m, pane komisa脜聶i a cel脙隆 va脜隆e Komise probu脛聫te a p脜聶esta脜聢te opakovat chyby, dokud je je脜隆t脛聸 脛聧as.
