脗聽脗聽Dariusz Jo脜聞ski (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Panie Komisarzu! Na pocz脛聟tku chc脛聶 bardzo gor脛聟co podzi脛聶kowa脛聡 za t脛聶 debat脛聶. Dyskusja o strategicznej bran脜录y, jak脛聟 jest bran脜录a chemiczna, i przedstawienie przez Pana Komisarza pakietu chemicznego dotyczy r脙鲁wnie脜录 mojego kraju, w kt脙鲁rym w bran脜录y chemicznej zatrudnionych jest 341 tysi脛聶cy os脙鲁b wypracowuj脛聟cych 10% PKB. Ale warto tu wspomnie脛聡, 脜录e jeszcze 20 lat temu Europa w przemy脜聸le chemicznym by脜聜a kilkakrotnie silniejsza od Chin, mniej wi脛聶cej w proporcjach 3:1. A dzisiaj te proporcje si脛聶 odwr脙鲁ci脜聜y.

Dlaczego? Przede wszystkim z powodu cen pr脛聟du. I co脜聸 si脛聶 musi zmieni脛聡. By脜聜em ostatnio na kongresie polskiej chemii w Krakowie, gdzie polskie firmy przedstawi脜聜y manifest polskiej chemii dok脜聜adnie wyszczeg脙鲁lniaj脛聟cy aspekty, kt脙鲁re musz脛聟 si脛聶 zmieni脛聡, a wi脛聶c ceny energii, uproszczenie, modernizacja zak脜聜ad脙鲁w.

Ciesz脛聶 si脛聶, 脜录e w pakiecie przedstawionym przez Pana Komisarza to si脛聶 znajduje. Chc脛聶 jeszcze doda脛聡, Panie Komisarzu, 脜录e nadszed脜聜 czas na dzia脜聜anie, i jest tutaj pe脜聜ne wsparcie polskiej delegacji, bo dla nas miejsca pracy i przedsi脛聶biorstwa konkurencyjne wzgl脛聶dem Chin, Indii czy Azji to jest to, o co te脜录 tutaj walczymy w Europie.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Europe's chemical industry employs over 1.2脗聽million脗聽people. But how many small companies have already given up because the EU makes them report more than they produce?

Simplification is promised, but too often we see just more rules, not less burden. A kind reminder for the Commission: you are not the ultimate master of every sector. Different regions need different solutions. What suits big players in central Europe may harm small companies in the north. We must protect health and the environment, but we must also trust the Member States to know what works at home.

Real simplification means flexibility, national responsibility and space to breathe. Without it, we don't just lose competitiveness, we lose trust in our European system.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, as we now come to the end of today's discussion, let me first start by saying thank you for your inputs and intervention.

I would like to start, as Vice-President S脙漏journ脙漏 said, in the beginning. Our chemical sector in our Member States are facing both real challenges and also opportunities, and this is exactly the balance that we have tried to strike in the action plan. We all see the pressure from economic uncertainty and global shifts, but at the same time, there is a growing demand for clean, sustainable and innovative solutions. And this is not only a societal demand: companies are themselves driving innovation and change. Many have today mentioned competitiveness and it has also been mentioned, of course, strong protection for health and the environment. These two things must go hand in hand and by doing so, we not only protect people and the planet, but also give our companies a competitive edge, enabling them to lead the consumer in trust, innovation and for sustainable solutions. We also heard strong calls 芒聙聯 both here and in our different dialogues that we have脗聽芒聙聯脗聽asking for boosting investment in European critical production, ensuring access to affordable energy, accelerating decarbonisation and making sure global trade rules are fair and properly enforced. And these are all priorities that we are addressing in the chemical action plan, and they will remain central as we move ahead with implementation and shape future policy to support both industry and environment and public health.

I want to make clear that the Commission is fully committed to helping the chemical sector navigate this transition that is important for all industries because it's about the whole value chain. And the European Green Deal and the Clean Industrial Deal remain our guiding framework, but now we are shifting from setting goals to delivering results through investments, incentives and action. And the action plan is a key tool to support chemical industry that is modern, competitive and sustainable, but also it is our aim to provide predictability, clarity, fairness, so that businesses can invest and grow while keeping Europe's high standards.

There was a lot of questions about labelling. It is important to note that the changes do not affect the core principles, such as the readability and legibility of labels, but help avoiding disproportionate burden that does not bring any tangible improvement corresponding to the cost. They aim also at bringing safer products quicker to the market, and ensure that companies can adapt to important changes affecting their products under clear procedures and timelines, and do not have to fulfil redundant administrative obligations.

There were also questions about cosmetics products. Of course consumer protection and safety remains an absolute priority for the Commission, and companies will still need to provide strong scientific evidence showing that the use of the CMR substances is safe and meets strict legal conditions. The changes respond to the long-standing feedback from businesses and Member State authorities facing practical and legal uncertainties under the current rules.

And also some of you and also Vice-President S脙漏journ脙漏 mentioned PFAS, and I will just add that this is a big concern for the citizens and for us also. PFAS is everywhere in the environment and in our waters. PFAS is in our bodies, as a recent Dutch study shows. And here we are working with two working streams, consumer and industry. And when it comes to the consumer side, we want to ban PFAS in consumer products such as clothing and kitchen equipment. And ECHA is working on this matter and we are urging them to speed up.

When it comes to industrial applications, PFAS are present in many important and strategic products and processes, for example, some medical devices and in semiconductors. Sometimes, there are safer alternatives available, but sometimes there are not. We want to encourage companies to continue to develop alternatives, while recognising that in some cases, there are not yet good alternatives to PFAS. In these cases, it is important to ensure that PFAS are handled in a way which is safe to employees and to the environment.

To conclude, the Commission is committed to continue the work in close cooperation with you to find practical, balanced solutions that benefit industry, citizens and the environment, so thank you very much and I'm looking forward to working together in the months ahead.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 La discussione 脙篓 chiusa.

脗聽

14. Revision of the European Climate Law (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 L'ordine del giorno reca la discussione sulla dichiarazione della Commissione sulla revisione della normativa europea sul clima (2025/2802(RSP)).

Ricordo agli onorevoli deputati che per questa discussione si terr脙聽 un unico turno di interventi di oratori di gruppi politici. Non 脙篓 prevista la procedura "Catch the eye" e non saranno accettati i cartellini blu.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, thank you very much, ladies and gentlemen, I truly appreciate the opportunity to have this conversation here with you.

Let us be upfront about the fact that the proposal we're going to discuss is sensitive, that it is complicated, also from a content and political perspective, and yet it is hugely important. If someone would ask me what the broader goal is that we are envisioning in the next few years, I would say that we need to make sure that decarbonisation is at the heart of our growth and competitiveness strategy, our climate strategy and also our strategy for independence. The way we need to go about this should be ambitious, it should be pragmatic and agile.

Let me move on to each of the topics to just articulate what we, as the Commission, view should be the way forward. First, on competitiveness or growth. Let us be upfront about that as well 芒聙聯 we need more of it. We need more clean economic growth, we need more competitiveness because, frankly speaking, we're not doing as well as we should.

But let's also not have the illusion that the future will be like today. We're facing massive change and the industry, the economy of the future, will be one that is heavily decarbonised. So exposing ourselves to that, making sure we're going to be the winners in the domain of cleantech, re-industrialising Europe and making sure that the growth opportunities are being reaped over here is of essence. That means creating a level playing field. That means continuing with simplification no matter how difficult it is. It means lead markets for those industries that need it. It means significantly more investment in electrification and making sure that we decarbonise also as a way to drive down prices for both consumers and industry, when talking about energy prices.

Secondly, on climate 芒聙聯 sometimes it might be tempting to just forget about the problem and act as if it is no longer there, but what is the reality? The reality is that the planet, on average, is warming up by 1.5脗聽脗掳C, and Europe is at double that number, so 3脗聽脗掳C. And that is an average for Europe 芒聙聯 some would be on the higher end of that average.

The impact of that is huge. Just think about what that means for our ecosystem, but also think about what it means for our economy. We're easily talking about EUR脗聽50芒聙聯100脗聽billion per year for the Union at large, and that number will go up significantly in years to come. And I'm not even talking, because we don't have the time today, about the geopolitical consequences of climate change all over the globe that will certainly be of huge consequence to us here in the European Union.

So what then makes sense? Well, it makes sense to listen to science and listen to what they articulate as what should be the true north. It also makes sense to be predictable, because that is one of the key things our companies across the Union ask from us as politicians. That is also why we decided to arrive at the 90脗聽% number by 2040. Having said that 芒聙聯 and I already mentioned agility or flexibility 芒聙聯 it is important to also be 'street smart', if that is the way to phrase it, in how this proposal is going to work. That is why, as the Commission, we've decided to bake in three types of flexibilities into the design.

The first is to allow companies or governments, for a relatively small percentage of emissions, to take action outside of the European Union. Yes, I do know it is unorthodox, and I do agree with all those who are articulating for certification, for verification, for making sure that this is additional 芒聙聯 but if we can pull this off, it truly matters, and it does mean climate action. By the way, this is a point that is undervalued in the debate, in my view, here in Europe: it builds a tremendously important bridge with our friends in the Global South who do see the merits in this endeavour.

Second, flexibility: making sure that negative emissions created here in Europe can be filtered back into the ETS system. I saw an amazing example recently in beautiful Sweden where the largest CCS project in Europe is taking place. I won't go into all the details of it now, but just to give you the numbers, we're talking about roughly 2脗聽% of total emissions in Sweden with one project 芒聙聯 that is absolutely huge. Yes, it takes a lot of investment, but it is a tremendous step forward because it would only take 50 of those type of projects to basically eradicate all emissions in Sweden. So let's continue with these types of projects, and then let's make sure that we also reward that in our system.

The third flexibility is not allowing Member States who do well in a couple of categories but who might do less well in another to lower the bar, but actually giving them room to compensate what they did well in a couple of categories in a category where they might do slightly worse. I think that is testimony to pragmatism and it also makes sense given the huge diversity of our European Union.

Final point, independence 芒聙聯 let's not make the mistake that we made in the past again. We have been way too dependent on oil and gas from Russia. Unfortunately, it is actually still coming in, and we have a range of dependencies vis-脙聽-vis countries and also dubious regimes in other categories across the board. Just to give you the number, we're talking about EUR脗聽400脗聽billion that the Union, which is of course, in the end, the Member States, pays every single year 芒聙聯 EUR脗聽400脗聽billion for oil and gas. Wouldn't it be more appealing to actually invest that money over time in independence and in energy production and electrification in Europe? I think it would, and it would solve the problem that we're currently facing.

So once again: competitiveness, climate and independence. Doing it in a way that is ambitious, that is pragmatic and that is agile. That, to me, should be the road forward. I'm very much looking forward to the debates, I'm very much looking forward to the interactions that we have ahead of us, and let's make sure we bring this home.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽L脙颅dia Pereira, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, Europe was borne out of the courage to turn conflict into cooperation, to build peace through industry and energy. Today, we need that courage once again.

The European Climate Law is more than targets on paper; it is a promise made to the next generation, those people sitting over there.

(The speaker gestures to the visitors' gallery)

More important than setting a numerical target is making sure we stay on the right course with ambition, with balance, and with real results.

What truly defines European leadership is not an obsession with numbers, but the ability to deliver meaningful, sustainable progress without sacrificing the competitiveness that sustains our European social model and democracy. Because this is not just about saving the planet; it is about building a new economic model: green, fair and competitive.

But let us be clear: our industries are under pressure like never before. Without investment, without innovation, without affordable energy, this transition will come at the cost of deindustrialisation, and that cannot happen. We need flexibility, we need to reduce energy costs, we need to invest in a new generation of technologies 芒聙聯 ones that not only reduce emissions but capture them, that make emissions negative.

Establishing a European Industrial Decarbonisation Bank with over EUR脗聽100脗聽billion in funding could be a strong step forward, but private investment will also be essential.

And above all, we must ensure no one is left behind. Compensation mechanisms such as the ETS must support Europeans, turning the transition into an opportunity, not a burden. Our goals must be guided by science and by courage 芒聙聯 that courage to act, to lead, to believe. Because this law is not just about the climate, it is about us and about what we leave to those who come after us.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tiemo W脙露lken, im Namen der S&D-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrter Herr Kommissar, lieber Wopke! Sch脙露n, dass Sie heute hier sind, und vielen Dank f脙录r den Vorschlag. Es ist immer schwierig, mit einer Korrektur anzufangen, aber ich muss es leider. Der wissenschaftliche Beirat脗聽芒聙聯 Ihr wissenschaftlicher Beirat 芒聙聯 hat gesagt, 90脗聽% ist das Mindeste, was wir 2040 schaffen m脙录ssen, und wir k脙露nnen dar脙录ber hinaus Klimakredite als Flexibilisierungselement nutzen. Aber sie Teil der 90脗聽% zu machen, ist gegen den Vorschlag Ihres eigenen wissenschaftlichen Dienstes. Wir 脙录bernehmen damit nicht mehr die Verantwortung, die wir 脙录bernehmen m脙录ssten. Deswegen sieht meine Fraktion den massiven Einsatz von internationalen Krediten auch so kritisch.

Was ich auch kritisch sehe, ist, dass der Vorschlag erst jetzt kommt. Wir hatten das Vergn脙录gen, als Sie im letzten Mandat nachnominiert wurden, gemeinsam die Anh脙露rung zu verbringen, und Sie wurden gefragt, ob ein 90芒聙聭%芒聙聭Ziel denn in Ihrem Sinne sei und im Sinne der Kommission. Schon vor Jahren haben Sie deswegen gesagt, 芒聙聻ja芒聙聹. Und erst jetzt, wenige Wochen vor der Klimakonferenz in Bel脙漏m, gibt es den Vorschlag der Europ脙陇ischen Kommission, und es gibt nicht ausreichend Zeit, im Europ脙陇ischen Parlament und im Rat dar脙录ber zu diskutieren. Das ist nicht mein Verst脙陇ndnis von Demokratie.

Gleichzeitig stehen wir vor der Klimakrise. Die Klimakrise ist ja nichts, was wir uns ausdenken, und wir m脙录ssen vielleicht mal ein neues Wort daf脙录r finden. Vielleicht sagen wir nicht, wir k脙陇mpfen gegen die Klimakrise, sondern wir k脙陇mpfen um das 脙聹berleben der Menschheit. Dann sind auch viele Argumente, die hier in diesem Raum gemacht werden, langsam etwas komisch. Wenn dann die Wirtschaft wieder sagt: Aber es geht doch um Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit, dann ist die Frage nicht Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit oder Klimaschutz, sondern Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit oder 脙聹berleben der Menschheit. Darum geht es. Deswegen finde ich es auch besch脙陇mend脗聽芒聙聯 und das geht jetzt an die EVP 芒聙聯, dass Sie sich weigern, mit uns 脙录ber einen Dringlichkeitsantrag abzustimmen und daf脙录r zu sorgen, dass wir dieses Thema endlich abhaken. 2019 hat das Europ脙陇ische Parlament hier die Klimakrise erkl脙陇rt. Die EVP war nicht dabei, sondern sie hat gesagt: Wir d脙录rfen nicht Klimakrise sagen, sondern wir m脙录ssen climate urgency sagen. Jetzt auf einmal ist die urgency wieder ein Problem. Das halte ich, lieber Peter, liebe EVP, f脙录r ein Problem. Ihr verschlie脙聼t die Augen vor dem, was wir gemeinsam schaffen m脙录ssen. Wir m脙录ssen vor Bel脙漏m eine ambitionierte Positionierung hinbekommen, damit wir international glaubw脙录rdig r脙录berkommen. Daran sollten wir ab morgen gemeinsam arbeiten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Silvia Sardone, a nome del gruppo PfE. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, continuate a proporre obiettivi ideologici e irrealizzabili, come quello della riduzione del 90脗聽% delle emissioni entro il 2040.

Eppure, ogni santo giorno interi settori produttivi europei chiedono un cambio di rotta. Von der Leyen e Ribera ignorano il grido di allarme di imprese, lavoratori, famiglie e consumatori, che sono sempre pi脙鹿 colpiti dalle vostre scelte folli.

Il vostro Green Deal ha un costo stimato, da qui al 2050, di circa 1脗聽285脗聽miliardi di euro. Solo per l'Italia si parla di 40脗聽miliardi di euro all'anno. Queste spese per acquistare nuove auto, ristrutturare le abitazioni o pagare le tasse verdi ricadono interamente su cittadini e imprese.

Siamo in piena crisi e secondo i sindacati l'industria europea perde 500脗聽posti di lavoro al giorno. Ma a voi non interessa assolutamente nulla. Diciamolo chiaramente: secondo alcune stime, negli ultimi dieci anni, a fronte di una riduzione di dieci punti delle emissioni dei paesi dell'Unione europea, il resto del mondo le ha aumentate, le emissioni, di脗聽120, quindi dodici volte tanto.

I cittadini europei devono sapere che questa Commissione sta costruendo un futuro di decrescita, che piace solo a qualche fanatico della sinistra. E questo lo dico anche a Lei, Commissario, a cui teoricamente questi fanatici della sinistra non dovrebbero proprio piacere.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexandr Vondra, za skupinu ECR. 芒聙聯 Pane komisa脜聶i, ten V脙隆脜隆 n脙隆vrh je 脜隆patn脙陆 od za脛聧脙隆tku a脜戮 do konce. A j脙隆 to tady 脜聶eknu 脛聧esky, proto脜戮e u脜戮 tady anglicky skoro nikdo nemluv脙颅. Evropu tady stejn脛聸 poh脜聶b脙颅v脙隆te sv脙陆mi n脙隆rodn脙颅mi jazyky. Pro脛聧 je 脜隆patn脙陆? Za prv脙漏, c脙颅l sn脙颅脜戮en脙颅 emis脙颅 o 90 % na rok 2040 je nerealistick脙陆. Nen脙颅 obh脙隆jen 脜戮脙隆dnou po脜聶脙隆dnou dopadovou studi脙颅, to i V脙陆bor Komise pro kontrolu regulace p脜聶iznal. Ned脙隆me to technologicky, ned脙隆me to finan脛聧n脛聸. Za druh脙漏, pr脙隆vn脙颅 z脙隆klad je chybn脙陆. M脙隆 z脙隆sadn脙颅 dopad na energetick脙漏 mixy 脛聧lensk脙陆ch st脙隆t脜炉, kter脙漏 jsou v jejich v脙陆lu脛聧n脙漏 kompetenci, a nem脜炉脜戮ete ho sv脙漏voln脛聸 m脛聸nit, n脙隆siln脛聸 a v脛聸t脜隆inov脛聸. Za t脜聶et脙颅, ten n脙隆vrh definitivn脛聸 zlikviduje energeticky n脙隆ro脛聧n脙陆 pr脜炉mysl a poh脜聶b脙颅 na脜隆i obranyschopnost. Z jak脙漏 oceli budeme vyr脙隆b脛聸t tanky? Z 脛聧脙颅nsk脙漏 nebo tureck脙漏? Jak budeme d脙隆l d脛聸lat munici bez chemie? Jak budeme d脛聸lat letadla bez hlin脙颅ku? A ten plyn?

Ano, j脙隆 jsem r脙隆d, 脜戮e jsme se osvobodili od plynu z Ruska, ale Va脜隆e zem脛聸, Nizozemsko, ho dodne脜隆ka odeb脙颅r脙隆 jako jeden z nejv脛聸t脜隆脙颅ch odb脛聸ratel脜炉 v Evrop脛聸. A co jste ud脛聸lal Vy, abyste to zastavil?

Za 脛聧tvrt脙漏, ty n脙隆vrhy na flexibilitu, to je jenom zelen脙陆 kolonialismus, proto脜戮e ten pom脜炉脜戮e bohat脙陆m zem脙颅m, jako je Nizozemsko nebo N脛聸mecko, velk脙陆m nadn脙隆rodn脙颅m korporac脙颅m a chud脙漏 zem脛聸 a mal脙漏 firmy to zaplat脙颅. A za p脙隆t脙漏, blackouty ve 脜聽pan脛聸lsku a v 脛聦esku ukazuj脙颅, kam n脙隆s tato politika dominovan脙隆 ideologi脙颅 jednou dovede 芒聙聯 b脙颅da, anarchie a chaos. 脛聦ili nic n脙隆s nenut脙颅 ten z脙隆kon p脜聶ij脙颅mat te脛聫, jenom ambice v脙漏st sv脛聸t, kdy脜戮 se n脙隆m Amerika a 脛聦脙颅na sm脛聸j脙颅. Nejlep脜隆脙颅 bude, kdy脜戮 to vezmete a st脙隆hnete to zp脙隆tky.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gerben-Jan Gerbrandy, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Mr脗聽Commissioner, dear colleagues, do we need an ambitious climate target in 2040 of at least 90脗聽%? And the answer is clearly yes. And if you're not convinced, ask the relatives of the victims of the Valencia floods. Ask those farmers who are out of business because the crops do not grow any more on their dried-out land. Ask people who lost literally everything except their lives due to forest fires. Or ask Slovenia, where the floods of August 2023 alone already cost them roughly 10脗聽% of GDP.

'Yes, but our economy', I hear colleagues on the right saying. Yes, what about the economy? Increased climate change might cost us up to 34脗聽% of our economic output, while keeping the rise below two degrees will cost us just 1 to 2脗聽% of global GDP. So tell me, what makes more sense economically? Fighting climate change is not a leftist ideological agenda. It's the harsh reality we're faced with. It's the harsh consequence of our own behaviour, and it's our responsibility to all the people represented in this House to save them from these consequences.

So yes, it makes absolutely sense to set an ambitious 90脗聽% target in 2040, but it's also important to do so in time 芒聙聯 in time for the Paris Agreement process, in time for a strong EU position at the upcoming COP30. So, I urge the EPP to support the urgent procedure, because that is the only way to be in time in this House, partly due to the late proposal from the Commission, but please don't endanger the Paris Agreement process and vote in favour of the urgent procedure this week.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lena Schilling, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, I also need to start with this House. The 2040 climate now is in the hands of the far right 芒聙聯 the people that deny the climate crisis 芒聙聯 that has to be the final wake up call for this Parliament.

We have tabled an urgency procedure tomorrow, and it's time for everybody here to really take responsibility. If we want to save the 2040 target, we need this urgency procedure now. And maybe you remember, six years ago, this House stood up and declared a climate emergency. The European Parliament made a promise to the people, to the European citizens, and now we must ask ourselves, are we acting like this is an emergency? Ask yourself for a moment.

And honestly, the science could not be clearer; it's so obvious what we have to do. We need to cut our emissions by at least 90脗聽% in 2030, and stay on track for climate neutrality in 2050. We don't need 85脗聽%, not 87脗聽%,脗聽minimum 90脗聽%.

And yet, what do we see? Instead of urgency, we see delay. Instead of clarity, we see loopholes. Instead of actions, we see games.

Colleagues, every so-called 'flexibility' you put into this law is not just a footnote; it's a gamble with the lives of the future generations. A percentage point less may sound harmless in this room, but outside those emissions are the flooded homes, the dead soil, and the collapsing ecosystems our children will inherit.

Dear colleagues, I'm not just speaking here as a Member of the European Parliament, I'm also speaking as a 24-year-old woman who dares you to act. And I'm not the only one. I think many of your children are honestly my age. If you don't do it for me, do it for them. Do it for the children that need to grow up in the next years, for the future generations. We declared a climate emergency. Now, let's act on it. A woman said a few years ago, we owe it to our children. These children are still here.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Martins, em nome do Grupo The Left. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, acredita que a Terra 脙漏 plana ou a ci脙陋ncia ainda tem algum lugar?

A Lei do Clima estabeleceu um corpo cient脙颅fico para informar sobre as decis脙碌es e sobre a meta de redu脙搂脙拢o. Os cientistas disseram uma coisa fundamental: reduzir as emiss脙碌es num s脙颅tio para aumentar no outro n脙拢o resolve. O com脙漏rcio de emiss脙碌es, est脙隆 provado, n脙拢o funciona. Parece bastante 脙鲁bvio. O planeta 脙漏 s脙鲁 um. As metas t脙陋m de ser mesmo para cumprir.

E o que faz a Comiss脙拢o? Ignora a ci脙陋ncia. Apresenta as metas depois do prazo e em m脙颅nimos de ambi脙搂脙拢o e prop脙碌e mecanismos de flexibiliza脙搂脙拢o que os cientistas avisaram expressamente que n脙拢o servem. Afinal, as metas n脙拢o s脙拢o para cumprir. Depois da sa脙颅da dos Estados Unidos do Acordo de Paris, a Comiss脙拢o decide mostrar que tamb脙漏m n脙拢o 脙漏 de confian脙搂a. Para a Comiss脙拢o, tudo se negoceia no mercado, at脙漏 o clima.

Problema: o planeta n脙拢o vai negociar a temperatura ou o n脙颅vel do mar com ningu脙漏m. Vamos perder todos, a come脙搂ar pelos mais pobres, que enfrentam, sem prote脙搂脙拢o, as ondas de calor e as tempestades. Mas esses, a Comiss脙拢o nem os v脙陋.

Dichiarazioni scritte (articolo 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Markus Buchheit, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Nun, nachdem ja sehr viele heute auf die Zeit von vor sechs Jahren rekurriert haben, m脙露chte auch ich das kurz tun脗聽芒聙聯 es war sehr spannend, insbesondere hier in Stra脙聼burg. Man hat sich quasi bei der EVP darum geschlagen, ein Foto mit diversen S脙陇ulenheiligen wie Anna Greta Thunberg hier zu t脙陇tigen. Ich wei脙聼 nicht, ob diese Fotos noch h脙陇ngen, aber der Ungeist dieser Zeit, der schl脙陇gt bis heute durch.

Und wenn ich mir anschaue, was mit unserer Schwerindustrie passiert脗聽芒聙聯 Thyssen ist ja nur ein Beispiel unter vielen脗聽芒聙聯, was mit unseren Zulieferern passiert, dass auch die IHK, die Industrie- und Handelskammer, jetzt gerade Alarm schl脙陇gt und sagt: Wir erwarten weitere 1,2脗聽Millionen Arbeitspl脙陇tze, die auf der Kippe stehen, und wir erwarten weitere Zusatzkosten bis 2030 nur infolge der derzeitigen Klimaziele von bis zu 15脗聽Milliarden脗聽Euro.

Dann frage ich mich, wof脙录r? Ist es nicht Zeit, diesem Ungeist einer Greta Thunberg, den Klimaklebern von damals, die es heute vielleicht sogar bis auf die Parlamentsb脙陇nke geschafft haben, abzusagen und zu sagen: Schluss, wir wollen wirklich Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit. Und dann ist auch der heutige Titel verfehlt. Denn es sollte nicht um eine Reform gehen, es sollte um die Abschaffung dieser Klimaziele gehen, genauso, wie das auch in den USA praktiziert wird. Stichwort: Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, I say thank you very much, ladies and gentlemen, for your input, your feedback, your support, and, in some cases, also your criticism. I would implore you that if the combination of growth, climate and independence, and even if it is only two or just one of them is appealing to you, then that would actually make sense to support the Clean Industrial Deal, this proposal and the whole approach by this Commission.

There are four things I feel are worthwhile to clarify, also listening to the various speakers. Tiemo W脙露lken I think has left the building, but nonetheless he was asking, why now? Well, because in my experience in politics, it is at least as much about process, about communication, about building majorities as it is about just plain content. So have we overinvested in this? Absolutely. Are we going to continue to overinvest in this in the weeks and months going forward? Absolutely. Because what we do need, particularly for these types of policies, are solid majorities. And yes, sometimes that takes more time than you anticipate beforehand.

Secondly, Ms Sardone, but also others were alluding to the cost and that takes a longer conversation than what we can just do here. But let me be very, very clear what elements you should take into account and whatnot. One is, let's never confuse out-of-pocket costs with investments, which is typically done in this debate. Secondly, it is of huge importance to take into account the cost of doing nothing and what that will do and what that damage would bring. And Mr脗聽Gerbrandy and others were already alluding to the damage that has been caused, but let me give you the number for Europe as a whole, once again: EUR脗聽5.2 trillion and counting, and that number will simply only go up.

Last, on the domain of cost, let's not confuse ourselves or fool ourselves that even if we would forget about this whole thing, that no cost would be involved. It would have huge ramifications in cost in terms of funding what we currently already have. And I was just giving you the number of EUR脗聽400 billion we invest every single year as Europeans in oil and gas. Do we want that with all the dependencies that come with it? I think we don't.

Third, Alexandr Vondra was mildly critical, if that is the way to label his contribution, but there's one thing I wanted to articulate to him as well: I would like to invite him to the text because he has often pleaded for technological neutrality,脗聽and he, of course, has often alluded to the importance of including nuclear. And I've said many times before that, in my view, particularly if you want to solve the problem we have in terms of climate, nuclear is part of the solution space. The wording is there. So the question for him as well is, is the glass half-full or is it half-empty? I would say it is half-full, even from his perspective.

Last but not least, there were also a couple of comments by Ms脗聽Martins and others about, 'hey, but how does it work with the percentage that actually comes from from outside of the European Union?' Well, just to be clear on a couple of things 芒聙聯 one is, climate change does not discriminate. It doesn't matter where stuff is being pumped into the air. Even if all the emissions take place outside of Europe then still we have an incentive to do something about it. Why? Well, because we are on the receiving end of the impact and the impact in Europe, unfortunately, simply because where we are located on the globe is significantly larger, twice as large as on average, and therefore it is way more damaging for our economy and our geopolitical situation.

Let me also add to that, because Ms脗聽Martins was also slightly critical, that we are doing more than our fair share, more than our fair share in terms of climate action. We're currently at 6脗聽% of our share in global emissions. That means that 94脗聽% of emissions are from elsewhere, and we're doing far more than our fair share in terms of the financing of climate action abroad. We're talking easily about USD脗聽30 billion out of USD脗聽100 billion. If the rest of the developed world and countries like China and countries in the Gulf and others that have made giant leaps in their progress would do the same, we would be having a completely different debate.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 La discussione 脙篓 chiusa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Gonz脙隆lez Casares (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 El nuevo objetivo clim脙隆tico del 90 % para 2040 es un paso decisivo hacia la neutralidad clim脙隆tica en 2050. Por eso, desde la Delegaci脙鲁n Socialista Espa脙卤ola en el Parlamento Europeo apoyamos con firmeza la propuesta de la Comisi脙鲁n y celebramos la inclusi脙鲁n de este objetivo en la revisi脙鲁n de la Ley Europea del Clima. Los socialistas creemos que reforzar el marco legal europeo es clave para avanzar de forma ordenada, justa y ambiciosa.

Adem脙隆s, hemos votado a favor del procedimiento de urgencia para garantizar que la Uni脙鲁n pueda presentar su contribuci脙鲁n determinada a nivel nacional (CDN) al Acuerdo de Par脙颅s a tiempo y alineada con el nuevo objetivo para 2040. El liderazgo clim脙隆tico europeo requiere coherencia, ambici脙鲁n y capacidad de respuesta.

Sin embargo, expresamos nuestras reservas sobre el uso de cr脙漏ditos internacionales de carbono, que solo deben ser un recurso excepcional, con mecanismos estrictos de verificaci脙鲁n y transparencia. Estos cr脙漏ditos no pueden sustituir los esfuerzos dom脙漏sticos ni permitir retrasos en la transformaci脙鲁n estructural de nuestra econom脙颅a.

La acci脙鲁n clim脙隆tica es una responsabilidad intergeneracional. Frente a la presi脙鲁n de quienes quieren frenar o diluir la transici脙鲁n, reafirmamos nuestro compromiso con una Europa clim脙隆ticamente neutra, socialmente justa y con visi脙鲁n de futuro.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽C脙漏sar Luena (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 El nuevo objetivo clim脙隆tico del 90 % para 2040 es un paso decisivo hacia la neutralidad clim脙隆tica en 2050. Por eso, desde la Delegaci脙鲁n Socialista Espa脙卤ola en el Parlamento Europeo apoyamos con firmeza la propuesta de la Comisi脙鲁n y celebramos la inclusi脙鲁n de este objetivo en la revisi脙鲁n de la Ley Europea del Clima. Los socialistas creemos que reforzar el marco legal europeo es clave para avanzar de forma ordenada, justa y ambiciosa.

Adem脙隆s, hemos votado a favor del procedimiento de urgencia para garantizar que la Uni脙鲁n pueda presentar su contribuci脙鲁n determinada a nivel nacional (CDN) al Acuerdo de Par脙颅s a tiempo y alineada con el nuevo objetivo para 2040. El liderazgo clim脙隆tico europeo requiere coherencia, ambici脙鲁n y capacidad de respuesta.

Sin embargo, expresamos nuestras reservas sobre el uso de cr脙漏ditos internacionales de carbono, que solo deben ser un recurso excepcional, con mecanismos estrictos de verificaci脙鲁n y transparencia. Estos cr脙漏ditos no pueden sustituir los esfuerzos dom脙漏sticos ni permitir retrasos en la transformaci脙鲁n estructural de nuestra econom脙颅a.

La acci脙鲁n clim脙隆tica es una responsabilidad intergeneracional. Frente a la presi脙鲁n de quienes quieren frenar o diluir la transici脙鲁n, reafirmamos nuestro compromiso con una Europa clim脙隆ticamente neutra, socialmente justa y con visi脙鲁n de futuro.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sirpa Pietik脙陇inen (PPE), in writing. 芒聙聯 Climate change is intensifying and the impacts of climate change are worsening each year. Climate change poses also an economic crisis. The cost of inaction is as high as 10 times the cost of necessary climate adaptation.

The European Parliament approved a resolution declaring a climate and environmental emergency in Europe and globally in November 2019 芒聙聯 and rightfully so. Parliament urged the Commission to ensure that all relevant legislative and budgetary proposals are fully aligned with the Paris Agreement by limiting global warming to under 1.5脗聽脗掳C.

Despite this, we are still debating political compromises on climate targets while ignoring clear signals from scientists to reach domestic net-zero emissions far before 2050. While I welcome the Commission芒聙聶s proposal on the 2040 climate target, we are still falling short in aligning it with the 1.5脗聽脗掳C temperature limit. The EU should reach its gross emissions reductions domestically without the inclusion of international carbon credits that severely delay the process and undermine the EU contribution. In addition, we should not rely on carbon removals such as CCS, as we should focus on gross emissions reduction to avoid sidetracks and delays in climate action.

脗聽

15. Amendment of the agenda
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I would like to inform you that the President has received three separate requests from Renew, S&D and the Greens for an urgent procedure pursuant to Rule脗聽170(5) on the European Climate Law.

The vote will be taken tomorrow. If adopted, the vote will be added to the draft agenda for a future part session.

脗聽

16. Situation in the Middle East (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 L'ordine del giorno reca la discussione sulle dichiarazioni del Consiglio e della Commissione sulla situazione in Medio Oriente (2025/2772(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioner, the European Union remains strongly committed to peace, security and stability in the Middle East region. In this regard, we welcome the recent stop of hostilities between Israel and Iran. Both sides must respect international law and refrain from actions that could escalate into broader regional destabilisation.

The European Union's view on Iran's nuclear program is clear. Iran must never be allowed to acquire a nuclear weapon and must comply with its obligations under the Non芒聙聭Proliferation Treaty. That is why we find it very worrying that the Iranian government has decided to suspend cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency. Such cooperation should be resumed without delay.

We have also said clearly that a lasting solution to the Iranian nuclear issue can only be achieved through negotiations. The European Union stands, therefore, ready to continue to contribute to all diplomatic efforts aimed at deescalating current tensions.

In Gaza, the situation has not improved. The European Council called again for an immediate ceasefire and the unconditional release of all hostages, leading to a permanent end to hostilities. Heads of state and government called once more on Israel to fully comply with its obligations under international law, including international humanitarian law, in ensuring that humanitarian aid reaches all those in need at scale.

The European Union is fully committed to a lasting and sustainable peace based on the two芒聙聭state solution. We are ready to contribute to all efforts towards this solution and call on all parties to refrain from actions that undermine its viability. We keep the engagement on the High-level International Conference for the Peaceful Settlement of the Question of Palestine and the implementation of the two芒聙聭state solution, and we look forward to its organisation as soon as possible.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 Siamo in attesa della Commissaria Roswall. Siamo costretti ad aspettare che arrivi la Commissaria. No, sostituisce la Commissaria Roswall il Commissario Hoekstra, prego.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, indeed, it is not yet Commissioner Roswall, but she is on her way. This reminds me of the fairy tale where you are constantly waiting for someone to arrive, but at some point she will arrive, I promise you. Until she's here, ladies and gentlemen, you have to do it with me and I'm going to speak on her behalf. She was actually planning to speak on the behalf of Kaja脗聽Kallas, who then, of course, speaks on behalf of the Commission.

Madam脗聽President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, during the last plenary session, we discussed the developments in the Middle East following the escalation between Israel and Iran and the military operation on Iranian nuclear sites. Since then, we've all seen that there has been a fragile ceasefire, if I may call it that.

The European Union has always been clear and unequivocal in its position when it comes to Iran. Let's have no illusions and let's make it clear that Iran can never be allowed to acquire a nuclear weapon. Let me stress that: it can never be allowed to acquire a nuclear weapon. In our view, therefore, the sustainable solution is to build through diplomacy.

Our High Representative Kaja Kallas clearly stated the EU's position in her discussion with the Iranian Minister of Foreign Affairs and she committed once again the commitment to dialogue for a peaceful solution. Ladies and gentlemen, it is in this context that we're deeply concerned by the approval of the law suspending cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency and the direct attacks 芒聙聯 because it's nothing less than that 芒聙聯the direct attacks against the agency and against the Director General Mr脗聽Grossi in particular.

Therefore, let me state clearly once again our full support to the International Atomic Energy Agency and Director General Grossi in what we view as an absolutely essential task of continuing to monitor Iran's nuclear program in a professional and impartial matter because that is exactly what he and his team were doing before. The Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons remains, in our view, the cornerstone of the global non芒聙聭proliferation regime. And Iran, particularly because it is a signatory, must live up to its commitments.

It is also important to recall that there are also other grievances when it comes to Iran and the list is long, including their military support to Russia, the human rights situation 芒聙聯 that includes arbitrarily detained EU citizens, many of us know examples by heart 芒聙聯 and destabilising activities in the region for years that the EU continues to address with Iran.

Honourable Members, the humanitarian situation in Gaza continues to be also at the centre of EU concerns. At the last Foreign Affairs Council, ministers were clear that the situation does need to change on the ground and, as tasked, the EU has since been concluding a humanitarian dialogue with Israel. We're asking Israel for concrete, precise and tangible changes in the way the humanitarian assistance is being delivered in Gaza.

Of course, the EU脗聽also acknowledges recent developments, including the reopening of the Kerem Shalom crossing in southern Gaza on 19脗聽May, which allowed UN manifested humanitarian trucks to enter, followed by the opening of the Erez West Zikim crossing on 11脗聽June. In our view, these steps represent progress in facilitating the delivery of aid by humanitarian actors.脗聽However, also in our view, these remain insufficient to address the scale and severity of the humanitarian crisis that is going on within Gaza due to a range of ongoing challenges.

Ladies and gentlemen, minimum elements need to be in place to ensure that humanitarian aid reaches the people in need. Allow me to provide you with some examples. A total collapse 芒聙聯 there is no other way to phrase this 芒聙聯 a total collapse of humanitarian operations in the Gaza Strip is imminent if fuel is not urgently allowed to enter. For humanitarian aid at scale, there is also the need to significantly increase the flow of trucks across borders equally in the north and in the south. Looting and self芒聙聭distributions continue affecting most of humanitarian trucks and this has to be addressed, along with ensuring the safety of humanitarian personnel.

These are elements we are currently insisting on in our dialogue that we're carrying out with Israel. The ministers of foreign affairs will revert to the situation in the Foreign Affairs Council of July and then decide on the way forward.

So, ladies and gentlemen, honourable Members, at what I would call a truly critical junction, we reaffirm our strong support once again for the security of all states in the region. We urge all actors in the region to avoid any steps that could jeopardise the fragile calm and potentially lead to catastrophic consequences.

Thank you very much and I am happy to see that the next Commissioner is here.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler, im Namen der PPE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, werte Kollegen! Was zum Iran gesagt wurde, unterstreiche ich, brauche ich nicht zu wiederholen. Ich konzentriere mich auf Gaza, und habe den Eindruck, dass es hier schon recht weitgehenden Konsens im Hause gibt, was dort zu geschehen hat. Erstens braucht es dringend einen Waffenstillstand. Die Menschen in Gaza und die noch lebenden Geiseln haben lange genug unter den K脙陇mpfen zwischen der israelischen Armee und der terroristischen Hamas gelitten, die Geiseln noch zus脙陇tzlich durch die Gefangenschaft unter schrecklichen Bedingungen. Zweitens m脙录ssen die noch lebenden Geiseln freikommen. Sie d脙录rfen nicht weiter als Faustpfand von Hamas missbraucht werden und m脙录ssen auch Priorit脙陇t der Bem脙录hungen der israelischen Regierung sein. Drittens m脙录ssen alle Hilfsorganisationen, die an der Grenze mit ihren Versorgungsg脙录tern warten, ungehinderten Zugang haben. Die Organisationen der UN und des Roten Kreuzes k脙露nnen das, die Gaza Humanitarian Foundation kann es nicht. Sie hat weder die Kapazit脙陇ten noch den Auftrag, etwa 600脗聽Lastwagenladungen pro Tag dort auszuladen. Viertens muss klar sein, dass es in Gaza keine irgendwie geartete Zukunft f脙录r Hamas geben kann. Die Organisation war auch schon vor dem 7.脗聽Oktober gegen脙录ber den Menschen in Gaza eine repressive Organisation und hat weiterhin die Zerst脙露rung Israels als Ziel. Und f脙录nftens muss klar sein: Die Menschen in Gaza haben eine Zukunft dort. Den gestern 脙露ffentlich 脙录bertragenen Lunch芒聙聭 oder Dinnertalk zwischen Trump und Netanjahu fand ich schon fast gespenstisch脗聽芒聙聯 wie dort zwischen Salat und Filetsteak 脙录ber die Zukunft von zwei Millionen Menschen geplaudert wurde. Gegen den Willen der Menschen dort und gegen den Willen der arabischen Nachbarn und der Europ脙陇er wird es keine Umsiedlung, keine Vertreibung oder Deportation geben. Einen gerechten Frieden in einer Zukunft ohne Angst und Gewalt f脙录r beide V脙露lker kann es nur in einer von beiden Seiten ausgehandelten Zweistaatenl脙露sung geben.

脗聽脗聽Sebastiaan St脙露teler, on behalf of the PfE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, this debate about the situation in the Middle East will likely be taken as yet another opportunity to bash Israel. But the situation is: the Middle East has become a lot safer thanks to Israeli actions in response to the horrific and traumatic day of 7脗聽October.

The Middle East is dealing with the dark terror forces of Hamas, Hezbollah, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, the Houthis and so many other terror factions in the region. It was those Iranian proxies that started the war on 7脗聽October 芒聙聯 not the only true democracy in the Middle East that upholds our common interests and values. It is the State of Israel that is fighting for its people's survival.

Yet, those dark forces, those terror groups (namely the Houthis) still find ways to make the world a more dangerous place. Just yesterday and today, the Houthis attacked commercial ships off the coast of Hodeidah, Yemen. Yesterday they attacked Liberian-registered ship Magic Seas. Fortunately, the crew of 22 people could be saved. And then this morning, another merchant vessel was attacked by reportedly five rockets, sustaining severe damage and losing power. From what we know now, two crew members were injured and two of them are still missing. We hope they found safety and are well.

Dear colleagues, this is the situation in the Middle East. The Islamic Republic of Iran fear the Houthis, fear other proxies attacking world trade, world order and thereby seeking to destabilise the world and economy and security. We should support Israel and America in their efforts to putting things in order.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Arkadiusz Mularczyk, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Od dekad s脜聜yszymy, 脜录e sytuacja na Bliskim Wschodzie jest na tyle skomplikowana i pe脜聜na niuans脙鲁w, 脜录e osi脛聟gni脛聶cie trwa脜聜ego pokoju jest tam niemal niemo脜录liwe. Polityka Donalda Trumpa spod znaku pokoju poprzez si脜聜脛聶 zada脜聜a k脜聜am tej tezie i przynios脜聜a efekty w bezprecedensowym tempie. Przyw脙鲁dcy kraj脙鲁w zachodnich musz脛聟 zrozumie脛聡 bowiem jedn脛聟 zasad脛聶: kraje Bliskiego Wschodu rz脛聟dz脛聟 si脛聶 innymi prawami i wyznaj脛聟 inne warto脜聸ci ni脜录 zachodnie demokracje.

Dla ajatollah脙鲁w w Iranie pr脙鲁by ust脛聶pstw s脛聟 dowodem s脜聜abo脜聸ci i niezdecydowania, czego wyrazem by脜聜a fatalna umowa dotycz脛聟ca kontroli ira脜聞skiego programu rozwoju broni j脛聟drowej zawarta pod egid脛聟 prezydenta Obamy. W rezultacie, gdyby脜聸my mieli dzisiaj innego gospodarza Bia脜聜ego Domu, tak jak tego chcia脜聜aby Ursula von der Leyen, perspektywa posiadania przez jeden z najgorszych re脜录im脙鲁w na 脜聸wiecie najbardziej 脜聸mierciono脜聸nej broni by脜聜aby bardzo realna. Na szcz脛聶脜聸cie zamiast tego czarnego scenariusza mamy os脜聜abiony Iran i potencja脜聜 na poszerzenie liczby cz脜聜onk脙鲁w Porozumie脜聞 Abrahamowych. Za to prezydent Trump zas脜聜u脜录y脜聜 na Nagrod脛聶 Nobla.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Minister, Commissioner, colleagues, the European Union stands at a crossroads. The recent reports confirm what we can no longer ignore: grave violations of human rights by Israel, including attacks on hospitals, the blockade of humanitarian aid and forced displacement. Over 17脗聽000 children have been killed in Gaza, according to Unicef today.

There is no doubt, colleagues, these acts breach Article脗聽2 of the EU-Israel Association Agreement. And international courts have spoken. So, if we fail to act now, we not only undermine our own laws, we erode our global credibility.

Therefore, I call for the immediate suspension of the association agreement. No trade, no cooperation, no impunity 芒聙聯 this until international law is upheld. Let us prove that our values are not up for negotiation. And colleagues, if this is not happening, maybe we really should give a strong signal and not vote for the discharge of the Council in October.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hannah Neumann, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Minister, Commissioner, dear colleagues, the Iranian regime fuels terror at home and abroad, through executions, through proxies, through a nuclear programme built for impunity. Hamas, one of its proxies, committed the horrific 7脗聽October attacks. Netanyahu's government responded with brutal force in Gaza, blocking humanitarian aid, expanding illegal settlements in the West Bank.

But what did Netanyahu's and Trump's bombs achieve? The centrifuges will spin again. The hate has deepened. This is not good versus evil. It is a tragedy of leaders abusing power and of people caught in between. Because Israel is also the protesters in the streets. Palestine is also the civilians trapped between occupation and Hamas. Iran is also the women who burn their hijabs and whisper 'azadi'. These people are not collateral to geopolitics; they are the path to peace. And if we centre our foreign policy on them, on human dignity and international law, we can support their call for a lasting peace rooted in freedom for everyone.

And because I was somehow just awarded 30 seconds, allow me to raise my frustration with the fact that Kaja Kallas is not here today to discuss this crucial topic for us here in the European Union and for the future of the region, and I would hope that she could be here with us a bit more often.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Antonio L脙鲁pez-Ist脙潞riz White (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, our last plenary session three weeks ago saw the launching by Israel of Operation Rising Lion, aiming to contain Iran's nuclear threat. We have to recognise that, three weeks later, Israeli citizens, Arab, moderate people in the region and us Europeans, we feel safer. Thank you to Israel. And thank you for the United States for this, that we feel safer.

Although we Europeans, we are a little bit hypocritical, because we celebrate this in private, but we don't do it in public. It was about time to end this nuclear threat, not only for Israel, but also for ourselves, because as I always have stated in this plenary, we are the next target.

Make no mistake, the plans of the ayatollahs to threaten and blackmail us are continuing. Terrorist organisations continue to plan how to destroy our democracies and our freedoms. In this House, we have to be alert to this fact. The future of the Middle East is in the Abraham Accords, against terrorism, theocratic regimes, working with the opposition, democratic opposition to these regimes, whatever it is called, Tehran or Caracas in Venezuela or Moscow in Russia. This is what has to unite us in our common struggle.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nacho S脙隆nchez Amor (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, ante nuestros ojos se despliega con todo su esplendor una pol脙颅tica exterior europea presa del s脙颅ndrome de Chamberlain: apaciguar y tranquilizar a los nuevos l脙颅deres autoritarios del mundo. Solo se muestra verticalidad moral hacia Putin y hacia Rusia; para el resto 芒聙聰para Trump, para Erdo脛聼an, para Saied, para Al-Sisi, para Aliyev, para Netanyahu芒聙聰, apaciguamiento: es la 脙潞nica pol脙颅tica europea.

Exhibimos valores y principios como un pavo real, con Putin, aqu脙颅, en el lado oscuro de la calle Belliard, pero, para los otros, los dobles raseros, que se est脙隆n convirtiendo en un componente no ya tradicional, sino gen脙漏tico: las met脙隆stasis de nuestra pol脙颅tica exterior.

El caso de Israel es un buen ejemplo: la pereza en activar los mecanismos de defensa de los derechos humanos est脙隆 costando vidas todos los d脙颅as. En este Parlamento se recibe con normalidad al engendro supuestamente humanitario israelo芒聙聭estadounidense, cuya 脙潞nica funci脙鲁n es atraer a los hombres palestinos a las colas del hambre para ser all脙颅 tiroteados a placer por mercenarios americanos. Aqu脙颅 el dilema es suspender o no un Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n; all脙颅 el dilema es ver morir a los hijos o arriesgar la vida en una cola del hambre.

Yo se lo pongo f脙隆cil al Consejo, porque el Consejo tiene que elegir c脙鲁mo pasa la historia: o como Chamberlain, o como Churchill.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

VORSITZ: KATARINA BARLEY
Vizepr脙陇sidentin

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hermann Tertsch (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidente, me encantan los socialistas evocando a Churchill... Ahora te voy a contar yo a ti. No s脙漏 si saben ya que el primer ministro Netanyahu ha propuesto a Donald Trump para el Premio Nobel de la Paz; un premio que le ten脙颅an que haber dado ya en su primer mandato por unos Acuerdos de Abraham que cambiaron Oriente Medio ya entonces y que avisaban de cu脙隆l iba a ser el camino de la aut脙漏ntica paz y de las posibilidades de estabilidad en Oriente Medio. No se lo dieron, por supuesto, a Trump por ideolog脙颅a; por ideolog脙颅a se lo hab脙颅an dado preventivamente a Obama, que sembr脙鲁 todo el mundo de guerras con su debilidad y su progresismo.

Es m脙隆s, yo les propongo que, junto a Donald Trump, le den el Premio Nobel a Israel, porque Israel ha cambiado Oriente Medio 芒聙聰est脙隆 cambiando Oriente Medio芒聙聰 para resistir al mal, para romper el eje del mal, y ha acabado con 脙漏l. Hoy ya no existe Hizbul脙隆. Hoy no existe Al-Asad. Hoy est脙隆n debilitados los grandes lazos terroristas de Ir脙隆n por todo el mundo, a pesar de que Ir脙隆n sigue ah脙颅 y todav脙颅a no ha ca脙颅do. Pero Oriente Medio tiene unas perspectivas de paz cuando se den cuenta de que Israel est脙隆 ah脙颅 para quedarse, y se ha dado un paso de gigante para eso. Israel est脙隆 defendi脙漏ndonos a nosotros, a todo Occidente, e Israel, que da la vida de sus hijos, de sus soldados, en una guerra cruel, se merece este reconocimiento.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carlo Ciccioli (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, noi dobbiamo essere chiari: l'Europa in Medio Oriente 脙篓 di fatto inesistente, non ha alcuna capacit脙聽 di interlocuzione, n脙漏 come Unione europea n脙漏 come singoli Stati.

Certamente, i problemi in Medio Oriente hanno radici antichissime, storiche e culturali.

L'Islam pi脙鹿 radicale coltiva l'idea di cancellare la presenza ebraica e lo Stato di Israele 脙篓 vissuto come una provocazione intollerabile. Attorno a questa ferita aperta si sono costruite ideologie di odio, reti di terrorismo e finanziamenti occulti.

L'attacco del 7脗聽ottobre ha rivelato la vulnerabilit脙聽 di Israele e l脙聽 si 脙篓 fatta strada la convinzione che l'unica risposta sia annientare tutte le basi del terrorismo e chi le alimenta. 脙聢 questa la logica dietro le operazioni contro Hamas, Hezbollah e gli Houthi.

Ammettiamolo: finch脙漏 esister脙聽 la Repubblica islamica dell'Iran 芒聙聯 che arma, addestra e finanzia gruppi terroristici 芒聙聯 non ci sar脙聽 vera pace. Serve una posizione univoca dell'Occidente, con Unione europea e Stati Uniti allineati. Necessario 脙篓 anche un atto di autocoscienza: Israele deve darsi regole chiare di tutela verso le popolazioni civili.

Ma la scelta 脙篓 una sola: o l'Occidente ritrova compattezza e coraggio per imporre la sua visione forte o lascer脙聽 spazio all'odio e al terrorismo per altri decenni.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Barry Andrews (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Minister, Commissioner and colleagues, yesterday we bore witness to the very grotesque image of Benjamin Netanyahu presenting Donald Trump with his nomination papers for the Nobel Peace Prize. The words that come to mind are 'beyond parody', 'beyond ridicule', 'perversity', 'incomprehensible' 芒聙聯 because on the same day, Israel Katz, the Minister for Defence, proposes to move Gazans to an enclosed area in Rafah, perhaps satirically referred to as 'humanitarian city'. George Orwell could not have invented this kind of language, described as well by Israeli academics and lawyers as, I quote, 'a blueprint for crimes against humanity'.

Eventually, all Gazans will have to go there. Even the Professor of Jewish History at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, Amos Goldberg, said the plan was for, and I quote, 'the creation of a concentration camp or a transit camp for Palestinians before they expel them'.

I think the one thing that we can agree on in this divided House is that the Nobel Committee in Oslo will not dwell too long on those nomination papers.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Villy S脙赂vndal (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Jeg bliver altid overrasket over de af mine kolleger fra det yderste h脙赂jre, der kun kan tale om smerten for israelere, der mister, men ikke er i stand til med et eneste ord at forst脙楼 tabene for pal脙娄stinenserne i en situation, hvor det bliver mere og mere desperat for den pal脙娄stinensiske befolkning i Gaza, p脙楼 Vestbredden og i 脙聵stjerusalem. De israelske bombardementer efterlader et Gaza i ruiner, mennesker uden boliger, uden hospitaler og uden skoler. Det er en kollektiv afstraffelse af et helt folk for Hamas' terrorangreb den syvende oktober. Israel blokerer for alt: mad, vand, medicin, br脙娄ndstof, og ved de f脙楼 n脙赂dhj脙娄lpsuddelinger bliver de beskudt af milit脙娄ret, n脙楼r de kommer. Mennesker sulter og d脙赂r, og verden ser p脙楼. Israel beg脙楼r forf脙娄rdelige krigsforbrydelser i Pal脙娄stina. Det er ogs脙楼 derfor, at modstanden mod krigen vokser i Europa og i Israel, hvor de k脙娄mpestore demonstrationer forts脙娄tter for at stoppe krigen og for at f脙楼 gidslerne hjem. Vi er n脙赂dt til at tvinge den h脙赂jreekstreme israelske regering til at stoppe bombardementerne og udsultningen af et helt folk.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Ministre, Monsieur le Commissaire, vous l芒聙聶avez lu comme moi, Isra脙芦l veut concentrer au moins 600脗聽000脗聽Palestiniens dans un camp, 脙聽 Gaza. Ce serait comme rassembler trois fois la population de Charleroi ou trois fois la population de Li脙篓ge dans un camp.

L芒聙聶objectif ultime, selon les propres mots des Isra脙漏liens, ce serait de voir effectivement l芒聙聶expulsion, l芒聙聶脙漏migration, la d脙漏portation des Palestiniens de la bande de Gaza. Isra脙芦l renoue ainsi ouvertement avec les pires pages de l芒聙聶histoire du colonialisme europ脙漏en. En effet, rappelons-le-nous, bien avant les horreurs de la Seconde Guerre mondiale, l芒聙聶Espagne coloniale avait d脙漏j脙聽 cr脙漏脙漏 des camps de concentration 脙聽 Cuba, o脙鹿 des dizaines de milliers de personnes sont mortes de faim et de maladies. Le Royaume-Uni en a aussi construit en Afrique du Sud, l芒聙聶Allemagne lors de son g脙漏nocide en Namibie, et la France, 脙漏videmment, en Alg脙漏rie.

Aujourd芒聙聶hui, le fait que m脙陋me ces plans d芒聙聶un camp de concentration n芒聙聶脙漏branlent pas votre soutien 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙聣tat d芒聙聶Isra脙芦l est inacceptable. Entre-temps, nous avons eu les luttes pour les droits de l芒聙聶homme, nous avons les luttes contre le colonialisme. Mais vous, vous restez partenaire d芒聙聶Isra脙芦l, sans sanctions. Vous continuez m脙陋me de traiter ce pays en partenaire privil脙漏gi脙漏. C芒聙聶est inacceptable. Aujourd芒聙聶hui, sanctionnons Isra脙芦l d芒聙聶urgence!

脗聽脗聽脛陆ubo脜隆 Blaha (NI). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, s 脙潞ctou h脛戮ad脙颅m na velik脙隆nov 脜戮idovsk脙漏ho p脙麓vodu od Karla Marxa cez Rosu Luxemburgov脙潞 a脜戮 po Noama Chomsk脙漏ho. Izrael p脙隆cha genoc脙颅du a vra脜戮d脙颅 脜戮eny a deti v desa脜楼tis脙颅coch. Za posledn脙漏ho polroka Izrael napadol p脙陇脜楼 suver脙漏nnych 脜隆t脙隆tov vr脙隆tane Ir脙隆nu a Palest脙颅ny. Washington Izraelu asistuje a krimin脙隆lne za脙潞to脛聧il na Ir脙隆n a Brusel ticho prit脙隆ka. Leyenov脙隆 dokonca nach脙隆dza pochopenie pre zjavn脙漏 poru脜隆enie medzin脙隆rodn脙漏ho pr脙隆va. U脜戮 len za toto by mala by脜楼 okam脜戮ite odvolan脙隆. Povedzme to u脜戮 na rovinu, v 脛聧asoch Hitlerovho Nemecka tu bola diabolsk脙隆 os Berl脙颅n, R脙颅m, Tokio. Dnes je tu nov脙隆 imperi脙隆lna os, a to Washington, Brusel, Tel Aviv. Americk脙陆 sociol脙鲁g William Robinson m脙隆 pravdu, ke脛聫 dokazuje, 脜戮e sionizmus je nacizmom dvadsiateho prv脙漏ho storo脛聧ia. Leyenov脙隆 a Kallasov脙隆 tu chrlia sankcie proti Rusku, no pri izraelskom terorizme s脙潞 ticho. Kto e脜隆te m脙隆 bra脜楼 tieto farizejky v脙隆脜戮ne? Odst脙潞pte!

脗聽脗聽Sebasti脙拢o Bugalho (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, em dezembro, o enviado da Comiss脙拢o Europeia para a paz no M脙漏dio Oriente disse-nos: 脗芦N脙拢o podemos ficar surpreendidos se virmos um aumento do terrorismo nos pr脙鲁ximos anos.脗禄 O relat脙鲁rio anual da Europol confirmou: as imagens de morte e destrui脙搂脙拢o em Gaza impactam a seguran脙搂a da Uni脙拢o Europeia, com um apoio que j脙隆 vai al脙漏m da Al-Qaeda e do Estado Isl脙垄mico.

Senhora Presidente, mesmo que a Europa n脙拢o olhe para o M脙漏dio Oriente, o M脙漏dio Oriente olha para a Europa. A trag脙漏dia em Gaza 脙漏 uma realidade para aqueles que est脙拢o em Gaza e um risco para aqueles que a ignorarem. No acordo de parceria entre Israel e a Uni脙拢o Europeia, est脙隆 previsto o respeito pelos direitos humanos como elemento essencial. Civis mortos junto de centros de ajuda humanit脙隆ria p脙碌em em causa esse acordo.

脙聣, por isso, fundamental que a Comiss脙拢o Europeia apoie uma miss脙拢o de observa脙搂脙拢o externa e independente, que afira as responsabilidades destas mortes na eventualidade de crimes de guerra. Num territ脙鲁rio fechado a observadores e jornalistas, qualquer avalia脙搂脙拢o de qualquer acordo ficar脙隆 incompleta. Fa脙搂amo芒聙聭la a tempo.

(O orador aceita responder a uma pergunta 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhor Deputado Sebasti脙拢o Bugalho, acabou de fazer refer脙陋ncia aos crimes de guerra cometidos por Israel nos postos de distribui脙搂脙拢o de alimentos, onde est脙拢o a ser assassinados adultos e crian脙搂as a sangue芒聙聭frio.

E a pergunta que lhe fa脙搂o 脙漏 muito direta: o que 脙漏 que 脙漏 preciso mais entrar芒聙聭nos pelos olhos dentro para exigir 脙聽 Uni脙拢o Europeia que suspenda o Acordo de Associa脙搂脙拢o com Israel?

Senhor Deputado, n脙拢o h脙隆 comiss脙碌es externas, n脙拢o h脙隆 avalia脙搂脙碌es que possam passar um pano e apagar a responsabilidade e a crueldade daquilo que est脙隆 a ser feito 脙聽 frente dos nossos olhos, daquilo que est脙隆 a acontecer em Gaza h脙隆 640 dias.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebasti脙拢o Bugalho (PPE), Resposta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhor Deputado, s脙鲁 posso interpretar a sua interpela脙搂脙拢o como uma interpela脙搂脙拢o que era para a Comiss脙拢o Europeia e n脙拢o para um deputado. Por uma raz脙拢o muito simples: como o senhor deputado sabe, o governo da minha coliga脙搂脙拢o apoia a suspens脙拢o desse acordo em qualquer decis脙拢o que seja tomada em sede de Conselho, depois da avalia脙搂脙拢o da Comiss脙拢o.

O senhor deputado diz-me: quanto mais tempo teremos que esperar por essa avalia脙搂脙拢o? Na minha perspetiva, j脙隆 esper脙隆mos tempo demais.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, what was it 芒聙聯 one, two, three hours ago that脗聽Tomas Tob脙漏, on behalf of the EPP, was talking about the victims of communism? The only difference between us is that we never make a difference between 'victims' and 'victims'.

It should not be controversial to assert that killing, starving, mutilating and slaughtering children constitutes a clear violation of international law. That is what is happening right now in Gaza, what the far-right government of Israel is doing to the children of Gaza.

Make no mistake, history will judge each person that refused to act to end the ongoing genocide in Gaza. Hate is not the opposite of peace, apathy is. Apathy equals complicity. Those who don't want to be complicit to the genocide on the Palestinian people must demand the suspension of the EU-Israel Association Agreement now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a (PfE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, n脙拢o h脙隆 d脙潞vida nenhuma que grandes mudan脙搂as se passaram no M脙漏dio Oriente nestes 脙潞ltimos tempos.

Parte do cerco do terror foi quebrado. O Ir脙拢o vai conhecer uma transi脙搂脙拢o 脙聽 qual n脙拢o vai poder escapar e que vai ser preconizada pelos seus pr脙鲁prios cidad脙拢os. N脙拢o vai ser uma influ脙陋ncia externa, mas vai ser uma mudan脙搂a de dentro para fora no Ir脙拢o, por um lado. Por outro lado, os seus proxys, como os Hutis, como o Hamas, como o Hezbollah, v脙拢o deixar de ter a cama fant脙隆stica que tinham, com o Ir脙拢o a apoiar.

Meus caros amigos, 脙漏 tempo de mudan脙搂a, mas 脙漏 tempo de mudan脙搂a complicada porque temos a S脙颅ria tamb脙漏m, que tem uma situa脙搂脙拢o que n脙拢o 脙漏 f脙隆cil e n脙拢o vai ser f脙隆cil de resolver, em per脙颅odo quer de transi脙搂脙拢o, quer final.

Portanto, n脙鲁s vamos ter que estar muito atentos a toda esta evolu脙搂脙拢o. O quadro mudou. Os direitos humanos, como toda a gente aqui disse (n脙拢o vou falar mais sobre isso), est脙拢o em perigo, mas o que 脙漏 facto 脙漏 que toda a geopol脙颅tica na regi脙拢o mudou e vai ser muito problem脙隆tica.

(O orador recusa uma pergunta 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄 de Jo脙拢o Oliveira)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Dear colleague, do you accept another blue card from Mr脗聽Oliveira?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, no, because I was elected to the European Parliament, not to the Portuguese Parliament.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bernard Guetta (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, lorsque Benyamin Netanyahou dit que les Palestiniens devraient 脗芦avoir la capacit脙漏 de se gouverner eux-m脙陋mes, mais pas celle de nuire 脙聽 Isra脙芦l脗禄, il a raison. Sauf si cela signifie pour lui 芒聙聯脗聽et il y a tout lieu de le craindre脗聽芒聙聯 que les Palestiniens ne doivent pas disposer d芒聙聶un 脙聣tat. Ce peuple vivrait alors dans l芒聙聶ambition de se retrouver l芒聙聶ann脙漏e prochaine 脙聽 J脙漏rusalem, capitale de la Palestine. Rien ne le ferait renoncer 脙聽 ce r脙陋ve, pas m脙陋me deux脗聽mille ans d芒聙聶attente, et ce ne serait donc pas la paix qu芒聙聶il faut aujourd芒聙聶hui b脙垄tir.

Si les Isra脙漏liens savaient, en revanche, proposer aux Palestiniens de n脙漏gocier les conditions d芒聙聶une coexistence pacifique entre leurs deux 脙聣tats, la compl脙篓te victoire qu芒聙聶ils viennent de remporter leur permettrait d芒聙聶asseoir leur s脙漏curit脙漏 sur la noblesse de cette offre. Parce qu芒聙聶il en est le vainqueur, Isra脙芦l peut faire que cette nouvelle guerre soit la derni脙篓re, mais encore faudrait-il pour cela que ce Premier ministre sache enfin s芒聙聶en convaincre.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mounir Satouri (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Gaza, la Palestine, le Moyen-Orient, cela fait vingt脗聽et脗聽un mois que nous en parlons ici, encore, toujours. Nous avons parl脙漏 des preuves du g脙漏nocide en cours, nous avons parl脙漏 des logiques d芒聙聶apartheid, nous avons parl脙漏 de tous les 脙漏l脙漏ments qui constituent un nettoyage ethnique. Mais qu芒聙聶avons-nous fait?

Alors, pour une fois, je ne vais pas parler ici des enfants affam脙漏s, des h脙麓pitaux d脙漏truits, des civils tu脙漏s pendant la distribution de pain. Non, je vais parler de nous, de l芒聙聶Europe. Gaza est devenue le miroir de notre impuissance. Face aux errances de Trump nous nous terrons; face aux attaques contre la CPI nous ne d脙漏clenchons pas le processus de blocage; lorsqu芒聙聶il survole la Gr脙篓ce, la France ou l芒聙聶Italie, Netanyahou n芒聙聶est pas arr脙陋t脙漏. Ce qui nous fragilise, ce n芒聙聶est pas l芒聙聶absence d芒聙聶alli脙漏s. Nous pourrions avoir tout le Sud global align脙漏 avec nous. Ce qui nous fragilise, c芒聙聶est notre incapacit脙漏 脙聽 agir, 脙聽 faire appliquer le droit international, 脙聽 arr脙陋ter Benyamin脗聽Netanyahou et sa logique mortif脙篓re!

R脙漏veillez-vous 芒聙聯脗聽passez ce message 脙聽 Mme脗聽Kallas. Je le dis 脙聽 mes coll脙篓gues du PPE: r脙漏veillez-vous! Un continent qui veut sa s脙漏curit脙漏 sans sa souverainet脙漏 n芒聙聶aura ni l芒聙聶une ni l芒聙聶autre. L芒聙聶histoire vous jugera, et elle vous jugera s脙漏v脙篓rement!

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Arash Saeidi (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Chani, Noya, Carmela, Mansour, Niloufar, Mahsa, Abbad, Ahmad et tant de milliers d芒聙聶autres victimes civiles innocentes que je pleure toutes, quelle que soit leur nationalit脙漏, quelle que soit l芒聙聶identit脙漏 de leurs bourreaux. Qu芒聙聶elles aient p脙漏ri en Isra脙芦l des mains de terroristes, assassin脙漏es lors d芒聙聶une distribution alimentaire 脙聽 Gaza, r脙漏prim脙漏es par le r脙漏gime 脙聽 T脙漏h脙漏ran en se battant pour leur libert脙漏, ou, toujours 脙聽 T脙漏h脙漏ran, sous les bombes d芒聙聶un 脙聣tat agresseur qui n芒聙聶a jamais eu pour but de lib脙漏rer qui que ce soit, leurs vies, Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, Madame la Commissaire, leurs vies se valent toutes. Elles m脙漏ritent toutes notre empathie et notre indignation.

Madame la Commissaire, je m芒聙聶adresse solennellement 脙聽 vous. Les droits de l芒聙聶homme sont universels. Les invoquer parfois pour mieux les enterrer quand le bourreau se croit notre ami, c芒聙聶est faire preuve au mieux d芒聙聶hypocrisie et, sans doute, d脙漏j脙聽, de complicit脙漏.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elena Yoncheva (NI). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, in the Middle East, the crisis is spreading fast: from Palestine, to Lebanon, to Yemen, and now to Iran. People need urgent help, but peace feels farther away than ever.

This is already our sixth debate on the Middle East since our new mandate began last July. And honestly, it feels like we keep saying the same things. We call for peace, we ask for action, but very little changes.

Each time we meet, the situation gets worse, and each time our words stay the same. We are stuck in helpless debate while humanitarian help is very much needed, while lives are lost.

So I ask our High Representative, when will we finally see a real EU plan for peace? We need strategy. The European Union cannot just watch from the sidelines. You must do more. And we must do it now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Pascual de la Parte (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, the Middle East is enduring a change of geopolitics and of the geopolitical map, and one of the main elements for this change is to hold Iran accountable for its sponsorship of terrorism and destabilisation across the region.

We know that appeasement doesn't work with this regime, and we know as well that direct military intervention is not advisable either. What will work will be international isolation based upon the hardening of the sanctions against this criminal regime, as well as political and diplomatic pressure.

At the same time, we have to recognise and support the democratic opposition of Iran, which will be the only one that will put an end to this regime.

As far as Gaza is concerned, we reiterate our call for unhindered access of humanitarian aid to the Palestinian people, as well as a kind of future for them which has to be without Hamas. Hamas cannot be in the future political framework of the Palestinians. And to back the cease fire immediately.

Concerning my colleague Botenga from The Left, I must say that Spain never set up concentration camps in Cuba. He has to review his history books, because the communist regime of Fidel Castro and his followers has forced millions of Cubans out of his country and oppressed them and brought them misery and repression.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 I think you need to check your history, the Spanish colonial history and the fight with the United States. There were, at the end of the 19th century, concentration camps set up.

But this is okay 芒聙聯 you will check; let me ask you a real question.

Iran replied to an Israeli illegal attack by bombing Israel. It's easy to condemn Iran, as you have done time and time again. But over the last years, Israel has bombed, what is it, Syria, Lebanon, Iraq, Iran, Sudan, Yemen, of course Palestine, and is illegally occupying Palestine, parts of Syria and illegally occupying parts of Lebanon.

Are you able to condemn this? Or do you say 'no, I fully stand with Israeli violations of international law'? Do you have these double standards or do you adhere to international law?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Pascual de la Parte (PPE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Thank you, dear colleague, for your intervention.

It's totally unbalanced and untrue: Spain, again, never set up concentration camps in the modern time in Cuba. I mean, the real oppressors of Cuba are the communist regime under Fidel Castro and his successors. And you know that millions of Cubans have left the country because they cannot live in Cuba. These are the oppressors of Cuba: the communist regime that you represent.

The second point: I have not said anything about Israel. I have said that Iran is the head of the monster which will destabilise the entire region. And without this regime, we will probably leave the region and ourselves better off, and you as well.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, a situa脙搂脙拢o no M脙漏dio Oriente continua a devolver芒聙聭nos a imagem do desrespeito pela ordem internacional e pelo multilateralismo, mas tamb脙漏m a imagem do nosso fracasso coletivo.

No L脙颅bano, os termos do cessar芒聙聭fogo n脙拢o est脙拢o a ser respeitados e Israel realizou novos ataques a脙漏reos.

No Ir脙拢o, os bombardeamentos de Israel e dos Estados Unidos atrasaram o desenvolvimento de armas nucleares, provavelmente, mas levaram 脙聽 suspens脙拢o da coopera脙搂脙拢o com a Ag脙陋ncia Internacional de Energia At脙鲁mica e ao maior isolamento do pa脙颅s.

E em Gaza, onde mais de 20 ref脙漏ns israelitas permanecem nas m脙拢os do Hamas, continua o epicentro da maior trag脙漏dia humanit脙隆ria. Ap脙鲁s 33 meses de guerra, todos os dias sucumbem pessoas com fome. A UNICEF refere 112 crian脙搂as internadas por dia por subnutri脙搂脙拢o. Nas filas do acesso a ajuda humanit脙隆ria, agora nas m脙拢os da Funda脙搂脙拢o Humanit脙隆ria de Gaza, j脙隆 morreram 500 pessoas e foram feridas 4000 na disputa por alimentos.

Por isso, hoje, voltamos a fazer eco das vozes dos cidad脙拢os europeus que questionam as nossas institui脙搂脙碌es. E perguntamos a cada um dos ministros dos Neg脙鲁cios Estrangeiros dos nossos Estados-Membros que se v脙拢o reunir no pr脙鲁ximo dia 15 de julho: Senhores Ministros, que posi脙搂脙拢o v脙拢o tomar quando discutirem a quest脙拢o da suspens脙拢o do Acordo de Associa脙搂脙拢o?

(A oradora aceita responder a uma pergunta 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhora Deputada Marta Temido, em Gaza, Israel usa a fome como arma de guerra e assassina adultos e crian脙搂as em ponto de distribui脙搂脙拢o de comida e o pretexto s脙拢o os ref脙漏ns feitos pelo Hamas. Na Cisjord脙垄nia, n脙拢o h脙隆 ref脙漏ns feitos pelo Hamas. Israel persegue, desloca for脙搂adamente refugiados de campos de refugiados e assassina palestinianos com a sua pol脙颅tica dos colonatos.脗聽O que est脙隆 em curso 脙漏 um genoc脙颅dio, 脙漏 uma limpeza 脙漏tnica.

E enquanto isto acontece, a Uni脙拢o Europeia mant脙漏m o Acordo de Associa脙搂脙拢o; continua a financiar, atrav脙漏s dos fundos europeus da ci脙陋ncia, empresas israelitas de produ脙搂脙拢o de armas.

E a pergunta que lhe fa脙搂o, Senhora Deputada, 脙漏 se a senhora deputada se rev脙陋 nesta pol脙颅tica de cumplicidade da Uni脙拢o Europeia, ou se, pelo contr脙隆rio, entende que a Uni脙拢o Europeia devia tomar uma a脙搂脙拢o firme para pressionar Israel para p脙麓r fim ao genoc脙颅dio do povo palestiniano.

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D), Resposta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhor Deputado, estamos todos t脙拢o envergonhados com a recente postura da Uni脙拢o Europeia como o senhor deputado e os europeus sabem disso.
