脗聽脗聽Cristian Terhe脜聼 (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, an old Arab proverb says: listen more than you speak, for the ears are closer to the brain than the mouth.脗聽This wisdom is essential as we confront the complex realities of the Middle East today.

We need to hear the United Arab Emirates, a persistent supporter of humanitarian aid in Gaza, a prosperous economy and an inspiration of tolerance in the region.

We must acknowledge Israel's right to self芒聙聭defence and its legitimate security concerns in a region full of threats. We cannot ignore the grave danger that we all face when Iran openly pursues nuclear weapons with the explicit goal of wiping Israel off the map.

We must stand with Middle Eastern Christian communities, many of whom face existential threats of genocide or cultural cleansing by extremists.

And we must not forget the everyday Palestinians, people who seek to live in peace, not under the rule of a terrorist organisation like Hamas.

The Abraham Accords offered a concrete roadmap towards peace through truth and cooperation. Now, it's time for the EU to listen with the intent, to act with courage and to be part of a path through peace, through prosperity for all in the Middle East.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脙聳zlem Demirel (The Left). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! 21脗聽Monate ethnische S脙陇uberung脗聽芒聙聯 w脙陇hrend in Gaza die Tr脙录mmer noch rauchen, sterben die Menschen nicht nur durch Bomben, sondern auch an Hunger, Durst und der systematischen Zerst脙露rung. Wer versucht, Hilfsg脙录ter zu bekommen, wird erschossen脗聽芒聙聯 nicht versehentlich, sondern gezielt. Es ist eine Politik der Zerm脙录rbung, der Vertreibung, der Unmenschlichkeit. Nun planen die USA Lager脗聽芒聙聯 angeblich humanit脙陇r, aber in Wahrheit Orte des Abtransports.

Das Ziel ist klar: Pal脙陇stina soll von der Landkarte verschwinden. Die ultrarechten Zionisten wollen ein Gro脙聼israel und eskalieren die Lage im kompletten Nahen Osten. In Deutschland vergie脙聼t man Krokodilstr脙陇nen, doch liefert Waffen. Man spricht vom Frieden, aber schreibt Narrative, die Unterdr脙录ckung legitimieren.

Und der Kanzler sagt: Israel erledigt unsere Drecksarbeit. Gemeint d脙录rfte sein, das Ziel der gesamten Neuordnung des gesamten Nahen Ostens. Aber diese Ordnung, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, wird auf Sand gebaut sein. H脙陇nde weg vom Nahen Osten!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, I want to reiterate when we talk about the Middle East, we have always have to remember the most significant threat in the region stems unmistakably from Iran and its network of proxies like Hamas, Hezbollah and the Houthis. While Israel's pre-emptive strikes disrupted Tehran's operational capacity, the mullah regime, of course, will seek ways to continue destabilising the whole neighbourhood. And we saw it yesterday: the latest missiles were fired by the Houthis against Israel, and some weeks ago, Iran itself fired missiles on a major American base in Qatar.

What we need is to push for diplomatic, political and economic initiatives to help to stabilise the region, like the European Commission is doing it, with the negotiations for a free trade agreement with the UAE. This is a good example of how we should act as a European Union. But the real threat is Tehran.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Van Brempt (S&D). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, collega芒聙聵s, duizend. Minstens. Dat is het aantal slachtoffers in Gaza, sinds 26脗聽juni 2025, de dag waarop de Commissie en de Raad het conflict in het Midden-Oosten bespraken. Duizend levens en het merendeel daarvan vrouwen en kinderen. Met duizenden en duizenden levens die vandaag verder op het spel staan door de honger en ontbering die de regering Netanyahu bewust cre脙芦ert en organiseert.

In Europa ligt er een rapport dat ons toelaat om druk uit te oefenen, om sancties te nemen en het geweld te doen stoppen. Maar er kwam niets. Wel, het is te zeggen, een brief van de regeringsleiders om vriendelijk te vragen humanitaire hulp toe te laten. Nog geen dag later, nog geen dag later, stopt Netanyahu de voedselbedeling in Noord-Gaza.

Het gebrek aan actie vanuit Europa is niet alleen intriest, het is een schandvlek. Het is onverklaarbaar, onverdedigbaar en onverantwoord. Hoe lang nog, Commissie? Nog duizend doden? Tweeduizend misschien?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fabrice Leggeri (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Commissaire, cela fait aujourd芒聙聶hui six cent quarante et un jours que des otages isra脙漏liens sont retenus par le Hamas 芒聙聯脗聽six cent quarante et un脗聽jours d芒聙聶attente, de souffrance, de silence pour des familles abandonn脙漏es par la communaut脙漏 internationale. Pendant que les n脙漏gociations pi脙漏tinent 脙聽 Doha, Isra脙芦l continue de d脙漏fendre sa population, seul face 脙聽 une coalition islamiste 芒聙聯脗聽Hamas, Hezbollah, houthistes, qui sont tous financ脙漏s et arm脙漏s par l芒聙聶Iran.

Ce que nous voyons, c芒聙聶est une entreprise strat脙漏gique, m脙漏thodique, men脙漏e depuis T脙漏h脙漏ran pour encercler, affaiblir et, 脙聽 terme, d脙漏truire la seule d脙漏mocratie stable de la r脙漏gion. Ce n芒聙聶est pas un conflit local, c芒聙聶est une guerre hybride, r脙漏gionale, id脙漏ologique: une guerre contre l芒聙聶Occident. Pendant ce temps, l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne joue les 脙漏quilibristes. Elle distribue des aides humanitaires qui tombent trop souvent entre de mauvaises mains. Elle condamne les violences sans jamais nommer les agresseurs. Elle demande 脙聽 Isra脙芦l, victime d芒聙聶un pogrom le 7脗聽octobre, de faire preuve de retenue.

Mais Isra脙芦l fait ce que nous n芒聙聶avons jamais eu le courage de faire: combattre l芒聙聶islamisme sans na脙炉vet脙漏, sans double langage. Il est notre rempart. Il est notre alli脙漏 naturel dans cette guerre de civilisation que beaucoup refusent encore de voir. Il est temps d芒聙聶assumer notre camp: celui des d脙漏mocraties, celui de la fermet脙漏, celui de la libert脙漏.

脗聽脗聽Marco Squarta (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il 7脗聽ottobre ha segnato una ferita profonda: Israele 脙篓 stata colpita da un attacco sanguinoso, vigliacco.

Hamas 脙篓 un'organizzazione terroristica che va smantellata, cos脙卢 come ogni forza che sogna di cancellare Israele dalla carta geografica.

Ma non possiamo ignorare altre immagini: bambini palestinesi senza vita, madri tra le macerie, ragazzi mutilati, affamati, corpi accatastati e cibo distribuito come se la dignit脙聽 fosse un lusso. Non si pu脙虏 restare indifferenti di fronte a tutto questo.

L'Europa ha il dovere morale di alzare la voce, agire e mediare. Chiedo a Israele: sospenda subito i bombardamenti. Chiedo ad Hamas: liberi subito gli ostaggi. Fermiamo questa spirale che uccide innocenti.

Solo due popoli con due Stati potranno guardarsi non pi脙鹿 con paura, ma con rispetto. Solo cos脙卢 ogni madre, israeliana o palestinese, potr脙聽 smettere di temere che il futuro del proprio figlio sia una tomba.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ilaria Salis (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, everyday in Gaza and across Palestine, people are injured, killed, displaced. Land is stolen. Humanity denied. Life suppressed.脗聽How did we get to this point? And what is our responsibility?

This is not just a matter of international politics. It is also a matter of political economy, of capital, greed and profit.脗聽Private corporations actively fuel and profit from the Zionist settler colonial project, the occupation and the ongoing genocide. This is the key message of the latest UN report by Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese.

A powerful capitalistic machine of profit is feeding destruction. Arms manufacturers like Lockheed Martin, Leonardo and Thales supply the weapons and fuel military escalation. Tech giants such as Google, Amazon and Meta enable mass surveillance and repression with no ethical restraint. Construction and machinery firms like Caterpillar and Volvo deliver the bulldozers that demolish Palestinian homes.

Ladies and gentlemen, this is a coordinated system of profit. This is racial colonial capitalism. Genocide continues because it is profitable. But we have a duty. The European Union must suspend the EU-Israel Association Agreement now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alice Teodorescu M脙楼we (PPE). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Just nu f脙露rs avg脙露rande samtal om en m脙露jlig vapenvila mellan Israel och Hamas. Frisl脙陇ppandet av gisslan som Hamas h脙楼llit i 脙露ver 600 dagar m脙楼ste vara h脙露gsta prioritet.

Om vi vill undvika ett nytt sjunde oktober m脙楼ste Hamas, vars m脙楼l inte 脙陇r fred utan Israels utpl脙楼ning, besegras milit脙陇rt och ideologiskt. Det palestinska folket f脙露rtj脙陇nar b脙陇ttre 脙陇n ett liv under ett brutalt terrorstyre. Trots risk f脙露r sina egna liv har m脙楼nga modiga Gazabor b脙露rjat protestera mot Hamas f脙露rtryck. Det 脙陇r dessa r脙露ster som vi m脙楼ste st脙露tta.

En varaktig fred kr脙陇ver tv脙楼 stater, men bara om b脙楼da staterna erk脙陇nner varandras r脙陇tt att existera. F脙露ruts脙陇ttningarna f脙露r fred b脙露rjar inte i vapenvilan. De b脙露rjar med viljan till samexistens.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lucia Annunziata (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, insieme alla proposta di Trump come candidato al premio Nobel per la pace, abbiamo finalmente saputo anche qual 脙篓 il progetto con cui si presenta con la pace.

A una cena alla Casa Bianca ha parlato dei palestinesi, dicendo che nel loro futuro c'脙篓 un trasferimento sicuro, ma solo volontario. Netanyahu ha poi fatto capire a cosa serve questo trasferimento, perch脙漏 "ogni Stato palestinese sarebbe una piattaforma per Israele".

脙聢 arrivato niente dai paesi arabi? Niente. In realt脙聽, questi sono piani, colleghi, che non hanno nessuna possibilit脙聽 di essere portati a termine. Tutta la strategia americana, a cominciare dal 1990, cio脙篓 dalla prima guerra in Iraq, 脙篓 stato il fallimento dei tentativi di cambi di regime 芒聙聯 ogni cambio di regime 芒聙聯 semplicemente perch脙漏 nemmeno grandi potenze come gli USA, o regionali come Israele, hanno forze e uomini a sufficienza per poterlo fare.

Non bastarono 500脗聽000 uomini in Iraq a organizzare un cambio di regime, tanto meno basteranno a Israele i suoi soldati, sia pur messi con quelli americani, contro 80脗聽milioni di sciiti.

Come vedete, questi piani non sono realistici: non dovrebbe essere molto difficile trovare un modo per metterli in discussione.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tonino Picula (S&D). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, kao 脜隆to se svijet po脛聧eo nepovratno mijenjati 11.脗聽rujna脗聽2001. tako je i Hamasov teroristi脛聧ki napad na Izrael 7.脗聽listopada脗聽2023. pokrenuo lanac doga脛聭aja koji radikalno mijenja odnose na Bliskom istoku. Netanyahu poti脛聧e sukobe kako bi transformirao sam Izrael, uspostavljaju脛聡i dominaciju nad 脜隆irim prostorom. Obra脛聧unava se s cijelim palestinskim narodom, ne samo s Hamasovim teroristima.

Paralelno, razina unutarnje represije u Iranu oja脛聧ala je nakon vanjske intervencije, dovode脛聡i re脜戮im na rub. Ali mo脜戮e li Europska unija uop脛聡e iza脛聡i iz permanentne duboke zabrinutosti zbog situacije na Bliskom istoku jer ni ranije nije mogla dogovoriti zajedni脛聧ke stavove? Europska unija kao 芒聙聻soft power芒聙聺 o脛聧ito ne uspijeva posredovati u sukobu 芒聙聻hard powers芒聙聺.

Europska unija razmatra raspolo脜戮ive mjere, uklju脛聧uju脛聡i mogu脛聡u suspenziju Sporazuma o stabilizaciji i pridru脜戮ivanju izme脛聭u Europske unije i Izraela. Bliski istok je na kriti脛聧noj prekretnici koja zahtijeva odlu脛聧nije djelovanje. Na脜隆a uloga u postizanju JCPOA芒聙聭a dokazuje da Europska unija, kada je ujedinjena, mo脜戮e posti脛聡i ozbiljne globalne diplomatske uspjehe.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thijs Reuten (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I stand here rather desperate, to be honest, especially脗聽listening to the Commission and Council. But let's try one more time to find unity on one point 芒聙聯 on humanitarian values, empathy and respect for the lives of innocent people. We here should, as a minimum, agree to the need to open Gaza for immediate and unrestricted access to humanitarian aid, food and medical supplies into Gaza by independent, internationally respected organisations.

This Parliament, it seems, and for sure a large majority of our citizens want this. Our citizens have lost respect for Europe's inaction over Gaza and Europe's double standards. There are no excuses. After all the well-documented violations of international law, the lack of a recommendation to suspend all or part of the agreement is a violation of the Commission's role as guardian of the Treaties, and the High Representative / Vice President knows that. Equally, the Council's lack of action is inexplicable and undermines protection of rules-based international order. We should find the political resolve to ensure that Europe acts now to open Gaza.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Chlo脙漏 Ridel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, parfois je me demande: 脗芦Dans quel monde vit-on?脗禄 Pendant que Benyamin脗聽Netanyahou nomme Donald脗聽Trump au prix脗聽Nobel de la paix, pendant que ces deux-l脙聽 sont en ce moment m脙陋me 脙聽 Washington et qu芒聙聶ils discutent 脙聽 nouveau du transfert forc脙漏 de Palestiniens hors de la bande de Gaza, 脙聽 Gaza le cauchemar perdure: les bombardements, la faim, la soif, la mort.

Depuis que l芒聙聶aide humanitaire est g脙漏r脙漏e par la Gaza Humanitarian Foundation, au moins 613脗聽Palestiniens ont 脙漏t脙漏 tu脙漏s lors de distributions d芒聙聶aide. Cette d脙漏shumanisation d芒聙聶une population en d脙漏tresse est insupportable. Des soldats isra脙漏liens sortent du silence pour dire l芒聙聶horreur de la guerre. Ils expriment leur sentiment de trahison face 脙聽 Netanyahou. Yotan, pilote de char, t脙漏moigne: 脗芦Cette guerre me hante. Personne ne nous avait dit les intentions du gouvernement, le nettoyage ethnique de la bande de Gaza pour s芒聙聶y r脙漏installer ensuite.脗禄 Par vengeance et par messianisme, Netanyahou s脙篓me la terreur sans ouvrir aucune perspective politique de justice et de paix pour la r脙漏gion. Encore aujourd芒聙聶hui, 脙聽 Washington, il a refus脙漏 la cr脙漏ation d芒聙聶un 脙聣tat palestinien et le retrait d芒聙聶Isra脙芦l de la bande de Gaza 脙聽 terme.

Alors, le 15脗聽juillet, les chefs d芒聙聶脙聣tat europ脙漏ens ont rendez-vous avec l芒聙聶histoire. Ils peuvent s芒聙聶afficher complices d芒聙聶un criminel de guerre ou ils peuvent suspendre l芒聙聶accord d芒聙聶association entre l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne et Isra脙芦l. Ils peuvent encore montrer qu芒聙聶ils d脙漏fendent la solution 脙聽 deux 脙聣tats, pour deux peuples vivant c脙麓te 脙聽 c脙麓te en 脙漏gales libert脙漏 et s脙漏curit脙漏.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, people in Gaza and in the Middle East are still being killed every day in hospitals, in refugee centres and in the lines for humanitarian aid.

We may have now narrowly avoided the escalation last month in the Israel-Iran conflict into a full-scale war, but the region remains far from peace. The people are still suffering with very little hope. A ceasefire must be achieved now.

The review of the EU-Israel Association Agreement confirmed violation of Article脗聽2. Next week the EU foreign ministers must urgently come to a decision on concrete measures: suspend the agreement; sanction those responsible for war crimes; stop trade with illegal West Bank settlements; impose an arms embargo on Israel; ban Israel accessing EU funds and programmes; and protect international law and institutions.

Europe must act now. If not, EU leadership and institutions, including our foreign ministers, will be criminally responsible for their complicity under international law. Not in our name.

(The speaker declined to take a blue-card question from Petras Gra脜戮ulis)

脗聽脗聽Aodh脙隆n 脙聯 R脙颅ord脙隆in (S&D). 芒聙聯 I dt脙潞s b脙隆ire, n脙颅 m脙鲁r don Ardionada脙颅 Kallas, leithsc脙漏al a ghabh脙隆il as an m脙漏id a d脙潞irt s脙颅 le gairid 脙鲁 thaobh stair na h脙聣ireann de. D脙潞irt s脙颅 nach ionann an taith脙颅 at脙隆 againne agus na t脙颅ortha san Iarthar, iad si脙潞d a chaill a gcult脙潞r agus a dteangacha. N脙颅 m脙鲁r di staid脙漏ar a dh脙漏anamh ar stair na h脙聣ireann agus ceistigh c脙漏n f脙隆th a labhra脙颅tear B脙漏arla in 脙聣irinn agus c脙漏n f脙隆th a bhfuil na milli脙潞in daoine le sloinnte 脙聣ireannacha ar fud na cruinne mar thoradh ar fhor脙漏igean, ocras agus bochtanas, a Ardionada脙颅.

As Irish people, such as many others, we know what it means to have your language, your culture and your very identity suppressed, we know what it means to live under occupation, to have foreign powers dictate your fate. This is why we have a deep sense of solidarity with Palestinian people and why we stand here calling for action from the EU. When EU ministers meet on 15脗聽July, we must scrap the EU-Israel Association Agreement and we must sanction Israel now.

Saoirse don Phalaist脙颅n!

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lynn Boylan (The Left). 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, we're now at a point where gunning down hungry people as they desperately try to get aid is a daily occurrence. There was international shock the first time, but just like with the bombing of the first hospital, when there are no consequences for Israel, they are emboldened and they carry on.

700 Palestinians have now been slaughtered at aid sites; baby formula is blocked. They are literally starving babies to death, and still the EU does not act. In fact, instead of holding Israel to account, President von der Leyen has Netanyahu, a war criminal, on speed dial, and she's actively encouraging him to carry out Illegal strikes on Iran. There is more outrage from EU leaders about artists calling out a genocide than there is against those who are actually carrying out that genocide.

Solidarity with Palestine is being criminalised and the EU is sacrificing its civil liberties to support Israel. But Palestinian solidarity grows stronger every day. The people won't be silenced. The people are demanding suspension of trade with Israel. The people are demanding sanctions against Israel. The people are demanding an end to their complicity with Israel's war crime and its genocide, and they are demanding that their EU leaders act and listen to them.

脗聽脗聽Jean-Marc Germain (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, le cessez-le-feu entre l芒聙聶Iran et Isra脙芦l est un soulagement, mais il ne doit pas d脙漏tourner notre regard du Moyen-Orient, 脙聽 commencer par Gaza, o脙鹿 les morts civiles doivent cesser, o脙鹿 les destructions doivent cesser, o脙鹿 la guerre de Netanyahou doit cesser, o脙鹿 la paix durable doit arriver. L芒聙聶Europe, on doit aussi l芒聙聶entendre sur la r脙漏pression sanguinaire en Iran. 脙聙 la suite du conflit, trois opposants politiques ont 脙漏t脙漏 pendus, et des centaines d芒聙聶arrestations ont eu lieu, dont huit d芒聙聶Europ脙漏ens. Un Fran脙搂ais de 18脗聽ans est port脙漏 disparu: il pourrait avoir 脙漏t脙漏 pris en otage. Pour la vingtaine d芒聙聶otages europ脙漏ens en d脙漏tention, la situation s芒聙聶est encore aggrav脙漏e. J芒聙聶脙漏tais hier et avant-hier avec les familles de deux otages, C脙漏cile脗聽Kohler et Jacques脗聽Paris, emprisonn脙漏s dans des conditions inhumaines. Ils sont 脙聽 bout! Depuis la guerre, leur lieu de d脙漏tention est inconnu et le r脙漏gime a ajout脙漏 脙聽 la torture psychologique la menace de la peine de mort sous des chefs d芒聙聶inculpation infond脙漏s. Amnesty International alerte aussi sur le risque d芒聙聶ex脙漏cution imminente de leur cod脙漏tenu su脙漏dois, Ahmadreza脗聽Djalali.

Alors, Madame la Commissaire, je demande 脙聽 l芒聙聶ex脙漏cutif europ脙漏en de mobiliser tous les leviers possibles pour obtenir la lib脙漏ration imm脙漏diate des otages europ脙漏ens en Iran.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, what would a better version of the Middle East look like?脗聽Hezbollah that is a shadow of itself. The Assad regime gone. Iran severely weakened.

This is already a reality. For the first time in a long time, there is genuine hope. What else we would need for a better future for the Middle East is the end of Hamas.

The EU must be careful to not undermine this goal. Israel is aiming for peace through strength. We cannot be pushing for a Palestinian state until the terrorist entity is removed from power.

Recognising a Palestinian state now would be an endorsement of terror. The intentions might be good, but the result will be catastrophic. Instead, we must demand that Hamas disarms, releases the hostages and surrenders.

脗聽脗聽Jaume Asens Llodr脙聽 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, dentro de unos a脙卤os las nuevas generaciones nos van a preguntar qu脙漏 hicimos mientras en directo se estaba produciendo un genocidio en Gaza.

Hoy en Espa脙卤a se ha admitido una querella contra Netanyahu por el asalto violento a la Flotilla de la Libertad. Somos muchos 芒聙聰y estoy orgulloso de ser abogado芒聙聰 los que no vamos a parar hasta que los responsables tengan que responder ante la justicia y ante la historia. Y ustedes est脙隆n en esa lista.

脗聽脗聽Irene Montero (The Left). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, Israel contin脙潞a con su plan de exterminio y, como los nazis, est脙隆 ejecutando su soluci脙鲁n final para Palestina. Dos a脙卤os de genocidio y, ahora, sobre las ruinas de R脙隆fah, va a construir un campo de concentraci脙鲁n. Esto en Varsovia se llamaba 脗芦gueto脗禄, el terror nazi, se脙卤or脙颅as.

脗聽脗聽Cecilia Strada (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, anch'io volevo fare un paio di precisazioni.

E va fatto non per bont脙聽, per carit脙聽 o per potersi guardare allo specchio senza vergogna 芒聙聯 cosa che comunque 脙篓 molto importante 芒聙聯 va fatto perch脙漏 脙篓 un obbligo di comportamento, che deriva dal diritto internazionale: non cooperare con chi sta commettendo queste cose. Je脜聸li wierzymy w prawa cz脜聜owieka, musimy g脜聜o脜聸no pot脛聶pi脛聡 te prze脜聸ladowania i domaga脛聡 si脛聶 ochrony dla chrze脜聸cijan. Milczenie czyni nas wsp脙鲁脜聜winnymi tej barbarzy脜聞skiej przemocy.

脗聽脗聽Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, huele a sangre, huele de verdad a muerte, pero, sobre todo, huele a hipocres脙颅a. Construir una ciudad en las ruinas de R脙隆fah para encerrar a 600脗聽000脗聽gazat脙颅es ya es la 脙潞ltima invenci脙鲁n de Netanyahu, otro elemento m脙隆s que configura la definici脙鲁n de genocidio. 脗驴Qu脙漏 m脙隆s pruebas de genocidio queremos?, 脗驴qu脙漏 m脙隆s pruebas quiere la Uni脙鲁n Europea?, 脗驴por qu脙漏 no est脙隆 hoy la se脙卤ora Kallas aqu脙颅?, 脗驴por qu脙漏 no se puede cambiar este sistema de ayuda humanitaria de Naciones Unidas, que Netanyahu y Trump quieren cambiar por un negocio?

Se脙卤ora comisaria, el negocio de Trump y Netanyahu con la ayuda humanitaria y el traslado es precisamente para destruir el sistema de las Naciones Unidas y el sistema del UNRWA. No lo dice la eurodiputada del BNG, lo dice la se脙卤ora Albanese, que es la relatora especial sobre Palestina. Y le digo al nuevo Consejo, que est脙隆 hoy sentado aqu脙颅, que tomen medidas el 15 de julio: el Consejo de la Uni脙鲁n Europea y la Comisi脙鲁n no pueden seguir siendo c脙鲁mplices.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jessika Roswall, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, the European Union will actively continue to contribute to all diplomatic efforts aimed at deescalating tensions and achieving a lasting solution.

We stand ready to engage constructively with Iran, remaining firm in our expectation and united in our resolve. A sustainable and credible resolution to the Iranian nuclear issue is only possible through a comprehensive, verifiable and durable agreement.

We are also committed to find ways to improve the humanitarian situation in Gaza and, together with the foreign affairs ministers, we will assess the way forward at the next Foreign Affairs Council on the 15脗聽July. With that, I thank you very much for the debate.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioner, the European Union reaffirms its commitment to regional peace and stability.

We urge continued restraint and adherence to international law and we renew our commitment to all diplomatic efforts to bring about a lasting solution to the Iranian nuclear issue. We will do so in coordination with our international partners, as we have recently done in the context of the G7.

Your interventions today were clear about the need to bring an end to the humanitarian catastrophe in Gaza as a matter of urgency. In order to do so, the European Union will continue to support the efforts of the mediators to reach a sustainable ceasefire.

Furthermore, you can rest assured that the council will follow up on the reviews of Israel's compliance with Article 2 of the EU芒聙聭Israel Association Agreement. The matter will be on the agenda of the next Foreign Affairs Council next week.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 In Bezug auf die vom INTA芒聙聭Ausschuss vorgelegte Empfehlung f脙录r einen Beschluss, keine Einw脙陇nde gegen einen delegierten Rechtsakt zu erheben, die am Montag, den 7.脗聽Juli, im Plenum bekannt gegeben wurde, wurden innerhalb der Frist von 24脗聽Stunden keine Einw脙陇nde erhoben. Gem脙陇脙聼 Artikel脗聽114 Absatz脗聽6 gilt die Empfehlung somit als angenommen, und sie wird mit den angenommenen Texten ver脙露ffentlicht.

脗聽

19. Corrigenda (Rule 251) (action taken)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Gem脙陇脙聼 Artikel脗聽251 Absatz脗聽4 der Gesch脙陇ftsordnung m脙露chte ich Ihnen mitteilen, dass zu der Berichtigung, die dem Plenum am Montag, den 7.脗聽Juli, bei der Er脙露ffnung der Sitzung bekannt gegeben wurde, kein Antrag auf Abstimmung gestellt wurde. Die Berichtigung gilt somit als angenommen und wird auf der Website der Plenartagung ver脙露ffentlicht.

脗聽

20. Situation in Belarus, in particular the release of political prisoners (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rungen des Rates und der Kommission zur Lage in Belarus, insbesondere zur Freilassung von politischen Gefangenen (2025/2789(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioners, let me start my intervention by reaffirming the EU's steadfast commitment to supporting the people of Belarus in the legitimate aspirations for a free, democratic, sovereign and independent country.

Following the fraudulent presidential election of August 2020, the Belarusian authorities have pursued a widespread campaign of persecution against all segments of society, including civil society organisations, independent media, human rights defenders and peaceful protesters. The continued crackdown on dissenting voices is unacceptable and must end. The recent release of several political prisoners, including Siarhei Tsikhanouski and those announced on 2脗聽July, are positive steps after prolonged injustice. At the same time, they must not overshadow the broader picture. More than 1脗聽100 individuals remain arbitrarily detained for political reasons. Many are reportedly held in degrading and inhumane conditions, with limited access to legal counsel, medical care or communication with their families.

This is why I state again here today the EU's call for the immediate and unconditional release of all political prisoners. The Belarusian authorities must end the practice of arbitrary detention and must ensure the full respect for the human rights of all detainees, in line with the Belarus international obligations, including under the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights.

Beyond international repression, we remain gravely concerned by Belarus's continued involvement in Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine, including through logistical and political support. Since 2021, Belarus has also been instrumentalising migration for political purposes to put pressure on the EU's external borders, a practice we have consistently condemned. In response to these developments, the EU has adopted restrictive measures, both individual and sectoral, targeting those responsible for serious human rights violations, undermining of democratic principles and the rule of law, and Belarus's complicity in the war against Ukraine.

Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioners, the Council will remain fully engaged on the situation in Belarus. The European Union will continue providing substantial support to Belarusian civil society, independent media and all those working towards a peaceful and democratic future for Belarus. We will continue to monitor developments, to apply pressure when necessary, and to support the resilience of Belarusian society. The European Union will not spare its efforts to stand by the Belarusian people in their struggle for freedom, dignity and self-determination.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Madam Minister, we are together in the European Parliament for the first time, distinguished Members of the European Parliament, two weeks ago, it was a happy day. Siarhei Tsikhanouski and several other Belarusian and European citizens regained their freedom and were united with their families. And last week, another 16 individuals were pardoned by the Belarusian authorities.

These releases are part of a broader pattern that has seen over 300 political prisoners freed since July last year. These steps, while welcome, are not enough. Belarus still detains approximately 1脗聽150 political prisoners, among them around 300 individuals in highly vulnerable situations 芒聙聯 people suffering from serious health conditions, elderly detainees, persons with disabilities 芒聙聯 all held under arbitrary charges.

Siarhei Tsikhanouski's description of his detention conditions are absolutely appalling. Like him, countless political prisoners endure inhumane conditions: solitary confinement, torture, denial of medical care and incommunicado detention.

Let us be clear: if Belarus intends to signal real and lasting change, it must first release all political prisoners immediately and unconditionally, repeal repressive laws, especially the so-called extremism provisions, and end politically motivated prosecutions and new arbitrary arrests. Third, guarantee fair trials, transparent proceedings, and respect for every detainee's fundamental rights. Fourth, eliminate torture, stop incommunicado detention, ensure proper access to medical care, and end all forms of discrimination and abuse against political prisoners. The regime must also stop its complicity with Russia in its war of aggression against Ukraine.

Honourable Members, since the fraudulent elections of August 2020, tens of thousands of Belarusians have faced brutal repression. Belarus has also become an accomplice in Russia's war against Ukraine 芒聙聯 a war that Belarusian people do not support.

The European Union has been steadfast in its response. In March, we adopted the latest package of sanctions on Belarus, targeting those responsible for human rights violations against the Belarusian people and for the so-called presidential elections of January. The EU has also continued to raise awareness on the situation of political prisoners and urge their immediate and unconditional release. We have also opened doors to Belarusians fleeing persecution; over 400脗聽000 now live in the EU.

We continue to support accountability efforts, leading resolutions at the Human Rights Council and supporting the International Accountability Platform for Belarus. We have provided support to Belarusian civil society human rights defenders, including via financial assistance to victims and families of political prisoners.脗聽In December last year, we reaffirmed our commitment with a new contribution of EUR脗聽30 million.脗聽This brings our overall support to EUR脗聽170 million since 2020.

We call upon all Belarusian civil society to join the efforts and intensify their joint engagement to achieve long and lasting changes. We will continue to be united and determined in exercising pressure on the regime and demanding justice for the crimes committed. We will also continue our solidarity with Belarusian society, including by welcoming Belarusian citizens, especially youth and students, to the EU.

And we will work together with you, dear Members of the European Parliament and other public and non-governmental organisations, to help the people of Belarus realise their rightful place as a free and democratic nation in a peaceful Europe. Thank you for your support for Belarus.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrzej Halicki, w imieniu grupy PPE. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Pani Komisarz! Pani Minister! Presja ma sens. Rzeczywi脜聸cie, 21 czerwca prze脜录yli脜聸my bardzo wa脜录ny moment, kiedy Siarhiej Cichanouski do脜聜脛聟czy脜聜 do rodziny, do swojej dzielnej ma脜聜脜录onki, prezydent Cichanouskiej, do swoich dzieci. Razem z trzynastoma innymi wi脛聶脜潞niami politycznymi opu脜聸ci脜聜 脜聜agry. Nie z脜聜amali go.

Ale w 脜聜agrach i w bia脜聜oruskich wi脛聶zieniach s脛聟 jeszcze tysi脛聟ce wi脛聶脜潞ni脙鲁w politycznych. Dyktator walczy ze swoim narodem, zw脜聜aszcza z tymi, kt脙鲁rzy maj脛聟 odwag脛聶 wypowiadania si脛聶. T脜聜amsi wolno脜聸脛聡 s脜聜owa. W脜聸r脙鲁d tych, kt脙鲁rzy s脛聟 w wi脛聶zieniu, jest dziennikarz, Polak, Andrzej Poczobut. Upominamy si脛聶 o uwolnienie ich wszystkich i b脛聶dziemy o tym g脜聜o脜聸no m脙鲁wi脛聡, i g脜聜o脜聸no walczy脛聡 o ich wolno脜聸脛聡.

Ostatnio tak脜录e zaatakowa脜聜 wsp脙鲁lnoty wyznaniowe, zburzy脜聜 ko脜聸ci脙鲁脜聜 protestancki 芒聙聻Nowe 脜录ycie芒聙聺, zabra脜聜 wsp脙鲁lnocie katolickiej Ko脜聸ci脙鲁脜聜 Czerwony w Mi脜聞sku. Katolickiego ksi脛聶dza Henryka Oko脜聜otowicza skaza脜聜 na 12 lat wi脛聶zienia pod pretekstem szpiegowania na rzecz Watykanu. Czy jest co脜聸 bardziej absurdalnego? 脜聛ukaszenka si脛聶 boi, ale jest odpowiedzialny za zbrodnie i stanie przed Mi脛聶dzynarodowym Trybuna脜聜em Karnym. A wam, Bia脜聜orusini, chc脛聶 powiedzie脛聡 tylko jedno: widzimy was, jeste脜聸my z wami. Miejcie wiar脛聶 i nadziej脛聶. Bo presja ma sens, bo wiara jest wa脜录na dla Waszej to脜录samo脜聸ci, a nadzieja to wolna i niepodleg脜聜a Bia脜聜oru脜聸. Ona b脛聶dzie. Ona b脛聶dzie. 脜禄ywie Bie脜聜aru脜聸.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Robert Biedro脜聞, w imieniu grupy S&D. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Ale脜聸 Bialacki, Maryja Kalesnikowa, Ihar 脜聛osik, Wiktor Babaryka, Andrzej Poczobut: to tylko kilka nazwisk z listy ponad 1150 wi脛聶脜潞ni脙鲁w politycznych, kt脙鲁rych brutalny re脜录im 脜聛ukaszenki nadal nielegalnie trzyma, zamkni脛聶tych w nieludzkich warunkach. Kilka kilometr脙鲁w od granicy z Uni脛聟 Europejsk脛聟 re脜录im 脜聛ukaszenki utrzymuje nadal ostatnie w Europie kolonie karne, torturuje ludzi, gwa脜聜ci kobiety w 2025 roku.

Dlatego my jako Unia Europejska nie mo脜录emy zapomnie脛聡 o najbardziej zapomnianym narodzie Europy, jak m脙鲁wi Swiat脜聜ana Cichanouska. Nie mo脜录emy sta脛聡 bezczynnie. Dlatego apeluj脛聶 do Wysokiej Przedstawiciel Kai Kallas, do wszystkich ministr脙鲁w spraw zagranicznych kraj脙鲁w Unii Europejskiej: b脛聟d脜潞my solidarni z Bia脜聜orusinami i Bia脜聜orusinkami. Bia脜聜oru脜聸 jest naszym s脛聟siadem. Bia脜聜oru脜聸 jest cz脛聶脜聸ci脛聟 Europy. Tak d脜聜ugo, jak Bia脜聜oru脜聸 nie b脛聶dzie wolna, Europa nie b脛聶dzie wolna. 脜禄ywie Bie脜聜aru脜聸!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Bielan, on behalf of the ECR Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Commissioner, we all remember the rigged presidential elections in Belarus in 2020 and the wave of peaceful demonstrations that followed.脗聽The regime responded, not with dialogue, but with brutal repression. Since then, we've seen the dismantling of civil society, attacks on religious freedom and mass repression.

The recent release of 14 political prisoners, including Siarhei Tsikhanouski, facilitated by the United States, is a positive step. But let us be clear: it is not enough. Over 1脗聽100 political prisoners, including Andrzej Poczobut, remain behind bars, many in harsh, inhumane conditions, facing torture, isolation and medical neglect.

My group calls on the EU and its Member States to hold the Lukashenka regime accountable for its crimes. The release of a few must not distract us from the continued suffering of many. There can be no normalisation without genuine democratic reforms and the unconditional release of all political prisoners.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helmut Brandst脙陇tter, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Commissioner, dear Minister, when I think about Lukashenka, I don't think of a president. I think of a dictator. But moreover, I also think of a traitor because he sold out his country to a war criminal, to Putin. He told him he can make war against Ukraine also out of Belarus.

Of course, we talked about the political prisoners. I was always impressed when I talked to Ms脗聽Tsikhanouskaya. I really didn't dare to ask, 'Did you hear anything from your husband?', because once she told me, 'No, for over a year I haven't heard from him', and now we know that it's for many years.

Just imagine what kind of torture it is, not only to throw people into a prison, but then you say you will never hear from your family again. As it was mentioned, it's at least 1脗聽100, 1脗聽200 political prisoners who are still there and we have to talk about them. We have to put pressure on Lukashenka, as it was mentioned again.

Also, thank you very much, Commissioner, you talked about those 400脗聽000 people here. We have to help them. We talked about action already 芒聙聯 action is media. We need more information for the people who still live in Belarus. Belarus is Europe. It will be free. It belongs to the EU. Zhyve Belarus!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sergey Lagodinsky, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, I'm not going to be the first one in this round who will repeat the facts, but it is important to repeat them again and again.

The release of Siarhei Tsikhanouski was a moment of hope, but the grim reality is the 1脗聽100 more who are still there behind bars. Every reunited family 芒聙聯 every father who can embrace his daughter 芒聙聯 is a moment of hope. But, how many of them cannot? How many of them are even in unknown locations? Maryia Kalesnikava, Maksim Znak 芒聙聯 we don't know where they are, whether they are still alive.

And one more thing is important: this was not an act of mercy. These are cold-blooded strategy calculations by Lukashenka. Let us not be deceived by what he is playing here. This is not genuine reform; it's manipulation.

Genuine reform would be the unconditional release of every political prisoner in Belarus and, for us in Europe, it would be a policy where every Belarusian refugee would feel at home, that they would not have problems with their documents, that they will not be begging in our own foreign ministries for new passports because Belarus does not issue for them the passports of their own nation. This we owe to them. Zhyve Belarus!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Per Clausen, for The Left-Gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru formand! Det var med stor gl脙娄de at jeg l脙娄ste, at Siarhej Tikhanovskij og 13 andre politiske flygtninge i juni blev l脙赂sladt i Belarus, l脙赂sladt fra domme, de aldrig burde have haft. L脙赂sladt til et samfund, der stadig undertrykkes med jernh脙楼rd h脙楼nd af diktatoren Lukasjenka. De 14 blev l脙赂sladt, men der er stadig mere end 1100 politiske fanger i belarusiske f脙娄ngsler. Folk, der aldrig skulle have v脙娄ret bag tremmer. Derfor m脙楼 vi ogs脙楼 v脙娄re klare i vores besked til Lukasjenka-regimet. Alle politiske fanger skal l脙赂slades, og der skal indf脙赂res respekt for demokrati, ytringsfrihed, retten til at v脙娄re i fagforening og ogs脙楼 for de rettigheder, som man har som arbejdstager. F脙赂r det sker, b脙赂r EU ikke s脙楼 meget som overveje at sl脙娄kke p脙楼 foranstaltningerne mod det diktatoriske styre i Belarus. At tro p脙楼, at Lukasjenka ville respektere demokratiet, er som at tro p脙楼, at Putin eller Netanyahu respekterer international lov.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽David McAllister (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, ladies and gentlemen, as colleagues have already mentioned, the recent release of a number of political prisoners in Belarus indeed offers a glimmer of hope amid a dark reality. Yet over a thousand remain unjustly imprisoned still.

Belarus remains a battleground for truth. Journalists and dissenters face brutal repression, but their courage shines as a beacon for change.

I recall the words of Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya in this House. She said, 'the EU from Lisbon to Minsk is a nightmare for Putin, but for us it is a reality we strive to live in'.

Not despite, but precisely because of, the recent releases, we must stand firm, strengthening sanctions and supporting those who risk everything for freedom.

Dictatorships thrive when alternatives seem absent. The European Union is that alternative, empowering the democratic forces of Belarus to keep the dream of democracy and the rule of law alive. The future of Belarus belongs to its brave citizens and not to the dictator in Minsk.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pina Picierno (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, accogliamo naturalmente con sollievo la liberazione di Sergei Tikhanovsky e di altri prigionieri politici bielorussi, ma sappiamo che i loro corpi segnati raccontano la brutalit脙聽 della repressione carceraria, l'isolamento, i maltrattamenti, le privazioni. E sappiamo che per ogni persona liberata decine restano rinchiuse in condizioni disumane.

Anch'io aggiungo la mia voce a chi da quest'Aula oggi ne ha chiesto l'immediato rilascio senza condizioni. Sappiamo che l'ONU parla di crimini contro l'umanit脙聽 e per questo le sanzioni contro la Bielorussia devono essere ferme, allineate a quelle contro la Russia.

Ma sappiamo che serve di pi脙鹿. Questo Parlamento e la Commissione devono rafforzare il sostegno alla resistenza bielorussa in esilio, dare voci, dare strumenti, dare protezione a chi lotta per la libert脙聽. La libert脙聽 della bielorussa si difende anche da qui, con fermezza, con verit脙聽 e senza distogliere lo sguardo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

IN THE CHAIR: SABINE VERHEYEN
Vice-President

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ma脜聜gorzata Gosiewska (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! 脜聛ukaszenko wypu脜聸ci脜聜 30 zak脜聜adnik脙鲁w. Nie wi脛聶脜潞ni脙鲁w, a zak脜聜adnik脙鲁w. Brutalnie torturowanych, wyniszczonych psychicznie i fizycznie. Ich wygl脛聟d m脙鲁wi wszystko. Sp脙鲁jrzcie na twarz Siarhieja Cichanouskiego. To nie jest obraz cz脜聜owieka, kt脙鲁ry odzyska脜聜 wolno脜聸脛聡. To 脜聸wiadectwo zbrodni.

Re脜录im nie zatrzymuje si脛聶. W 2025 r. mamy ju脜录 ponad 1700 zatrzyma脜聞. Setki nowych zak脜聜adnik脙鲁w. Pseudos脛聟dy dzia脜聜aj脛聟 ka脜录dego dnia, jak w przypadku Wiktorii Kulszy. Pi脛聟ty wyrok. Dramatyczny stan zdrowia. Bezterminowe przetrzymywanie. To nie jest prawo. To jest system terroru.

U脜聜askawienia to cyniczna gra re脜录imu, zas脜聜ona dymna. 脜聛ukaszenka s脜聜abnie. Sankcje dzia脜聜aj脛聟. I dlatego w脜聜a脜聸nie nie wolno ich teraz 脜聜agodzi脛聡. Trzeba je wzmocni脛聡. Uderza脛聡 dok脜聜adniej i g脜聜脛聶biej. Unia musi m脙鲁wi脛聡 jednym g脜聜osem. 脜禄adnych ust脛聶pstw. 脜禄adnych iluzji. Zawieszenie sankcji mo脜录e by脛聡 rozpatrywane tylko w jednym przypadku: pe脜聜nego natychmiastowego uwolnienia wszystkich wi脛聶脜潞ni脙鲁w politycznych. Uniewa脜录nienie wszystkich spraw politycznych. Zero nowych represji. Ka脜录dy krok wstecz to przyzwolenie na zbrodni脛聶. 脜禄ywie Bie脜聜aru脜聸.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Kobosko (Renew). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Pani Komisarz! Pani Minister! To oczywi脜聸cie dobra wiadomo脜聸脛聡, 脜录e 14 os脙鲁b na czele z Siarhiejem Cichanouskim wysz脜聜o na wolno脜聸脛聡, jednak nie pozw脙鲁lmy, by ta dobra wiadomo脜聸脛聡 przys脜聜oni脜聜a nam najwa脜录niejsz脛聟 prawd脛聶: ci ludzie nigdy nie powinni byli trafi脛聡 do wi脛聶zienia. Nie pope脜聜nili 脜录adnej zbrodni. Ich jedynym przest脛聶pstwem (w cudzys脜聜owie) by脜聜a walka o wolno脜聸脛聡, prawd脛聶 i demokracj脛聶. Uwolnienie tych os脙鲁b to nie 脜录adna 脜聜aska, to cz脛聶脜聸脛聡 wyrachowanej gry re脜录imu 脜聛ukaszenki, gry PR-owej, w kt脙鲁rej dyktator pr脙鲁buje pozyska脛聡 punkty w relacjach mi脛聶dzynarodowych. Nie dajmy si脛聶 temu zwie脜聸脛聡. Uwi脛聶zionych wci脛聟脜录 pozostaje ponad 1100 os脙鲁b, w tym polski dziennikarz Andrzej Poczobut.

Dop脙鲁ki cho脛聡 jeden wi脛聶zie脜聞 polityczny 脜聛ukaszenki pozostanie uwi脛聶ziony, b脛聶dziemy wzywa脛聡 do rozszerzania sankcji. Cho脛聡 wielu m脙鲁wi, 脜录e bia脜聜oruska gospodarka ju脜录 si脛聶 nie podniesie, to dane m脙鲁wi脛聟 co innego. Dla przyk脜聜adu bia脜聜oruskie linie lotnicze zwi脛聶kszy脜聜y sw脙鲁j wk脜聜ad do PKB o 14% rok do roku. To tylko jeden z wielu przyk脜聜ad脙鲁w, 脜录e re脜录im si脛聶 adaptuje, kombinuje, szuka luk w sankcjach. Nie mo脜录emy pomaga脛聡 mu przetrwa脛聡. Tak d脜聜ugo, jak rz脛聟dzi 脜聛ukaszenka, tak d脜聜ugo demokracja, prawa cz脜聜owieka b脛聶d脛聟 na Bia脜聜orusi martwe, nieistniej脛聟ce.

Panie Siarhieju, serdecznie zapraszamy do Parlamentu Europejskiego. Pana obecno脜聸脛聡 w脜聸r脙鲁d bia脜聜oruskich opozycjonist脙鲁w doda motywacji i doprowadzi do zrywu, kt脙鲁ry wreszcie obali re脜录im 脜聛ukaszenki.脗聽脜禄ywie Bie脜聜aru脜聸!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasa Juknevi脛聧ien脛聴 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, I have decided to address you using the words of the Belarusians who continue to fight.

The essence of this debate is captured in two quotes from Siarhei Tsikhanouski, who was just released from Lukashenka's prison.

First, about Putin, I quote: 'Because of Putin, this illegal government is still in Belarus.' Second, about the situation of political prisoners, I quote: 'There were skin diseases and everyone had kidney problems from the cold 芒聙聯 and no one really understood what was happening. Blood came out of my mouth, from my nose. Sometimes I had convulsions, but it was all because of the cold, that terrible cold when you sit in those punishment cells.'

So what should we, the EU, do? Not to be afraid to defeat Putin and his puppet Lukashenka. Slava Ukraini! Zhyve Belarus!脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mariusz Kami脜聞ski (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Debatujemy dzi脜聸 po raz kolejny o sytuacji na Bia脜聜orusi, o ponurej, trwaj脛聟cej ju脜录 30 lat dyktaturze 脜聛ukaszenki. Jego rz脛聟dy to czas brutalnego deptania praw cz脜聜owieka i podstawowego prawa narodu do 脜录ycia w demokratycznym i niepodleg脜聜ym pa脜聞stwie. 脜聛ukaszenka to kat w脜聜asnego narodu. To rosyjska marionetka, kt脙鲁ra bez wsparcia Putina i terroru nie by脜聜aby w stanie utrzyma脛聡 si脛聶 przy w脜聜adzy.

Dzi脜聸 symbolem Bia脜聜orusi nie jest jednak pod脜聜a twarz 脜聛ukaszenki. Dzi脜聸 Bia脜聜oru脜聸 ma swoich prawdziwych bohater脙鲁w, bojownik脙鲁w o wolno脜聸脛聡. Pami脛聶tajmy o Ramanie Bandarence zamordowanym 5 lat temu na ulicach Mi脜聞ska. Pami脛聶tajmy o przebywaj脛聟cym w wi脛聶zieniu laureacie Pokojowej Nagrody Nobla Alesiu Bialackim O wybitnych dziennikarzach Kaciarynie Andrejewej, Andrzeju Poczobucie, o tysi脛聟cach mniej znanych bohater脙鲁w, takich jak ma脜聜脜录e脜聞stwo Hanna Kmit i Andrej Selianinau. To s脛聟 prawdziwe twarze Bia脜聜orusi. Walka o ich wolno脜聸脛聡 jest naszym moralnym i politycznym obowi脛聟zkiem.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dainius 脜陆alimas (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, fear Commissioner, dear colleagues, the recent release of 14 political prisoners gives us little hope. More than 1脗聽000, including EU citizens, still remain behind bars in Belarus. They are still subjected to systemic torture and inhuman treatment. No doubt, our line to demand the release of all political prisoners and to ensure accountability for the crimes against humanity must remain intact.

In addition, first, we must call for a formal EU report on deaths of political prisoners. According to the available information, at least seven political prisoners 芒聙聯 Shtermer, Shlettgauer, Kulinich, Lednik, Khrasko, Klimovich, Pushkin and Ashurak 芒聙聯 died in recent years due to inhuman conditions.

Secondly, we must urge the EU to sanction all the accomplices of Lukashenko regime, including 110 pseudo-deputies of the so-called House of Representatives, 68 members of the so-called Council of the Republic, more than a thousand of so-called judges, prosecutors and investigators. All of them are puppets of the regime, complicit in imposing the repressive legislation and destroying lives.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandra Kalniete (PPE). 芒聙聯 Cien脛芦t脛聛 s脛聯des vad脛芦t脛聛ja! Kol脛聯脛拢i!脗聽Es patiesi priec脛聛jos, ka Sjarhejs Cihanouskis un v脛聯l da脜戮i politieslodz脛芦tie, galvenok脛聛rt 脛聛rvalstu pilso脜聠i, ir atbr脛芦voti, ta脛聧u cietumos v脛聯l arvien smok vair脛聛k nek脛聛 1200 politieslodz脛芦to.

Nesen public脛聯tais ANO zi脜聠ojums atkl脛聛j 脜隆ausm脛芦gu ainu. Tas ir k脛聛 no VDK rokasgr脛聛matas 芒聙聯 politieslodz脛芦tie sistem脛聛tiski ir pak脛录auti sp脛芦dzin脛聛脜隆anai, seksu脛聛lai vardarb脛芦bai, 脛录aunpr脛聛t脛芦gai izmanto脜隆anai un piespiedu psihiatriskai 脛聛rstniec脛芦bai.

Luka脜隆enka ir cinisks, brut脛聛ls diktators, kas rupji p脛聛rk脛聛pj starptautisk脛聛s ties脛芦bas un cilv脛聯kties脛芦bas. Vi脜聠脜隆 ir Putina sabiedrotais agresij脛聛 pret Ukrainu. Atbr脛芦vojot da脜戮us politieslodz脛芦tos, vi脜聠脜隆 m脛聯脛拢ina mazin脛聛t savu izol脛聛ciju un sankcijas. Tas nedr脛芦kst notikt! Par pastr脛聛d脛聛tajiem noziegumiem pret baltkrievu un ukrai脜聠u taut脛聛m Luka脜隆enkam un vi脜聠a rokaspui脜隆iem ir j脛聛atbild Starptautiskaj脛聛 krimin脛聛lties脛聛 H脛聛g脛聛.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie-Agnes Strack-Zimmermann (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, meine Damen und Herren! Wissen eigentlich die Putin芒聙聭Freunde hier im Haus, was die Marionettenregierung von Moskau in Belarus veranstaltet? Es ist bezeichnend, dass auch keiner von denen hier ist. Zurzeit befinden sich in Belarus 1脗聽160脗聽politische Gefangene in Haft, darunter 165脗聽Frauen. Im Verh脙陇ltnis zur Einwohnerzahl hat Belarus die h脙露chste Zahl politischer Gefangener. Am 14.脗聽Juli j脙陇hrt sich die Verhaftung des belarussischen Literaturwissenschaftlers Ales Bjaljazki. Er wurde 2021 verhaftet, zwei Jahre sp脙陇ter zu zehn Jahren Lager verurteilt 芒聙聯 isoliert und immer kr脙陇nker werdend. Der belarussische K脙录nstler Ales Puschkin ist inzwischen in der Haft 芒聙聯脗聽wie viele andere 芒聙聯 verstorben. Der belarussische Videoblogger Zichanouski war zwei Jahre in Isolationshaft. Seine Worte nach seiner Freilassung: 芒聙聻Ein Gefangener ist immer noch ein Mensch. Das ist unmenschlich. Das ist ein Albtraum. Das sind Folterungen. Das muss aufh脙露ren.芒聙聹 Alexander Lukaschenko ist die Marionette Moskaus. Er ist ein brutaler Diktator. Vergessen wir diese Verbrechen nicht und st脙陇rken die Opposition.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Miriam Lexmann (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, the release of Siarhei Tsikhanouski and other political prisoners gives hope to all of us who believe in a democratic Belarus.

But this single positive message doesn't signal the end of political repression in the country. Over a thousand political prisoners remain behind bars. Systematic crackdowns on citizens have only strengthened in recent months. Repressive measures have expanded to include unprecedented attacks on churches and on religious freedom in general.

Dear colleagues, we have to continue to exert pressure on Lukashenka, particularly by making sure our sanctions are not circumvented. We have to continue to support Belarusian civil society and the democratic opposition. And let's not forget that our support to Ukraine is also support for a sovereign, democratic and prosperous Belarus.脗聽Zhyve Belarus!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, the situation in Belarus remains one of the most critical challenges facing Europe today. Nearly four years after these fraudulent presidential elections, as my colleague Ms Strack-Zimmermann mentioned, 1脗聽160 Belarusians remain imprisoned not for crimes, but for bravely standing up for their fundamental rights of democracy, dignity and freedom. These political prisoners 芒聙聯 journalists, students, trade unionists 芒聙聯 are enduring unjust repression under this illegitimate Lukashenka regime.

Their detention constitutes a grave violation of fundamental human rights, and we must demand their immediate and unconditional release. Until this happens, we cannot think about normalisation with such a regime. The release of Siarhei Tsikhanouski is a signal for hope, but it's not the end of our fight against this regime.

Written Statements (Rule 178)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Catch-the-eye procedure

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). 芒聙聯 Gerbiama Pirmininke, kolegos, a脜隆 nor脛聴脛聧iau pamin脛聴ti vien脛聟 pavard脛聶 芒聙聯 Mykola Statkevi脛聧. Tai Baltarusijos socialdemokrat脜鲁 partijos lyderis, kuris vis脛聟 laik脛聟 nuo pat Baltarusijos nepriklausomyb脛聴s paskelbimo, vis脛聟 laik脛聟 aktyviai kovojo u脜戮 demokratij脛聟, 脜戮mogaus teises ir vis脛聟 laik脛聟 pats buvo aktyviausias pasiprie脜隆inimo autoritariniam re脜戮imui dalyvis. Visi puikiai 脜戮inome, kad Europos Parlamentas ne kart脛聟 pasisak脛聴 d脛聴l jo, ta脛聧iau 脜隆脛炉 kart脛聟 v脛聴l man tenka priminti, nes jo i脜隆nykimas... nieko nebe脜戮inoma apie jo 脛炉kalinim脛聟, nieko nebe脜戮inoma, kas su juo toliau yra atsitik脛聶, joki脜鲁 脜戮ini脜鲁. Ir a脜隆 raginu Komisij脛聟, raginu dar kart脛聟 atkreipti ypating脛聟 d脛聴mes脛炉 脛炉 脜隆脛炉 pat脛炉 vien脛聟 i脜隆 i脜隆kiliausi脜鲁 pasiprie脜隆inimo didvyri脜鲁.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, thank you for your interventions. Yes, let us celebrate the release of Siarhei Tsikhanouski and others, and call for their full rehabilitation and safe return home.

But we also insist that true progress will be measured not just by pardons, but by the comprehensive dismantling of a system of repression.

We call on the Belarusian authorities to immediately halt all political persecution, to enact meaningful reforms and to adhere to its international obligations.

And dear colleagues, let us continue to stand for freedom, justice and the rule of law in Belarus until every single political prisoner is free.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Bjerre, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioners, thank you for your valuable interventions and also very strong comments.

For instance, Robert Biedro脜聞 from the S&D mentioned specific names of prisoners. This is a brutal repression, as said by Adam Bielan from the ECR and also from many others. We have to talk about it and we have to be clear, as several of you said.

We will continue to call for the immediate and unconditional release of all political prisoners and for the end of repression against civil society, independent media and peaceful dissent. The EU will continue to urge the regime to end its violations while supporting the democratic aspirations of the Belarusian people.脗聽We will remain fully engaged and closely monitor developments on the ground and the issue will remain high on the EU agenda.

We remain united in our support to the brave people of Belarus in their quest for a free, democratic, sovereign and independent Belarus as part of a peaceful Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Dworczyk (ECR), na pi脜聸mie. 芒聙聯 Andrzej Poczobut jest symbolem oporu wobec re脜录imu 脜聛ukaszenki, walki o prawd脛聶, wolno脜聸脛聡 s脜聜owa i prawa mniejszo脜聸ci narodowych. Wielokrotnie represjonowany za swoj脛聟 dzia脜聜alno脜聸脛聡, obecnie przebywa ju脜录 4 rok w wi脛聶zieniu o zaostrzonym rygorze. Jego sprawa to przyk脜聜ad brutalno脜聸ci re脜录imu, ale tak脜录e test solidarno脜聸ci 脜聸wiata demokratycznego.

To w脜聜a脜聸nie z my脜聸l脛聟 o takich osobach jak Poczobut zorganizowa脜聜em podczas czerwcowej sesji plenarnej Parlamentu Europejskiego wydarzenie po脜聸wi脛聶cone wi脛聶脜潞niom politycznym na Bia脜聜orusi. Nie by脜聜a to jedynie kolejna debata, ale pr脙鲁ba oddania g脜聜osu tym, kt脙鲁rym go odebrano. Historia Kaciaryny Andrejewej 芒聙聰dziennikarki Telewizji Bie脜聜sat skazanej na 脜聜脛聟cznie 10 lat wi脛聶zienia za prowadzenie transmisji na 脜录ywo brutalnie t脜聜umionego pokojowego protestu w Mi脜聞sku, po 脜聸mierci Ramana Bandarenki, nie jest wyj脛聟tkiem. To symbol losu setek ludzi, kt脙鲁rzy p脜聜ac脛聟 za prawd脛聶 swoj脛聟 wolno脜聸ci脛聟. Potwierdzi脜聜y to dramatyczne 脜聸wiadectwa rodzin wi脛聶脜潞ni脙鲁w politycznych, kt脙鲁rzy opowiadali o nieludzkim traktowaniu swoich bliskich: izolacja, brak kontaktu z rodzin脛聟, brutalne przes脜聜uchania, psychiczna presja.

Dzi脜聸, wed脜聜ug danych organizacji praw cz脜聜owieka, w bia脜聜oruskich wi脛聶zieniach przebywa ponad 1400 wi脛聶脜潞ni脙鲁w politycznych. Ostatnie informacje o uwolnieniu kilku z nich s脛聟 oczywi脜聸cie wa脜录ne 芒聙聰 dla rodzin to wielka ulga. Ale wobec ca脜聜ej skali - to zaledwie gest.

Dlatego apeluj脛聶: nie zostawiajmy ich samych, pami脛聶tajmy o Andrzeju Poczobucie, o Kaciarynie Andrejewej i setkach innych bezprawnie wi脛聶zionych przez 脜聛ukaszenk脛聶.

脗聽

21. 2023 and 2024 reports on Albania (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the debate on the report by Andreas Schieder, on behalf of the Committee on Foreign Affairs, on the 2023 and 2024 Commission reports on Albania (2025/2017(INI)) (A10-0106/2025).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andreas Schieder, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Madam脗聽Commissioner, dear colleagues, in the last 30 years, Albania and the citizens of Albania have faced many obstacles. Just remember the hard Enver Hoxha regime, which was the hardest we had in the eastern Europe communist sphere. Then after the regime change, even in 1997, they had a pyramid scheme crisis where, again, everybody lost the little savings they had.脗聽The country had to overcome these obstacles and has also gone through an enormous change, like few other countries in Europe did.

Since 2009, Albania has been on its EU journey. In 2009, Albania applied for membership. In 2014, it became an official accession candidate. In 2022, this negotiation started and in 2024, the first negotiation chapter was finally opened. We see already that these statistics and numbers show that this is a long story.

Today, we discuss the first annual report in this term on the progress of Albania as a candidate country. The report covers the Parliament's response to the Commission enlargement, reports both of 2023 and 2024, the state of play and the latest developments of Albania's progress in joining a deal. I have to say 芒聙聯 and on this point, I also want to thank the colleagues and shadow rapporteurs in the AFET Committee 芒聙聯 the report was adopted with a large majority of 57 votes in favour, 11 against and six abstentions. Thank you all for this constructive work which has been done.

Secondly, before coming also a little bit to the content of the report 芒聙聯 I'm just looking for where they are 芒聙聯 I think also there is the Albanian delegation today here in the European Parliament from the Albanian Parliament. Welcome to the European Parliament and I hope that your work and our work will lead also that you in the future will sit amongst us here in the European Parliament. Welcome to your House.

So, the main topic of the report is, of course, progress concerning also some issues like the democratic institutions and how they have developed. Media freedom, what are the open issues there? For example, that we have in media freedom still the question of instant transparent ownership. So, it's not the freedom of journalists to ask questions, but it's the question of who owns the media platform. We have the question of involvement of civil society. We have the question of fundamental rights, the rule of law, of social economic reforms, of environment and of regional cooperation and foreign policy.

In October 2024, Albania started the negotiations on Cluster 1. In December came Cluster 6, in April 2025 came Cluster 2 and in May came Cluster 3. Now, Albania has opened 24 out of 33 negotiation chapters. We welcome this fast progress and Albania has been called a front runner by you, Madam脗聽Commissioner Marta Kos 芒聙聯 I think this is very right that they are a front runner.

There are a lot of positive remarks to be made. We see good progress in the implementation of the justice reform, including also the waiting process in SPAK, in steps in environmental protection and also in full alignment with foreign policy.

To come to an end, of course, we see a lot of things to be done. As I mentioned before, there is the media situation, but also the implementation of OSCE audio recommendations, which have to be done on a broader political maturity and have to be done also sure in the future.

Finishing, I think I just want to say that we fully support Albania's objective to be front runner and to open all the other clusters, but it's more important also to close the clusters and to work on the reforms. I think if the speed and this unification on the whole country to join the European Union will continue, they will also manage. Our report is a positive one, but putting the points also on the things which have to be changed.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, dear rapporteur. I would like to extend my gratitude to this House for holding this debate and especially to you, Mr脗聽Schieder, for managing to consolidate a very balanced resolution on the 2023 and 2024 Commission reports on Albania. Thank you for your excellent work.

This report comes at a crucial juncture, as accession negotiations between Albania and the EU are progressing at an unprecedented pace, as we heard, with several important milestones already met in the past few months and more to come. Since the opening of the first cluster on the so-called 'fundamentals' in October脗聽2024, 4 clusters and 24 out of 33 chapters have now been opened, as we also heard.脗聽This demonstrates that Albania has seriously engaged in enlargement, while the EU delivers, respecting the merit-based approach. We are working closely with the Danish Presidency to keep up the momentum. We trust that the two remaining clusters will also be opened as soon as feasible. Solid preparation on both the EU and the Albanian sides is already well advanced.

While work is moving forward across technical clusters, we continue to maintain a very strong focus on reforms in the area of the rule of law and fight against corruption. Albania is expected to consolidate further the very good progress in this area. The continued progress with justice reform and the vetting of magistrates, as well as the good results of the special structure against organised crime and corruption (SPAK) are positive steps which are also highlighted in the Parliament's report.

It is crucial that Albania continues to demonstrate concrete progress in key related areas, guided by three principles. First, justice reform is an irreversible process and its progress must not only be preserved but further consolidated.

Second, the efforts to combat corruption, particularly at high level, must continue with great determination. In this context, the independence of the special structure against corruption and organised crime (SPAK) must be fully upheld. SPAK stands today as the most trusted institution in the country. This must be safeguarded and strengthened.

Third, more efforts must be made to uphold fundamental rights, especially media freedom. That begins with stronger measures to protect journalists, ensuring the independence of both the media regulator and the public broadcaster, and promoting true pluralism and transparency of media funding. The growth plan for the Western Balkans will also continue to support Albania on reforms in these areas, which are necessary for its accession path, while granting them access to parts of the single market before actual membership in the EU.

Honourable Members, in the current geopolitical context, Albania and the Western Balkans remain a geostrategic priority for the EU. The swift progress in the accession negotiations is a clear signal that we stand by Albania, and we are genuinely committed to marking out a clear pathway for EU accession. But EU accession will remain, above all, a merit-based process. Rest assured that the Commission will continue to actively engage with Albanian authorities to ensure that Albania carries out the reforms needed to become an EU Member State.

I look forward to continuing close cooperation towards our common objective: Albania successfully concluding the accession negotiations with the European Union as soon as feasible. Ultimately, of course, all these reforms are first and foremost essential to guarantee that all citizens in Albania enjoy strengthened protection of their rights, increased welfare, prosperity and security.

Let me conclude with the observation that the transformational power of enlargement is clearly seen in Albania. Thank you, dear Members of the European Parliament for working on Albania. I am looking forward to our discussion.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrey Kovatchev, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, in light of the growing threat from Russia and China, EU enlargement is no longer a possibility, but a necessity and an essential geostrategic investment in building a united and strong Europe.

I welcome Albania's significant progress on its EU integration path, having opened (as mentioned by our colleagues) 24 out of 33 negotiation chapters in just seven months. The European Union remains fully committed to delivering on its promises.

I welcome particularly the reforms aimed at safeguarding the rights of minorities, notably the adoption of the implementing legislation on the right of persons belonging to minorities, as well as the successful 2023 population census.

The EU enlargement process must remain merit-based. Every country must meet the necessary criteria and implement the required reforms to join the European family.

In this regard, I will stress the urgent need for further efforts, particularly in combating corruption and ensuring the independence and proper functioning of the judiciary, free from undue influence and interference, and a free and independent media.

Albania's political system remains deeply polarised and fragmented, with alarming clashes between the ruling majority and the opposition. This was evident in the run-up to and during the parliamentary elections on 11脗聽May, with unlawful practices and persisting challenges, notably: vote buying and misuse of public resources by the ruling party, which were highlighted by the international observers.

A functioning opposition and genuine political plurality are vital for the health of any democratic system. Therefore, I call on the authorities to take decisive action to implement the OSCE/ODIHR final recommendations and to address the complaints and signals received during the electoral process.

The Albanian authorities must uphold the rule of law and ensure the separation of powers in order to advance further on their European path. Good luck Albania!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marco Tarquinio, a nome del gruppo S&D. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, relatore Schieder, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, l'Albania ha intrapreso il suo percorso di adesione con impegno e incisivit脙聽 e con un encomiabile lavoro di riforma.

Persistono problemi reali, ma le questioni da risolvere sono ben individuate: ne sono testimone da copresidente del comitato parlamentare UE芒聙聭Albania.

Ora si tratta di procedere con una chiara assunzione di responsabilit脙聽 e con un'efficace convergenza tra le forze politiche, che 芒聙聯 lo ricordo 芒聙聯 nonostante le differenze sostengono senza esitazioni il processo di adesione.

Da quest'Aula mi permetto di rivolgere un appello ai colleghi parlamentari albanesi, alcuni qui presenti oggi, e lo faccio con il massimo rispetto e con la fiducia che abbiamo costruito in questi mesi: che la pur legittima polemica interna non porti ad argomenti denigratori, caricaturali e soprattutto dannosi per l'immagine del paese delle Aquile e per il processo in corso.

Stiamo lavorando per un'Albania pienamente parte della famiglia europea, facciamo s脙卢 che l'obiettivo diventi realt脙聽.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matthieu Valet, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, Madame la Commissaire, je ne comprends pas. Alors que mon pays, la France, est gangr脙漏n脙漏 par les 脗芦narcoracailles脗禄, les narcotrafiquants, les narcoterroristes et le narcobanditisme, vous vous obstinez 脙聽 maintenir le processus d芒聙聶avancement d芒聙聶adh脙漏sion de l芒聙聶Albanie 脙聽 l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne. Pourtant, l芒聙聶Albanie n芒聙聶est pas un partenaire fiable. Elle reste min脙漏e par la corruption, par les influences 脙漏trang脙篓res 芒聙聯脗聽notamment turque脗聽芒聙聯 et par des r脙漏seaux criminels solidement implant脙漏s 脙聽 travers toute l芒聙聶Europe.

Je ne sais pas, en revanche, si vous lisez les rapports d芒聙聶Europol, l芒聙聶agence de coop脙漏ration des polices europ脙漏ennes, mais ils sont tr脙篓s clairs: les groupes criminels albanais figurent parmi les plus mena脙搂ants pour notre s脙漏curit脙漏. Ils sont profond脙漏ment impliqu脙漏s dans le trafic de drogue, la traite des 脙陋tres humains, le blanchiment d芒聙聶argent et le commerce illicite d芒聙聶armes. D芒聙聶ailleurs, rappelons que les armes utilis脙漏es lors des attentats du Bataclan en脗聽2015 dans mon pays 芒聙聯脗聽qui avaient fait 131脗聽morts脗聽芒聙聯 provenaient en grande partie des Balkans. Elles avaient transit脙漏 par des routes de contrebande, vis-脙聽-vis desquelles les r脙漏seaux albanais jouent un r脙麓le d脙漏terminant.

Cette r脙漏alit脙漏 perdure. Lisez les rapports, Madame. Europol alerte encore en脗聽2025 sur la persistance de ce trafic depuis les Balkans occidentaux vers l芒聙聶Union. Enfin, l芒聙聶Albanie demeure un producteur majeur de cannabis, un point d芒聙聶entr脙漏e du trafic d芒聙聶h脙漏ro脙炉ne en provenance d芒聙聶Afghanistan et un autre acteur cl脙漏 dans les r脙漏seaux de coca脙炉ne. La mafia albanaise est omnipr脙漏sente dans ces circuits criminels.

Dans ces conditions, rapprocher davantage ce pays de l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne serait une faute politique grave et qui compromettrait directement la s脙漏curit脙漏 des Europ脙漏ens, et particuli脙篓rement des Fran脙搂ais.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alberico Gambino, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, signora Commissaria, onorevoli colleghi, la relazione in discussione riconosce con equilibrio i progressi compiuti dall'Albania nel percorso di adesione all'Unione europea.脗聽In particolare l'allineamento alla politica estera comune, il rafforzamento istituzionale e il contributo alla stabilit脙聽 regionale.

Resta fondamentale mantenere alta l'attenzione sull'indipendenza della magistratura, sulla tutela dei diritti fondamentali e sulla lotta alla corruzione, cos脙卢 come superare la polarizzazione politica, elementi essenziali per rafforzare la fiducia reciproca e per concludere i negoziati di adesione.

Tra le altre cose, ho potuto constatare, anche con il relatore Schieder, che anche su questo versante sono stati fatti significativi passi in avanti.

Vorrei infine sottolineare il valore della cooperazione in materia di immigrazione. L'intesa tra Italia e Albania dimostra che 脙篓 possibile affrontare sfide complesse con strumenti concreti e condivisi, nel rispetto del diritto europeo e della sovranit脙聽 nazionale.

L'Albania 脙篓 un partner strategico e l'Unione deve accompagnare con determinazione e rigore il cammino europeo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tineke Strik, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Commissioner, dear members of the Albanian delegation that may be here, dear rapporteurs and shadow rapporteurs, thank you very much for the report and the great constructive work.

Albania is an accession frontrunner, making solid progress to fulfil its aim of entering the EU by 2027. And that could be historic, of course. Albania's EU membership will accelerate economic prosperity for its citizens and increase the geopolitical strength of our union, but also encourage other candidate countries to work on their reforms.

Of course, we need more improvements on rule of law, fundamental freedoms, media freedom and tackling corruption. But I am confident that it is possible for the Albanian Government, dear Mr脗聽Kyuchyuk 芒聙聯 that the Albanian Government will deliver on these priorities in time. And I hope that the EU will give all its support for that aim.

But we can only credibly demand to stick to a merit-based approach to improve the rule of law and refrain from political blackmail if we do it ourselves too. The Italy-Albania agreement opposes exactly these principles, because it's an abuse of Albania's EU aspirations by one EU Member State to dump people out of its borders. This agreement is inhumane, expensive and ineffective. And the Commission's silence so far is deafening.

Let us end this hypocrisy and stick to the values that we demand from our candidate countries too.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Everding, im Namen der Fraktion The Left. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! 芒聙聻Jeder auf der Welt hat seine Tr脙陇ume芒聙聺 sagte schon der albanische Schriftsteller Ismail Kadare. Mein Traum ist es, dass Albanien auf dem Weg in die EU den Umweltschutz als Br脙录cke sieht und nicht als Hindernis. J脙录ngste Entwicklungen wie der Flughafen Vlora, der im Vjosa芒聙聭Narta芒聙聭Schutzgebiet gebaut wurde, geben jedoch Anlass zur Sorge und versto脙聼en gegen EU芒聙聭Umweltrecht. Wir brauchen zudem st脙陇rkere Ma脙聼nahmen zum Schutz gef脙陇hrdeter Arten, insbesondere des Balkanluchses, dessen 脙聹berleben von intakten Lebensr脙陇umen abh脙陇ngt. Ich begr脙录脙聼e ausdr脙录cklich Schritte wie die Ausweisung des Vjosa-Nationalparks, aber Umweltschutz im Tourismus-Boomland Albanien muss konsequent und nicht selektiv erfolgen.

Vergessen wir auch nicht die Tierschutzproblematik: In jeder albanischen Stadt leben tausende streunende Hunde auf den Stra脙聼en. Diese Herausforderung sollte ganzheitlich angegangen werden! Aber die L脙露sung, die gew脙陇hlt wurde, besteht darin, viele dieser Tiere auf grausame Art 脙露ffentlich zu t脙露ten. Die wenigen Tierheime sind 脙录berf脙录llt, und Kastrationsprogramme werden zu selten durchgef脙录hrt und dann nicht vom Staat, sondern von privaten NGOs finanziert.

Ja zur Mitgliedschaft Albaniens in der EU, aber nicht, bevor alle Bedingungen, einschlie脙聼lich Umweltschutz- und Tierschutzstandards, erf脙录llt sind.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexander Sell, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! 1脗聽Milliarde脗聽Euro will Frau von der Leyen an die Regierung in Albanien zahlen脗聽芒聙聯 an die Sozialistische Partei. Im Mai wurde dort gew脙陇hlt, aber mit Demokratie hat das wenig zu tun. Premierminister Edi Rama l脙陇sst politische Gegner wegsperren, internationale Wahlbeobachter kritisieren massenhafte Stimmenk脙陇ufe, Pressefreiheit gibt es kaum, TikTok war im Wahlkampf verboten.

Wirtschaftlich profitiert Albanien vor allem vom internationalen Drogenhandel. Mit extremer Brutalit脙陇t kontrolliert die albanische Mafia den Handel mit Kokain und Marihuana in Europa. Viele Milliarden Euro flie脙聼en so jedes Jahr nach Tirana脗聽芒聙聯 Hochh脙陇user und Glaspal脙陇ste sprie脙聼en aus dem Boden wie in Dubai. Die Regierung ist an diesem Gesch脙陇ft beteiligt, Korruption ist weitverbreitet. Sogar die Rosa-Luxemburg-Stiftung der deutschen Linkspartei spricht von, Zitat, 芒聙聻kleptokratischer Kontrolle des Staates durch die Sozialistische Partei芒聙聹. Experten nennen Albanien deshalb einen Mafiastaat脗聽芒聙聯 das Kolumbien Europas.

Dieses Land will Frau von der Leyen jetzt in die Europ脙陇ische Union aufnehmen. Ich frage Sie: Was ist der Mehrwert f脙录r unsere B脙录rger? Warum sollen deutsche Steuerzahler das Luxusleben von korrupten Politikern und Mafiabossen in Albanien finanzieren? Das machen wir nicht mit. Wir sagen Nein zum EU-Beitritt von Albanien, und wir sagen Nein zu dieser Kommission.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, Commissioner, dear Andreas Schieder, thank you for your report where you highlighted a broad range of issues that are of relevance for the accession of Albania to the EU.

You also highlighted some positive developments, like SPAK, this body that is combating corruption. Indeed there is a need for that. The Commission has also said that efforts to combat corruption at the highest level must be maintained.

Now, I was in Albania on occasion of the latest election. Technically, that went well, but indeed we as observers and the OSCE concluded that there was not a level playing field, that there was an abuse of incumbency, meaning that, for instance, they afforded to pay an extra EUR脗聽100 to pensioners just short of the election and traffic fines were abandoned two days before the election.

So these are issues that should not happen and that will have to be addressed, including also with the recommendations that had not yet been implemented.

Indeed, I have to say, the stories that you hear apart from the election observation, about the structures that relate to money laundering: that is a fact. There are 30脗聽000 apartments standing there empty because there have been built with laundered money and people looking for flats they cannot afford. There are also drug trafficking schemes.

We have to see 芒聙聯 and I'm confident we will manage this 芒聙聯 that these structures will not profit from entry into the European Union. They must be crushed before this country enters.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Brando Benifei (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il rispetto del diritto 脙篓 quello che sta alla base dell'adesione all'Unione europea.

Proprio questo manca nel vergognoso memorandum firmato da Giorgia Meloni con l'Albania, un accordo fallimentare da 800脗聽milioni, ora riciclato con l'idea di trasformare centri vuoti in CPR, buttando montagne di soldi dei contribuenti.

Un piano inumano, ma anche inutile e costoso, che non affronta davvero le sfide migratorie. Lo dimostrano anche le critiche arrivate da diversi governi europei, persino quelli pi脙鹿 vicini a Meloni.

Il governo pensi invece a sostenere davvero l'ingresso dell'Albania nell'Unione europea e non strumentalizzi il tema per accordi inutili e dannosi. Va invece sostenuto il percorso europeo dell'Albania con seriet脙聽 e impegno, sostenendo le riforme e sostenendo la vicinanza alla nostra Unione.

Noi continueremo a farlo con impegno, qui, dal Parlamento europeo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Aleksandar Nikolic (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, je ne sais pas si vous tenez les comptes, mais vous avez d脙漏j脙聽 donn脙漏 脙聽 l芒聙聶Albanie plus de 2脗聽milliards脗聽d芒聙聶euros depuis脗聽2007. Cet argent, les Fran脙搂ais et les Europ脙漏ens ne le reverront plus. Et pour quel r脙漏sultat? Vous l芒聙聶admettez vous-m脙陋mes dans vos rapports: l芒聙聶Albanie est gangr脙漏n脙漏e par la corruption, la libert脙漏 de la presse y est bafou脙漏e, et elle est une v脙漏ritable plaque tournante du crime organis脙漏 en Europe selon Europol.

Ce pays a tout d芒聙聶un 脙聣tat voyou et son adh脙漏sion 脙聽 l芒聙聶Union europ脙漏enne mettrait gravement en danger la s脙漏curit脙漏 de nos nations. En l芒聙聶admettant parmi nous, vous ouvrez encore plus grandes les portes au pire de la prostitution, du grand banditisme, du trafic d芒聙聶organes et des mafias. Quelles conclusions tirez-vous de ce constat catastrophique? Vous voulez leur donner encore plus: 922脗聽millions脗聽d芒聙聶euros suppl脙漏mentaires, 922脗聽millions脗聽d芒聙聶euros, dont on sait d芒聙聶exp脙漏rience qu芒聙聶ils ne changeront rien 脙聽 la situation d脙漏sastreuse de ce pays, malheureusement, et qu芒聙聶ils finiront en partie dans les poches d芒聙聶oligarques et de mafieux.

Dans quel monde finance-t-on un candidat pour qu芒聙聶il passe un examen d芒聙聶entr脙漏e tout en sachant qu芒聙聶il n芒聙聶est pas pr脙陋t? Alors, je n芒聙聶ai qu芒聙聶une seule question 脙聽 vous adresser, 脙聽 vous et 脙聽 Mme脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen: qui vous a donn脙漏 le droit de jouer ainsi avec l芒聙聶argent et la s脙漏curit脙漏 des Europ脙漏ens?

脗聽
